  \wordsense{n. 稳健派，（政治上的）温和主义者}
  \wordsense{v. ['mɒdəreit] 使缓和，使温和；使适中；主持（讨论等）}
  \wordexample{moderate-sized house（大小适中的房子） a man of moderate opinions（持温和观点的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{moderation}{ˌmɒdə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 缓和；适度；稳健；（M\textasciitilde{}）牛津大学的（文学学士）学位初试}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{moderator}{'mɒdəreitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 会议主席；调停者；仲裁者}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{modernism}{'mɒdənizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 现代主义；现代思想}
  \wordexample{modernity（现代性；现代事物） modernist（现代主义者）}
  \wordexample{modernistic（现代主义的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{modernize}{'mɒdənaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{v. 使现代化}
  \wordexample{modernization（现代化）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{modest}{'mɒdist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{a. 谦逊的；谨慎的；适度的，有节制的；（事物）朴素的；端庄的；稳重的；不太大的}
  \wordexample{modestly（谨慎地，审慎地；适度地）}
  \wordexample{modest little house（朴素的小房子） modest in speech, behavior and dress（言行、着装端庄的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{modesty}{'mɒdəsti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 谦逊，质朴；端庄；羞怯}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{modicum}{'mɒdikəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 少量，一点点}
  \wordexample{a modicum of sleep（小睡） a modicum of effort（微薄的努力） a modicum of wine（少量的葡萄酒）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{modify}{'mɒdifai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{v. 修改，变更；缓和；【语】修饰}
  \wordexample{modifier（【语】修饰语（如形容词、副词等））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{modification}{ˌmɒdifi'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 修改，变更；缓和；【语】修饰 *fic (= make)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{modish}{'məudiʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{a. 流行的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{module}{'mɒdju:l}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 模数（建筑中使用的度量单位）；【计】模块；组件，模件；（宇宙飞船的）舱}
  \wordexample{modular（组件的）}
  \wordexample{memory module（存储器模块） command module（（宇宙飞船的）指令舱） lunar module（（从宇宙飞船母体上分离出来的）登月舱）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{modus operandi}{ˌməudəs ɒpə'rændi:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 工作方法；一贯手法，惯技 *oper (= work)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{modus vivendi}{ˌməudəs vi'vendi:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MOD}
  \wordsense{n. 生活方式；妥协，权宜之计 *viv (= life)}
\end{word}

\chapter{QUI}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 qui 意为 quiet，to rest。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{acquit}{ə'kwit}
  \wordbreakdown{让某人心境平和 ac<ad (= to) + qui (= quiet)}
  \wordsense{vt. `sb + of + sth` 宣判……无罪；使（自己）作出某种表现；使（自己）履行（或完成）}
  \wordexample{acquittal 宣判无罪；履行 acquittance（债务的）清偿}
  \wordexample{The jury acquitted the prisoner after a short deliberation.（陪审团稍作审议，决定宣判犯人无罪。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{acquiesce}{ˌækwi'es}
  \wordbreakdown{面对某事变得安静 ac<ad (= to, intensive) + qui (= quiet)}
  \wordsense{vi. `to + sth` 默认；默从}
  \wordphrase{acquiescence 默认}
  \wordphrase{acquiescent 默认的}
  \wordexample{I finally acquiesced to my parents' demands that I stop smoking.（最后我还是默从了我父母的要求，答应戒烟。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{disquieting}{dis'kwaiətiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{不让心境平和、安宁的 dis (= not) + qui (= quiet, rest)}
  \wordsense{a. 使不安的，令人担忧的}
  \wordphrase{disquiet 使不安}
  \wordphrase{disquietude 不安，担忧}
  \wordphrase{disquietingly 不安地，担忧地}
  \wordexample{The soldier could not sleep due to the disquieting sounds of battle in the distance.（从远处传来的令人不安的战斗声使那个士兵无法入睡。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{tranquility}{træŋ'kwiləti}
  \wordbreakdown{完全安静的、平稳的状态 tran<trans (= intensive) + qui (= quiet, rest)}
  \wordsense{n. 平静；平稳}
  \wordphrase{tranquil 平静的；平稳的}
  \wordphrase{tranquilizer 镇静剂}
  \wordphrase{tranquilize 使安静；使镇定}
  \wordexample{The tranquility here at Loon Lake is marvelous.（卢恩湖非常平静。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{quiescent}{kwai'esnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 QUI}
  \wordsense{a. 静止的，不动的}
  \wordexample{quiescence（静止，不活动；沉默）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{inquietude}{in'kwaiətju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 QUI}
  \wordsense{n. 不安，担心 *in (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{quietism}{'kwaiətizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 QUI}
  \wordsense{n. 【宗】寂静主义（天主教神修学派的主张）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{quietude}{'kwaiətju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 QUI}
  \wordsense{n. 寂静；宁静}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{requiem}{'rekwiəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 QUI}
  \wordsense{n.（天主教）安魂弥撒；安魂曲；挽歌 *re (= again, intensive)}
\end{word}

\chapter{HER, HES}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 her，hes 意为 to stick。同义词根有 clam，cleav，visc 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{adhere}{əd'hiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{紧贴在某物上 ad (= to) + her (= stick)}
  \wordsense{vi. `to + sth` 黏附；坚持；追随}
  \wordphrase{adherence 附着；坚持；忠诚 adhesion 附着，黏着；坚持}
  \wordexample{adherent 黏着的；`(n.)` 追随者 adhesive 带黏性的；`(n.)` 黏合剂}
  \wordexample{Fried foods often adhere to the surface of a skillet.（油炸食品往往会粘在平底煎锅的表面上。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{coherent}{kəu'hiərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{粘贴在一起的 co<com (= together) + her (= stick)}
  \wordsense{a. 黏着的；协调一致的；条理清楚的}
  \wordphrase{cohere 黏着}
  \wordphrase{cohesive 有黏着力的}
  \wordexample{coherence 黏着（性）；凝聚（性）；连贯性 cohesion 黏着；凝聚（力）；团结（力）}
  \wordexample{I felt drowsy after the operation, but mentally coherent.（手术之后我昏昏欲睡，不过意识是清醒的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{hesitant}{'hezitənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER, HES}
  \wordsense{a. 犹豫的，踌躇的，迟疑的}
  \wordexample{hesitantly（犹豫地）}
  \wordexample{hesitance / hesitancy（踌躇，犹豫）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{hesitate}{'heziteit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER, HES}
  \wordsense{v. 踌躇，犹豫}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{hesitation}{ˌhezi'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER, HES}
  \wordsense{n. 踌躇，犹豫}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{incoherent}{ˌinkəu'hiərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER, HES}
  \wordsense{a. 不连贯的；无条理的；语无伦次的 *in (= not), co<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{incoherence（前后不一致；无条理）}
  \wordexample{so drunk as to be quite incoherent（醉得语无伦次的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{inherent}{in'hiərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER, HES}
  \wordsense{a. 与生俱来的，固有的；内在的 *in (= in)}
  \wordexample{inherently（固有地；天性地）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FLU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 flu 意为 to flow。同义词根有来自希腊语的 rrh 和来自拉丁语的 lix，man 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affluence}{'æfluəns}
  \wordbreakdown{继续、不断地流出 af<ad (= to, intensive) + flu (= flow)}
  \wordsense{n. 富饶，富有；丰富}
  \wordphrase{affluent 富饶的，富有的；丰富的}
  \wordexample{Affluence is a way of life for most American physicians.（大多数美国医生都过着富有的生活。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{fluctuate}{'flʌktʃueit}
  \wordbreakdown{流动 flu (= flow)}
  \wordsense{vi.（物价、温度等）上下变动，波动；动摇}
  \wordphrase{fluctuation 上下变动，波动；动摇}
  \wordexample{The exchange rate fluctuates wildly, sometimes as much as 10\% in one day.（汇率变动幅度很大，有时候甚至一天之内变动达 10\%。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{fluent}{'flu:ənt}
  \wordbreakdown{话从嘴中说出的 flu (= flow)}
  \wordsense{a. 流畅的，流利的}
  \wordphrase{fluency 流畅，流利}
  \wordphrase{fluently 流畅地，流利地}
  \wordexample{He's fluent in both Chinese and Japanese.（他的汉语和日语讲得都很流利。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{confluence}{'kɒnfluəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{n.（江河的）交汇（处） *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{confluent（汇合的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{effluent}{'efluənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{n.（江河、湖泊、阴沟等流出的）水流；流出物 *ef<ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{efflux}{'eflʌks}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{n.（液体或气体的）流出；流出物 *ef<ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{flue}{flu:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{n. 暖气管；烟道}
  \wordexample{clean the flues of soot（把满是烟灰的烟道打扫干净）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{fluid}{'flu:id}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{n. 流体（液体与气体的总称）；液}
  \wordsense{a.（像气体、液体一样）流动的；（思想等）不稳定的}
  \wordexample{fluidity（流动性；易变性）}
  \wordexample{fluid opinions / plans（易变的意见 / 计划）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{flume}{flu:m}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{n.（用来转动磨米房的水碓或运送木材的）水槽，水道}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{flurry}{'flʌri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{n. 阵风，阵雨，阵雪；慌张}
  \wordsense{v. 使（某人）慌张}
  \wordexample{in a flurry of alarm（一阵慌张）}
  \wordexample{Don't get flurried.（别慌。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{flush}{flʌʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{v.（脸）发红；使得意；用水冲洗；（水）奔流}
  \wordsense{n. 红晕；激动，兴奋，茂盛；（草木等的）发芽}
  \wordexample{The girl flushed when the boy spoke to her.（男孩跟女孩搭话时，女孩的脸红了。）}
  \wordexample{flush the pan（冲洗平底锅）}
  \wordexample{in the first flush of victory（沉浸在胜利带来的最初的喜悦中）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{fluvial}{'flu:viəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{a. 河流的，生长在河流中的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{flux}{flʌks}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLU}
  \wordsense{n.（水的）流动，流出；不断变动；焊剂；涨潮}
  \wordexample{in a state of flux（不断变动的）}
  \wordexample{the flux and reflux（涨潮落潮；《喻》不断消长）}
\end{word}

\chapter{LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 leg，lect 有 law，to gather，to choose，to read，to send 等多种含义。其变形有 lig，leag 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{allegiance}{ə'li:dʒəns}
  \wordbreakdown{朝向法的心 al<ad (= to) + leg (= law)}
  \wordsense{n. 忠诚；拥戴}
  \wordexample{I don't feel much allegiance to my company.（我对我们公司没有多少效忠之心。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{negligible}{'neglidʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不收集的 neg (= not, deny) + lig<lect (= gather)}
  \wordsense{a. 可以忽略的，无关紧要的，不重要的，微不足道的；不注意的，疏忽的，怠慢的}
  \wordexample{A negligible amount of marijuana was discovered in his automobile.（在他的车内发现了极少量的大麻。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{diligent}{'dilidʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{不同地、个别地选择的 di<dis (= apart) + lig<leg (= choose)}
  \wordsense{a. 勤奋的；勤勉的}
  \wordphrase{diligence 勤勉}
  \wordphrase{diligently 勤奋地}
  \wordexample{She is a diligent worker though she lacks imagination.（她虽然缺乏想像力，但工作还是很勤奋的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{eclectic}{i'klektik}
  \wordbreakdown{从某些东西当中挑选的 ec<ex (= out) + lect (= choose)}
  \wordsense{a. 折中的；兼收并蓄的；（兴趣爱好等）不拘一格的}
  \wordsense{n. 折中主义者，折中学派}
  \wordphrase{eclecticism 折中主义；折中学派}
  \wordphrase{eclectically 折中地}
  \wordexample{She has eclectic taste, as you can tell by her living room.（从她的客厅可以看出她不拘一格的品味。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{predilection}{ˌpri:di'lekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{事先个别地挑出来 pre (= before) + di (= apart) + lect (= choose)}
  \wordsense{n. 嗜好，偏爱}
  \wordexample{I have a strong predilection for sweets.（我特别偏爱甜食。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{legible}{'ledʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{能阅读的 leg (= read)}
  \wordsense{a.（字迹等）易读的；易辨认的}
  \wordphrase{legibility 易读，易识别}
  \wordexample{The postmark isn't legible. It might say "New York".（邮戳看不清，可能写的是“纽约”。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{illegible}{i'ledʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{无法阅读的 il<in (= not) + leg (= read)}
  \wordsense{a. 难读的，难辨认的}
  \wordphrase{illegibility 不可辨认}
  \wordphrase{illegibly 难辨认地}
  \wordexample{The secretary couldn't type the letter. Her boss's handwriting is illegible.（秘书无法录入那封信，因为老板的字迹难以辨认。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{allege}{ə'ledʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{用力向某人寄送 al<ad (= to, intensive) + leg (= send)}
  \wordsense{vt.（无充分证据地）主张，断言；辩解}
  \wordphrase{alleged 被断定的}
  \wordexample{allegation（无充分证据的）主张}
  \wordexample{He alleges that he was not speeding.（他辩解说自己没有超速行驶。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{relegate}{'religeit}
  \wordbreakdown{向后输送 re (= back) + leg (= send)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使地位降低，使降职；移交；放逐；委托}
  \wordphrase{relegation 降职；移交；委托；放逐}
  \wordexample{He relegated his stepson to the broken-down bedroom over the garage.（他把他的继子赶到车库上面那个破旧的卧室去了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{intellectual}{ˌintə'lektʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{在中间选择 intel<inter (= between) + lect (= choose, gather)}
  \wordsense{n. 有很高智力的人；知识分子}
  \wordsense{a. 智力的；智力超群的}
  \wordphrase{intellect 智力；思维能力；【总称】知识界}
  \wordexample{While bright, I hardly think he qualifies as an intellectual.（他虽然很聪明，不过我并不认为他够格做一个知识分子。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{legendary}{'ledʒəndri}
  \wordbreakdown{被传读下来的 leg (= read)}
  \wordsense{a. 传说的；《口》著名的}
  \wordphrase{legend 传说；传奇文学}
  \wordexample{His appetite is legendary. He can actually eat 25 hot dogs at once!（他的胃口大是出了名的，他一次可以吃掉 25 个热狗！）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{legitimate}{li'dʒitimit}
  \wordbreakdown{合乎法律的 leg (= law)}
  \wordsense{a. 合法的；合理的；正统的；嫡出的}
  \wordphrase{legitimatize 使合法化；承认嫡出}
  \wordexample{legitimacy 合法；正统（性）；嫡出}
  \wordexample{Is corporal punishment a legitimate teaching method? I think not.（体罚是一种合理的教育方式吗？我认为不是。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{legal}{'li:gəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 法律的；法定的；合法的 *leg (= law)}
  \wordexample{legalism（守法主义）}
  \wordexample{legally（法律上；合法地）}
  \wordexample{illegal（非法的，违法的，不合法的） legal adviser（法律顾问） take legal action（提起诉讼） legal holiday（法定假日） free legal aid（法律援助（指法庭指定辩护律师为没有能力聘请律师或进行诉讼的人提供的援助））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{legality}{li:'gæləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 合法（性） *leg (= law)}
  \wordexample{legality of an act（行为的合法性）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{legalize}{'li:gəlaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{v. 使合法化 *leg (= law)}
  \wordexample{legalize the sale of alcoholic drinks（使销售酒类合法化）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{legislate}{'ledʒisleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{v. 制定法律 *leg (= law), lat (= carry, produce)}
  \wordexample{legislation（立法，制定法律；法律） legislator（立法者；立法委员；国会议员）}
  \wordexample{legislature（立法机关；《美》州议会）}
  \wordexample{legislate against gambling（制定法律禁止赌博）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{legislative}{'ledʒislətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 立法的；法律规定的 *leg (= law), lat (= carry, produce)}
  \wordexample{legislative body（立法机关） legislative reforms（立法改革） legislative prerogative（立法特权）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{privilege}{'privilidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 特权；荣幸 *priv (= single, peculiar), leg (= law)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{privileged}{'privilidʒd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 享有特权的 *priv (= single, peculiar), leg (= law)}
  \wordexample{the privileged classes（特权阶层） underprivileged（没有享受适当权益的；贫困的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{analects}{'ænəlekts}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 论集，文选 *ana (= again), lect (= gather)}
  \wordexample{The Confucian Analects（《论语》）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{collect}{kə'lekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{v. 收集；收取（税金、捐款等）；接走；使（情绪）镇定}
  \wordsense{a./ad. 电话、发报等由受话人（或收报人）付款的（地） *col<com (= together), lect (= gather)}
  \wordexample{collected（镇定的）}
  \wordexample{collection（收集，采集） collector（收集者；收藏家；采集者）}
  \wordexample{collect call（对方付费电话）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{collective}{kə'lektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 集体的，共同的 *col<com (= together), lect (= gather)}
  \wordexample{collectively（全体地，共同地）}
  \wordexample{collectivism（集体主义）}
  \wordexample{collectivize（使集体化） collective farm（集体农场） collective leadership（集体领导制） collective security（集体安全） collective ownership（集体所有制） collective bargaining（（劳资双方的）集体谈判）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{colleague}{'kɒli:g}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 同事 *col<com (= together), leag<leg (= gather)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{college}{'kɒlidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 大学；学院；专科学校；团体，协会；群 *col<com (= together), leg (= gather)}
  \wordexample{college of music（音乐学院） college of surgeons（外科医生协会） college of cardinals（【天主教】红衣主教团（罗马教皇的枢密院）） college of bees（蜂群）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{collegiate}{kə'li:dʒiət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 大学（生）的；大学生用的 *col<com (= together), leg (= gather)}
  \wordexample{collegiate life（大学生活） collegiate dictionary（大学生词典）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{legion}{'li:dʒən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 古罗马军团；众多 *leg (= gather)}
  \wordexample{legionary（军团的）}
  \wordexample{Their numbers are legion.（他们数量众多。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{neglect}{ni'glekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{v./n. 疏忽（义务等），忽视，忽略 *neg (= not), lect (= gather)}
  \wordexample{neglectful（疏忽的，不注意的）}
  \wordexample{neglect one's family / appearance（忽视家庭 / 外表） neglect of duty（玩忽职守）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{negligence}{'neglidʒəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 过失（罪）；不注意，粗心；疏忽，玩忽；不修边幅 *neg (= not), lig<leg (= gather)}
  \wordexample{negligence of dress（不在意穿着）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{negligent}{'neglidʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 不注意的，粗心大意的 *neg (= not), lig<leg (= gather)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dialect}{'daiəlekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 方言，地方话；（某个阶层的）行话 *dia (= through), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{dialectal（方言的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dialectic}{ˌdaiə'lektik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 辩证的 *dia (= through), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{dialectician（辩证学家）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{dialectical}{ˌdaiə'lektikəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 方言的；辩证的 *dia (= through), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{dialectical materialism（辩证唯物主义）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{elect}{i'lekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{v. 选举；选择，决定}
  \wordsense{a. 当选的；选中的；当选而尚未上任的；`(the\textasciitilde{})` 被选定的人 *e<ex (= out), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{governor-elect（下一任州长（当选后，就任前））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{election}{i'lekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 选举；当选 *e<ex (= out), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{primary election（预选） general election（大选） election expenses（选举费用） election campaign（选举运动） by-election / special election（补缺选举 / 特别选举） off-year election（《美》非大选年的选举） election results（选举结果） election district（选区） Election Day（《美》选举日）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{elective}{i'lektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 选修的；可以选择的；有选举权的；由选举产生的 *e<ex (= out), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{elective course（选修科目） elective office（由选举产生的官职）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{elector}{i'lektə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 选举人 *e<ex (= out), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{electorate（选民）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{electoral}{i'lektorəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 选举（人）的；有关选举的 *e<ex (= out), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{electoral roll / register（选民名册） electoral college（《美》总统选举团（负责选举总统和副总统））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{elegant}{'eligənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 优雅的；精致的 *e<ex (= out), leg (= choose)}
  \wordexample{elegance（优雅）}
  \wordexample{elegant manners（优雅的举止）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{eligible}{'elidʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 合格的，有资格被选中的；合适的 *e<ex (= out), lig<leg (= choose)}
  \wordexample{eligibility（合格；被选举资格）}
  \wordexample{eligible for membership in a society（有资格成为社团会员的） an eligible young man（合适的年轻人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{intelligence}{in'telidʒəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 智能，智力；智慧；信息；（尤指有关军事机密的）情报 *intel<inter (= between), lig<leg (= choose)}
  \wordexample{a man of much intelligence（非常聪明的人） intelligence agent（情报人员，间谍） intelligence quotient（智商） intelligence officer（情报官）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{intelligent}{in'telidʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 有才智的；聪明的 *intel<inter (= between), lig<leg (= choose)}
  \wordexample{intelligent child（聪明的孩子） intelligent answers to questions（对问题的机智回答）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{intelligentsia}{inˌtelidʒent'siə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 【总称】`(the\textasciitilde{})` 知识分子 *intel<inter (= between), lig<leg (= choose)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{intelligible}{in'telidʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 可理解的，（意思）明了的 *intel<inter (= between), lig<leg (= choose)}
  \wordexample{intelligibility（易理解，明了）}
  \wordexample{intelligible speech（易听懂的演讲） intelligible explanation（易理解的说明）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{select}{si'lekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{v. 选择，选拔，挑选}
  \wordsense{a. 精选的，选拔出来的；选择成员时严格的 *se (= apart, away), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{selector（选择者，选拔者）}
  \wordexample{select committee（《英》（在下议院中受命作某项特别调查的）小型特别委员会）}
  \wordexample{select by vote（投票选拔）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{selection}{si'lekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 选拔，选择，精选；精选品；选集 *se (= apart, away)}
  \wordexample{selection committee（选拔委员会） natural selection（【生】自然选择） selection from the works of Hemingway（海明威选集）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{selective}{si'lektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 选择性的；有选择能力的；淘汰的 *se (= apart, away), lect (= choose)}
  \wordexample{selectivity（选择性）}
  \wordexample{selective service（选拔性征兵制）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{elegiac}{ˌeli'dʒaiək}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{a.（韵律）挽歌体的；哀伤的，哀悼的 *e<ex (= out), leg (= read)}
  \wordexample{elegiac couplets（挽歌体对句）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{elegy}{'elidʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n.（为死者的）哀歌，挽歌 *e<ex (= out), leg (= read)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{lectern}{'lektən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n.（教堂的）讲经台 *leg (= read)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{lecture}{'lektʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 演讲；谴责，训话}
  \wordsense{v. 讲课；演讲；训诫，训斥 *lect (= read)}
  \wordexample{lecturer（（特指大学的）讲师）}
  \wordexample{lectureship（（大学的）讲师的职位）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{delegate}{'deligit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 代表}
  \wordsense{vt. 代表；指派……为代表；授（权） *de (= away, off), leg (= send)}
  \wordexample{delegacy（代表团；代表制度）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{delegation}{ˌdeli'geiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 代表团；授权，委托 *de (= away, off), leg (= send)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{legacy}{'legəsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 遗产，留给后人的东西；遗赠 *leg (= send)}
  \wordexample{the legacy of the Korean War（朝鲜战争遗留下的东西） legacy of hatred（祖辈留下的积怨）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{legate}{'legət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 教皇的使节 *leg (= send)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{legatee}{ˌlegə'ti:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 遗产受赠人 *leg (= send)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{legation}{li'geiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 公使馆；公使馆全体人员 *leg (= send)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{recollect}{ˌrekə'lekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{v. 想起，回想 *re (= back), col<com (= together, completely), lect (= send)}
  \wordexample{recollect childhood（回想童年）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{recollection}{ˌrekə'lekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 回想，回顾；记忆（力）；追忆的往事 *re (= back), col<com (= together, completely), lect (= send)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sacrilege}{'sækrəlidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}
  \wordsense{n. 亵渎神圣 *sacr (= holy), leg (= send)}
  \wordexample{sacrilegious（犯渎圣罪的）}
\end{word}

\chapter{PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 par 意为 to arrange, to appear, to produce, equal。其变形有 pair, per, pir, peer 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{apparel}{ə'pærəl}
  \wordbreakdown{靠近排列 ap<ad (= to, near) + par (= arrange)}
  \wordsense{n. 衣着；服饰}
  \wordsense{vt. 给……穿衣}
  \wordexample{My teacher told me that shorts aren't appropriate apparel for class.（老师告诉我穿短裤来上课不合适。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{imperative}{im'perətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面排列、整理的 im<in (= in, intensive) + per<par (= arrange)}
  \wordsense{a. 必需的；紧急的；必须服从的，强制的；命令式的}
  \wordsense{n. 命令}
  \wordexample{It's imperative to remove your shoes once you enter the temple.（进入寺院时，你必须脱掉鞋子。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{imperious}{im'piəriəs}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面排列或整理的 im<in (= in, intensive) + per<par (= arrange)}
  \wordsense{a. 飞扬跋扈的，傲慢的，专横的；紧急的；迫切的}
  \wordexample{She's an imperious little tot, always bossing around her young cousins.（她是一个专横的小女孩，经常对她的堂弟妹颐指气使。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{irreparable}{i'repərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{无法重新整顿或安排的 ir<in (= not) + re (= again) + par (= arrange)}
  \wordsense{a. 无法修补的；无法挽回的}
  \wordexample{A broken teacup is irreparable, no matter what the glue package says.（无论胶水的外包装上怎么写，摔碎的茶杯都是无法修补的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{apparition}{ˌæpə'riʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{出现在附近 ap<ad (= to, near) + par (= appear)}
  \wordsense{n. 幽灵，鬼怪；幻影；特异景象}
  \wordexample{She claims to see apparitions in the graveyard at night.（她坚持说晚上在墓地看到幽灵。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{transparent}{træns'pærənt}
  \wordbreakdown{通过某种东西出现、显示到另一边的 trans (= through, across) + par (= appear)}
  \wordsense{a. 透明的；清澈的；明了的，明白的}
  \wordexample{transparency / transparence 透明（性）；幻灯片}
  \wordexample{The transparent turquoise waters of the Caribbean are spectacular.（加勒比海透明的蓝绿色海水非常壮观。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{disparage}{dis'pæridʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{认为某人和自己不平等 dis (= not) + par (= equal)}
  \wordsense{vt. 轻视，蔑视；中伤，诽谤；贬低}
  \wordphrase{disparagement 轻视；诽谤}
  \wordphrase{disparagingly 轻视地}
  \wordexample{Insecure people often disparage the achievements of others.（不安的人总是会贬低别人的成绩。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{disparity}{dis'pærəti}
  \wordbreakdown{不同等 dis (= not) + par (= equal)}
  \wordsense{n. 不同，不等}
  \wordexample{The disparity between rich and poor in Venezuela is startling.（委内瑞拉的贫富差距令人吃惊。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{impair}{im'peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{使与以前不一样 im<in (= not) + pair<par (= equal)}
  \wordsense{vt. 损伤；削弱}
  \wordphrase{impairment 损害，损伤}
  \wordexample{Alcohol had so impaired his faculties that he was unable to drive.（酒精极大地损伤了他的机能，以至于他开不了车了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{apparatus}{ˌæpə'reitəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n.（一套）装置，设备；器械，器具；（身体的）器官；（政党或地下活动的）机构 *ap<ad (= near), par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{heating apparatus（暖气） chemical apparatus（化学器械） electric apparatus（电器设备） digestive apparatus（消化器官） bureaucratic apparatus（官僚机构） espionage apparatus（间谍机构）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{empire}{'empaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 帝国 *em<en (= intensive), pir<par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{emperor（皇帝） empress（皇后；女皇）}
  \wordexample{the Roman Empire（罗马帝国） the British Empire（大英帝国） Empire City（帝国城，纽约市的别名） Empire State（帝国州，纽约州的别名） Empire State Building（纽约帝国大厦（102 层，381 米，1931 年竣工，1950 年在大楼顶部修建高 67.7 米的电视塔））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{imperial}{im'piəriəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 帝国的；皇帝的；宏大的；专横的 *im<in (= intensive), per<par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{Imperial City（帝都，罗马的别名） His Imperial Majesty（陛下） imperial president（专横的总统）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{imperialism}{im'piəriəlizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 帝国主义；（领土）扩张主义 *im<in (= intensive), per<par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{imperialist（帝国主义者）}
  \wordexample{imperialistic（帝国主义的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{parade}{pə'reid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 阅兵，阅兵式；行列；游行；炫耀}
  \wordsense{v.（列队）行进；炫耀，夸耀 *par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{hold a parade（举行阅兵式） march in parade（列队行进） political parade（政治性游行） be on parade（在游行） parade ground（阅兵场） make a parade of one's virtues（夸耀自己的优点） parade one's abilities（炫耀自己的能力）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pare}{peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{v. 削（水果等的）皮；修剪（指甲等）；修掉（边、角等）；消减 *par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{parings（削下、剥下的东西）}
  \wordexample{pare one's nails（剪指甲） pare (= peel) an apple（削苹果皮） pare down（削减，减少）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{prepare}{pri'peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{v. 准备 *pre (= before), par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{preparedness（准备）}
  \wordexample{be prepared to（准备好做……）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{preparation}{ˌprepə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 准备，预备；（药品的）调制，配制；配制好的东西 *pre (= before), par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{preparations for war（战备） make preparations for a voyage（准备航海） pharmaceutical preparation（药物制剂）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{preparatory}{pri'pærətri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 准备的，预备的 *pre (= before), par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{preparatory school（预备学校，预科）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{repair}{ri'peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{v. 修理；修补}
  \wordsense{n. 修补 *re (= again), pair<par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{repairable（可修理的）}
  \wordexample{repairer（修理工）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{reparable}{'repərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a.（损失等）可补偿的；可修缮的 *re (= again), par (= arrange)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{reparation}{ˌrepə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 补偿；赔偿；（pl.）（战败国的）赔款 *re (= again), par (= arrange)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{separate}{'sepəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{v. 分离，分开；分散}
  \wordsense{a. 分离的，分开的；单独的}
  \wordsense{n. 不是成套的女装 *se (= away), par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{separately（个别地）}
  \wordexample{separator（（牛奶的）脱脂器） separatist（（政治上或宗教上的）分离主义者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{separation}{ˌsepə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 分离；脱离；【律】（夫妇）分居 *se (= apart, away), par (= arrange)}
  \wordexample{separation of powers（分权） judicial separation（（法庭判决的）夫妇分居） after a separation of five years（分离五年后）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{separable}{'sepərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 可分离的 *se (= apart, away)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{apparent}{ə'pærənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 明显的；明白的；外观的，表面的 *ap<ad (= near), par (= appear)}
  \wordexample{apparently（明白地；外观上地，表面上地）}
  \wordexample{in spite of her apparent indifference（尽管她表面上冷淡）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{appearance}{ə'piərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 出现；外观，外表，外貌 *ap<ad (= near), pear<par (= appear)}
  \wordexample{appear（出现；（演员、歌手、讲演者等）露面）}
  \wordexample{make an appearance（演出，露面）}
  \wordexample{to / by all appearances（从外表看）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{compare}{kəm'peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{v. 比较，对照；比喻作；（可）与……相比；匹敌 *com (= together), par (= equal)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{comparison}{kəm'pærisn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 比较，对照；匹敌；比喻 *com (= together), par (= equal)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{comparable}{'kɒmpərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 可匹敌的；可比较的 *com (= together), par (= equal)}
  \wordexample{His achievements are comparable with the best.（他可以同成就最高的人相匹敌。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{comparative}{kəm'pærətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 比较的；【语】比较级的 *com (= together), par (= equal)}
  \wordexample{comparatively（比较地；有点）}
  \wordexample{comparative literature（比较文学） comparative religion（比较宗教） comparative politics（比较政治学）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{compeer}{kɒm'piə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 地位相等的人；同伴 *com (= together), peer<par (= equal)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{disparate}{'dispərət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a.（质、量等）无法比较的，根本不同的}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）无法比较的事物 *dis (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{pair}{peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 一对，一双，一副；一对（夫妻、情侣、舞伴等）}
  \wordsense{v. 成双，成对 *pair<par (= equal)}
  \wordexample{the happy pair（幸福的一对）}
  \wordexample{in pairs（成双地，成对地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{par}{pa:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 同等；平价；票面价值；平均，标准}
  \wordexample{nominal par（票面价值） issue par（发行价格） above / below par（高于 / 低于票面价值） on a par with（和……不相上下） par of exchange（外汇平价）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{parity}{'pærəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 同等，平等，同位；平价 *par (= equal)}
  \wordexample{parity of exchange（外汇平价）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{peer}{piə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 同辈；（社会地位或能力）同等的（或可以匹敌的）人；（英国的）贵族}
  \wordsense{v. 凝视，盯着看 *peer<par (= equal)}
  \wordexample{peeress（女贵族） peerage（【总称】贵族；贵族头衔；贵族名册）}
  \wordexample{be raised to the peerage（晋升为贵族）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{peerless}{'piələs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 无可匹敌的 *peer<par (= equal), less (= without)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{umpire}{'ʌmpaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 仲裁人；（比赛的）裁判员}
  \wordsense{v. 仲裁；裁判 *um<un (= not), pir<par (= equal)}
  \wordexample{act as umpire（担任仲裁；担任裁判）}
  \wordexample{umpire a cricket match（担任板球比赛的裁判）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{vampire}{'væmpaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 吸血鬼；吸血蝙蝠；高利贷者 *vamp (= temptress), pir<par (= equal)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{oviparous}{ˌəuvi'pərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a.（鸟、鱼、爬虫类）卵生的 *ovi (= egg), par (= produce)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{parental}{pə'rentl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 父母亲的 *par (= produce)}
  \wordexample{parents（双亲，父母） parentage（父母的身份）}
  \wordexample{parental authority（亲权） parental anxieties（父母的担忧） parental care（父母的照顾）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{parricide}{'pærisaid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 杀父母；杀父（或母）的人 *par (= produce), cid (= kill)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{parturition}{ˌpa:tjuə'riʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{n. 分娩，生产 *par (= produce)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{viviparous}{vi'vipərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}
  \wordsense{a. 胎生的 *viv (= life), par (= produce)}
\end{word}

\chapter{PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 pend, pens 意为 to hang, to weigh, to pay, to consider。其变形有 pond 和 penc。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{append}{ə'pend}
  \wordbreakdown{悬挂在某物之上 ap<ad (= to, add) + pend (= hang)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sth 附加，添加；悬挂}
  \wordphrase{appendix 附录 appendage 附加物 appendicitis 阑尾炎}
  \wordexample{She usually appends a postscript to her letters.（她通常在信上加一些附言。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{compendium}{kəm'pendiəm}
  \wordbreakdown{从某物上分离出来悬挂 com (= with) + pend (= hang, weigh)}
  \wordsense{n. 概论，概要；简编}
  \wordphrase{compendious 简洁的，简明的}
  \wordexample{This guidebook contains a compendium of common French idioms and expressions.（这本指南手册包括法语中常见的习惯用语和表达法的简编。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{impending}{im'pendiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{悬挂在上面的 im<in (= on) + pend (= hang, weigh)}
  \wordsense{a. 迫近的；悬挂的，悬置的}
  \wordexample{I'm dreading my impending tax audit with the Internal Revenue Service.（对国内收入署即将对我进行的税务审计，我感到很害怕。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{compensate}{'kɒmpenseit}
  \wordbreakdown{用某物来偿还 com (= with) + pens (= pay, hang)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + for + sth 补偿；赔偿}
  \wordsense{vi. for + sth 补偿；弥补}
  \wordphrase{compensation 赔偿，补偿；《美》报酬}
  \wordphrase{compensatory 补偿的；报酬的}
  \wordexample{I gave the girl 100 dollars as compensation for her pain when my dog bit her.（我的狗咬了那个小女孩，作为对她所受痛苦的补偿，我给了她 100 美元。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{propensity}{prə'pensəti}
  \wordbreakdown{预先挂好的 pro (= forth) + pens (= hang, weigh)}
  \wordsense{n. 倾向；习性}
  \wordexample{I have a propensity to tap my fingers when I'm nervous.（我在紧张的时候会弹手指。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{indispensable}{ˌindis'pensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不分开悬挂的 in (= not) + dis (= apart) + pens (= hang, weigh)}
  \wordsense{a. 不可缺少的，必需的}
  \wordphrase{indispensability 不可缺}
  \wordexample{A thesaurus is an indispensable writing tool for any university student.（词典是所有大学生不可或缺的写作工具。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{expend}{ik'spend}
  \wordbreakdown{支付以使某物完尽 ex (= out) + pend (= pay, weigh)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + on + sth 消费；耗尽，用光}
  \wordphrase{expenditure 支出，经费 expense 费用，支出；牺牲}
  \wordphrase{expensive 昂贵的 expendable 可花费的；可牺牲的}
  \wordexample{About 28\% of the library's annual budget is expended on new acquisitions.（那家图书馆约 28\% 的年预算用来购置新书。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{recompense}{'rekəmpens}
  \wordbreakdown{用某种东西偿还 re (= back) + com (= with) + pens (= pay)}
  \wordsense{vt. 报答；赔偿；补偿}
  \wordexample{The insurance company recompensed the people injured in the auto accident.（保险公司对在车祸中受伤的人给予了赔偿。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{depend}{di'pend}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{v. 依靠，依赖；取决于；相信 *de (= down), pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{dependable（可信赖的，可靠的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dependant}{di'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 受抚养的家属 *de (= down), pend (= hang)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{dependent}{di'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 依赖的，依靠的；由……决定的，取决于……的}
  \wordsense{n. 受抚养的家属 *de (= down), pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{dependent on his wife's earnings（依靠妻子的收入的） dependent upon weather（取决于天气的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dependence}{di'pendəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 依靠，依赖；信赖 *de (= down), pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{drug dependence（药物依赖）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dependency}{di'pendənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 附属国，附属地 *de (= down), pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{The island is no longer a dependency of the USA.（那座小岛不再是美国的附属地了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{independence}{ˌindi'pendəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 独立，自立 *in (= not), de (= down), pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{Independence Day（美国独立纪念日（7 月 4 日）） Independence Hall（独立纪念馆（位于费城，内部保存有自由钟）） The Declaration of Independence（（美国的）《独立宣言》） Independence Movement（独立运动）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{independent}{ˌindi'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 独立的，自主的}
  \wordsense{n. 无党派人士 *in (= not), de (= down), pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{independently（独立地，自主地）}
  \wordexample{independent of（独立于） independent research（独立的研究） Vote for the independents at the next election.（在下一次选举中投票给无党派人士。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{penchant}{'pentʃənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 强烈的倾向；嗜好 *penc<pens (= hang)}
  \wordexample{have a penchant for（嗜好……）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{pendant}{'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 垂饰；吊灯 *pend (= hang)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pendent}{'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 悬挂的，下垂的；向外伸出的；未决的，未定的 *pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{pendent rocks（向外突出的岩石）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pending}{'pendiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 悬而未决的}
  \wordsense{prep. 在……过程中，在……期间；在……之前 *pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{a patent pending（待批专利） The lawsuit was then pending.（那次诉讼当时未作判决。） pending the negotiations（在谈判之前） pending her decision（她作决定之前）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{pendulous}{'pendjuləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 悬垂的；摇摆的 *pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{the pendulous nests of weaver-birds（织巢鸟悬垂着的巢）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pendulum}{'pendjuləm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n.（钟等的）摆；摇摆不定的人（或物） *pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{the swing of the pendulums（摇摆不定的舆论；（政党等的）胜负交替）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{perpendicular}{ˌpə:pən'dikjulə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 成直角的，垂直的；直立的}
  \wordsense{n. 垂直面 *per (= completely), pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{out of (the) perpendicular（不垂直）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suspend}{sə'spend}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{v. 悬挂；使悬浮（于液体、空气中）；中止；暂缓作出（决定等） *sus<sub (= under), pend (= hang)}
  \wordexample{lamps suspended from the ceiling（悬挂在顶棚上的灯） dust suspended in the air（飘浮在空中的灰尘） suspend payment（中止支付） suspend judgment（暂缓判决） suspend a football player（暂时中止某足球运动员参赛） suspended animation（假死状态） suspended sentence（缓刑）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{suspense}{sə'spens}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 担心，不安；紧张感；（小说、电影等的）悬念 *sus<sub (= under), pens (= hang)}
  \wordexample{in great suspense（极为不安） keep her in suspense for five days already（已经让她担心五天了）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{suspension}{sə'spenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 悬挂；悬浮（状态）；悬而未决；暂令停职；中止 *sus<sub (= under), pens (= hang)}
  \wordexample{suspension bridge（吊桥） suspension of a congressman（中止议员资格）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{suspender}{sə'spendə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n.《美》裤子背带；《英》吊袜带 *sus<sub (= under), pend (= hang)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{dispense}{di'spens}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{v. 分配，分发；配药；（\textasciitilde{} with）免除 *dis (= apart, away), pens (= weigh)}
  \wordexample{dispenser（分配者；药剂师；分配器（可以不打开盖子而直接抽取物质的容器））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dispensation}{ˌdispen'seiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 分配，分发；【宗】天意，天道；教规；处方 *dis (= apart, away), pens (= weigh)}
  \wordexample{dispensation of food（食物分配） dispensation of providence（天意，上天的安排） Mosaic dispensation（摩西律法）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{dispensable}{di'spensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 非必要的；可分配的 *dis (= apart, away), pens (= weigh)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dispensary}{di'spensəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n.（医院等的）药房 *dis (= apart, away), pens (= weigh)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{ponderable}{'pɒndərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 可称重的；可衡量的；可估量的}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）可估量的事物 *pond<pend (= weigh, consider)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ponderous}{'pɒndərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 沉重的；笨重的；（文章、谈话等）沉闷的 *pond<pend (= weigh)}
  \wordexample{ponderous movements（笨重的动作） ponderous building（庞大的建筑）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preponderance}{pri'pɒndərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n.（重量、力量方面的）优势，优越 *pre (= before), pond<pend (= weigh)}
  \wordexample{preponderance theory（力量优势论）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{preponderant}{pri'pɒndərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a.（在重量、数量、力量等方面）占优势的，压倒的 *pre (= before), pond<pend (= weigh)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{preponderate}{pri'pɒndəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{v.（重量、数量、力量等）占优势 *pre (= before), pond<pend (= weigh)}
  \wordexample{reasons that preponderate over other considerations（优先考虑的理由）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{inexpensive}{ˌinik'spensiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 便宜的，花费不多的 *in (= not), ex (= out), pens (= pay)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pension}{'penʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 退休金；养老金}
  \wordsense{v. 发退休金 *pens (= pay)}
  \wordexample{pensionable（（根据职位、年龄等）有资格领退休金的） pensioner（领退休金者）}
  \wordexample{old-age pension（养老金） draw one's pension（领取养老金） retire on a pension（拿着退休金退休）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{stipend}{'staipend}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n.（学生定期领取的）生活津贴；（牧师等的）薪金，薪俸 *sti (= prick), pend (= pay)}
  \wordexample{stipendiary（领薪金的，有薪水的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pensive}{'pensiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a. 沉思的；忧心忡忡的 *pens (= consider)}
  \wordexample{pensive looks（沉思的表情） look pensive（看起来陷入了沉思）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{pensée*}{pɒn'sei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{n. 思想；（pl.）冥想录，随想录 *pens (= consider)}
  \wordexample{Je pense, donc je suis.（我思故我在。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ponder}{'pɒndə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{v. 深思，沉思 *pond<pend (= consider)}
  \wordexample{ponder on a difficulty（仔细思考困难）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{prepense}{pri'pens}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{a.【用于名词后】预谋的，故意的，蓄意的 *pre (= before), pens (= consider)}
\end{word}

\chapter{SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 spect, spec 意为 to look, to see。其变形为 spic, spi, spy 等。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 vid / vis 及变形 vic, view, voy, vey / vei, vy / vi, ud，来自拉丁语的 the, tu，来自希腊语的 orama, scop，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 see 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{aspect}{'æspekt}
  \wordbreakdown{看起来很近 a<ad (= to) + spect (= look)}
  \wordsense{n. 方面；外观；观点}
  \wordexample{I don't understand all aspects of the subject.（我并不能理解这个主题的所有方面。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{perspective}{pə'spektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{通过某物看 per (= through) + spect (= look)}
  \wordsense{n. 展望；观点，看法；透视画法；透视图}
  \wordphrase{perspectively 依透视画法}
  \wordexample{It's difficult to get a good perspective on problems while they're occurring.（人们很难对正在发生的问题有正确的看法。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{specious}{'spi:ʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{外表上看见的 spec (= look)}
  \wordsense{a. 外表美观的，华而不实的；似是而非的，貌似有理的}
  \wordexample{Al Gore was roundly criticized for his specious claim that he invented the Internet.（阿尔·戈尔貌似有理地宣称自己发明了互联网，遭到了严厉的指责。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{auspicious}{ɔ:'spiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{看鸟的 au<avi (= bird) + spic (= look) + ous (= having the quality of)}
  \wordsense{a. 吉利的，吉祥的；顺利的}
  \wordphrase{auspices 资助，赞助}
  \wordexample{I plan to propose when an auspicious moment presents itself.（我打算在一个幸运的时刻来临之时求婚。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conspicuous}{kən'spikjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{看起来非常好的 con<com (= intensive) + spic (= look)}
  \wordsense{a. 显眼的；引人注目的；显著的}
  \wordphrase{conspicuously 显著地}
  \wordexample{A conspicuous mole disfigures the beauty of her face.（一颗很显眼的痣破坏了她脸部的美。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{despicable}{di'spikəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{向下、向低处看的 de (= down) + spic (= look)}
  \wordsense{a. 卑鄙的，卑劣的}
  \wordphrase{despicably 卑劣地}
  \wordexample{Apologize to your mother for your despicable language.（你要为你卑劣的言辞向你妈妈道歉。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{perspicacious}{ˌpə:spi'keiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{通过某事来透视的 per (= through) + spic (= look)}
  \wordsense{a. 有洞察力的；聪明的，敏锐的}
  \wordphrase{perspicacity 敏锐，聪明}
  \wordexample{The chairman told me I was the most perspicacious student in his seminar.（主席告诉我，在参加研讨会的学生中，我最聪明。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{respite}{'respait}
  \wordbreakdown{在后边看或者拉开距离看 re (= back, away) + spi (= look)}
  \wordsense{n. 休息；延期，延缓；缓期执行}
  \wordexample{The shady pines offered respite from the burning sun.（松树的绿荫为我们提供了一个避开灼热阳光的休息场所。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{circumspect}{'sə:kəmspekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 慎重的，谨慎小心的 *circum (= around)}
  \wordexample{circumspection（慎重，谨慎小心）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{conspectus}{kən'spektəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n.（问题、事件等的）概观；概要，摘要；一览表 *con<com (= together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{expect}{ik'spekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{v. 期待；预计；认为；怀孕 *ex (= out), pect<spect (= look)}
  \wordexample{expected reply（所期望的回答） expected objections（预想到的反对） as was expected（正如所预期的） I am expecting.（我怀孕了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{expectation}{ˌekspek'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 期待，预想；期待、预想的事物；前程；可望继承的遗产 *ex (= out), pect<spect (= look)}
  \wordexample{beyond expectation（预料之外） contrary to expectation(s)（出乎预料） expectation of life（预期寿命） not come up to one's expectations（没有达到某人的预期）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{expectancy}{ik'spektənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 期待；预期 *ex (= out), pect<spect (= look)}
  \wordexample{life expectancy（预期寿命） with a look of expectancy（带着期待的表情）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{expectant}{ik'spektənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 预期的，期待的；待产的；（父或母）未来的 *ex (= out), pect<spect (= look)}
  \wordexample{expectantly（期待地，期望地）}
  \wordexample{expectant father（准爸爸） expectant mother（准妈妈，孕妇）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{inspect}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{v.检查；视察；检阅in（=into） [in'spekt] ?inspecttroops（检阅军队）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{inspectiorr}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n.检查；视察；检阅“in（=into） [in'spekJn] medical inspection（检疫）aerial inspection（空中视察）close inspection（严密的 检查）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{inspector}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n.（工矿等的）督察员；检阅者；检查员（官）；（警察）巡官*in（=into）}
  \wordsense{[intrau'spekt ] introspection（内省）}
  \wordphrase{[in'spekta(r)]}
  \wordexample{inspectorate（视察团）}
  \wordexample{inspectorgeneral（督察长）Inspectorof Taxes（税务稚 查员） introspecte v.内省，自省intro（=within）}
  \wordexample{introspective（内省的，自省的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{prospect}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{*? n.预想：期待的事物；前景；有望成为主顾的人 ['prpspekt] v.勘探（金矿、油矿等），勘察‘pro（=forth）}
  \wordexample{prospector（勘探者，勘察者）? prospectfor gold（勘探金矿）good / bad prospect （好的／不好的前景）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{prospective}{pra'spektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a.预期的；未来的‘pro（=forth）}
  \wordexample{prospectiveadvantage/wealth（预期的好处/财富）prospectivebuyer（可能的买 主）my prospective bride（我未来的新娘） prospective Republican candidate（未来 的共和党候选人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{prospectus}{prə'spektəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n.（新公司等的）创办计划书；（新出版书籍的）内容说明书 *pro (= forth)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{respect}{ri'spekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 尊敬，尊重；重视；关系；方面；（pl.）问候}
  \wordsense{v. 尊敬，尊重；重视；关于，涉及 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{in all / some / any / every / this respect(s)（在所有 / 某些 / 任何 / 每个 / 这个方面） with respect to（关于……） pay respect to（尊敬……）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{respectable}{ri'spektəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 值得尊敬的；体面的；相当的 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{respectability（可敬；体面；受尊敬者）}
  \wordexample{respectably（体面地；高尚地；相当地）}
  \wordexample{respectable citizens（正派的市民） respectable position（相当高的地位）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{respectful}{ri'spektfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 表示敬意的，恭敬的；有礼貌的 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{respectfully（恭敬地，谦恭地）}
  \wordexample{stand at a respectful distance from（恭敬地、保持距离地站着）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{respective}{ri'spektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a.［一般和复数名词一起使用］分别的，各自的，各个的 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{respectively（各个，各自，分别地）}
  \wordexample{have one's respective merits（各有优点） according to one's respective abilities（根据各自的能力）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{retrospect}{'retrəspekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 回顾，回想 *retro (= backward)}
  \wordexample{retrospection（回顾，回想）}
  \wordexample{in retrospect（回想起来，事后看来）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{retrospective}{ˌretrə'spektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 回顾的，回想的；（法律或决定）溯及既往的 *retro (= backward)}
  \wordexample{retrospective exhibition of a painter's work（画家的作品回顾展） retrospective legislation（有追溯效力的立法） retrospective wage increase（有追溯效力的加薪）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{spectacle}{'spektəkl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 壮观的景象；公开展示；（pl.）眼镜}
  \wordexample{spectacled（戴眼镜的）}
  \wordexample{see everything through rose-colored spectacles（乐观地看待每一件事）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{spectacular}{spek'tækjulə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 引人注目的；壮观的；惊人的}
  \wordsense{n. 奇观；精彩的表演；惊人之举}
  \wordexample{President Nixon's spectacular visit to Peking（尼克松总统访问北京的惊人之举） spectacular display of fireworks（壮观的烟火表演） Christmas T.V. spectacular（精彩的圣诞节节目）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{spectator}{spek'teitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n.（公开展示或比赛的）观众；旁观者}
  \wordexample{spectator sports（吸引众多观众的体育运动，如足球）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{specter}{'spektə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 幽灵，鬼怪；（萦绕在心头的）担忧}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{spectral}{'spektrəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 幽灵的，鬼怪的；光谱的}
  \wordexample{spectral apparatus（分光器） spectral colors（谱色）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{spectroscope}{'spektrəskəup}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 分光镜 *scop (= see)}
  \wordexample{spectroscopic（分光镜的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{spectrum}{'spektrəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 光谱；《喻》（宽广的）范围；系列}
  \wordexample{solar spectrum（太阳光谱） the whole spectrum of recent philosophical inquiry（近来哲学探索的总体范畴） a broad spectrum of（广泛的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suspect}{sə'spekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{v. 怀疑；疑有，察觉}
  \wordsense{n. 嫌疑人，嫌疑犯；可疑分子}
  \wordsense{a. 可疑的；不可信的 *sus<sub (= under), pect<spect (= look)}
  \wordexample{political suspect（政治嫌疑犯）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{especial}{i'speʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 特别的，特殊的 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{especially（特别，尤其）}
  \wordexample{in especial（特别，格外）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{spec}{spek}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 投机（speculation 的缩略形式）}
  \wordexample{on spec（碰运气）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{special}{'speʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 特别的，特殊的；专用的；额外的}
  \wordsense{n. 临时巡警；专列；特刊，号外；特色菜}
  \wordexample{specially（特别地）}
  \wordexample{special effects（特殊效果） Special Forces（《美》【军】特种部队）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{specialist}{'speʃəlist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 专家；专科医生}
  \wordexample{eye specialist（眼科医生） specialist in plastic surgery（外科整形专家）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{specialty}{'speʃəlti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 专业，专长；特制品；名产}
  \wordexample{make a specialty of（以……为专业，专攻某学科） Embroidery is her specialty.（她的专长是刺绣。） Wood-carvings are a specialty of this village.（木刻是这个村庄的名产。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{specialize}{'speʃəlaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{v. 专门从事，专攻；专业化；限定（意义、范围等） *ize (= make)}
  \wordexample{specialization（专业化；限定）}
  \wordexample{specialize in political science（专攻政治学） specialized knowledge（专业知识） specialized magazine（专业杂志）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{species}{'spi:ʃi:z}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n.【生】种}
  \wordexample{speciation（物种形成）}
  \wordexample{a species of（一种） The Origin of Species（《物种起源》）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{specific}{spi'sifik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a.（叙述等）明确的，具体的；特有的；【医】有特效的；【生】种的}
  \wordsense{n. 特效药 *fic (= make)}
  \wordexample{specifically（明确地；具体地）}
  \wordexample{specific orders（明确的命令） specific remedy（特效药） specific purpose（明确的目的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{specification}{ˌspesifi'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 详述；（产品的）说明书；（pl.）明细（单）；规格 *fic (= make)}
  \wordexample{technical specifications of a new car（新型汽车的技术规格）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{specify}{'spesifai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{v. 明确说明，载明；详述；把……列入计划或说明书 *fy (= make)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{specimen}{'spesimən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 样本，标本；（化验用的）抽样；家伙，怪人}
  \wordexample{zoological specimens（动物标本） urine specimen（化验用尿样） What a queer specimen he is!（他真是个怪人！）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{speculate}{'spekjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{v. 思索；玄想；推测；投机，做投机买卖}
  \wordexample{speculate in oil shares（做石油股票投机买卖）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{speculation}{ˌspekju'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 思索，沉思；推测；投机}
  \wordexample{speculation in rice（做大米投机买卖）}
  \wordexample{on speculation（碰运气）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{speculative}{'spekjulətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 思索的，冥想的；【哲】思辨的；推测的；投机的}
  \wordexample{speculative philosophy（思辨哲学） speculative purchase of grain（投机购买谷物） speculative market（投机市场） speculative stock（投机股票） speculative buying（投机购买）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{speculator}{'spekjuleitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 沉思者；投机商；票贩子}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{perspicuous}{pə'spikjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 明了的 *per (= completely, intensive)}
  \wordexample{perspicuity（明了）}
  \wordexample{perspicuously（明了地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{spice}{spais}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 调味品，香料；《喻》风味，情趣；香味}
  \wordsense{vt. 加香料于；使增添趣味}
  \wordexample{spicy（香料的；香的；下流的；痛快的）}
  \wordexample{mixed spice(s)（混合香料） a spice of humor（幽默情趣）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suspicion}{sə'spiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 嫌疑；怀疑，猜疑；少量 *sus<sub (= under), pic<spic (= look)}
  \wordexample{above suspicion（不容置疑）}
  \wordexample{There was a suspicion of sadness in her voice.（她的声音里带着一丝伤感。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suspicious}{sə'spiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{a. 可疑的；怀疑的；多疑的 *sus<sub (= under), pic<spic (= look)}
  \wordexample{inspect all suspicious cars（检查所有可疑的车辆） suspicious nature（多疑的性格） suspicious look（怀疑的眼神）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{despise}{di'spaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{v. 蔑视，鄙视 *de (= down)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{despite}{di'spait}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 侮辱；损害；恶意}
  \wordsense{prep. 尽管 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{despiteful（有恶意的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{espionage}{'espiənɑ:ʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 刺探；间谍活动 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{counterespionage（反间谍活动）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{espy}{i'spai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{v. 发现；窥见 *e<ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{frontispiece}{'frʌntispi:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n.（书的）卷首插画 *front (= forehead)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{spy}{spai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordsense{n. 间谍；密探}
  \wordsense{v. 暗中监视；发现}
  \wordexample{police spies（警方的密探） industrial spies（工业间谍） spy on the enemy's movements（侦察敌人的动态） spy satellite（间谍卫星） spy ship（间谍船）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sal 意为 to leap，其变形有 sul, sil, sail, saul 等。此外，sal 还有 salt 和 health 的意思。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{assailant}{ə'seilənt}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人跳的人 as<ad (= to) + sail<sal (= leap)}
  \wordsense{n. 攻击者}
  \wordphrase{assail 攻击}
  \wordexample{Three assailants robbed me of my purse.（三个袭击者抢走了我的钱包。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{salient}{'seiliənt}
  \wordbreakdown{跳上去的 sal (= leap, salt)}
  \wordsense{a. 显著的，明显的，突出的；凸起的}
  \wordsense{n. 凸角；【军】战线或筑城的突出部}
  \wordexample{In your composition, each paragraph should develop a single salient point.（写文章时，每一段落都应该有一个突出的论点。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{exult}{ig'zʌlt}
  \wordbreakdown{用力跳跃 ex (= intensive) + ul<sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordsense{vi. at / in + sth 狂喜，欢欣鼓舞}
  \wordphrase{exultation 狂喜}
  \wordphrase{exultant 狂喜的}
  \wordphrase{exultantly 狂喜地}
  \wordexample{She exults in her good fortune to have triplets.（她非常高兴自己有幸生了三胞胎。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{resilient}{ri'ziliənt}
  \wordbreakdown{回跳、跳上的 re (= back) + sil<sal (= leap)}
  \wordsense{a. 有弹力的；有复原能力的；富有活力的}
  \wordphrase{resilience / resiliency 弹性，弹力；恢复力}
  \wordexample{Most children are quite resilient and quickly recover from bumps and scrapes.（大部分孩子的恢复力都很强，磕碰、擦伤的地方很快就会好。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{salutary}{'sæljətri}
  \wordbreakdown{健康的 sal (= health)}
  \wordsense{a.（对身心）有益的}
  \wordexample{The doctor gave you salutary advice. Now you need to follow it.（医生给了你有益健康的忠告，你要遵守。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{assault}{ə'sɒlt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 突击；强攻}
  \wordsense{v. 强攻；偷袭（阵地等） *as<ad (= to), saul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordexample{assault and battery（暴力殴打）}
  \wordexample{assault craft（突击舟）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{consult}{kən'sʌlt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{v. 参考；与……商议 *con<com (= together), sul<sal (= leap)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{consultant}{kən'sʌltənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 咨询者；（公司等的）顾问；会诊医生 *con<com (= together), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordexample{consultant surgeon（外科会诊医生） firm of consultants（咨询公司）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{consultation}{ˌkɒnsl'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 商议，磋商；咨询；磋商会议 *con<com (= together), sul<sal (= leap)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{consultative}{kən'sʌltətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{a. 咨询的；商议的，磋商的；顾问的 *con<com (= together), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordexample{consultative committee（顾问委员会）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{desultory}{'desəltri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{a. 散漫的；无条理的；不连贯的 *de (= off), sul<sal (= leap)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{exile}{'eksail}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 放逐；背井离乡，流亡；被放逐者；离乡者}
  \wordsense{v. 放逐；使背井离乡 *ex (= out), il<sil<sal (= leap)}
  \wordexample{place of exile（流放地） after an exile of ten years（流亡十年以后） exiled for life（被终生流放的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{insult}{'insʌlt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 侮辱，无礼}
  \wordsense{v. 侮辱，羞辱 *in (= on, against), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordexample{insulting（侮辱的，无礼的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{result}{ri'zʌlt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 结果}
  \wordsense{v. 发生；导致 *re (= back), sul<sal (= leap)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{resultant}{ri'zʌltənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{a. 作为结果的；合成的}
  \wordsense{n. 结果 *re (= back), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordexample{resultant force（合力） resultant velocity（合速度）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{salacious}{sə'leiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{a.（言语、书、画等）猥亵的，淫秽的，下流的 *sal (= leap)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sally}{'sæli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n.（被包围军队的）突围，出击；俏皮话}
  \wordsense{v.（情感）迸发 *sal (= leap)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{salmon}{'sæmən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 鲑鱼；鲑鱼肉；橙红色 *sal (= leap)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sultry}{'sʌltri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{a.（天气）闷热的，酷热的；（人）易动感情的，热烈的 *sul<sal (= leap)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{salad}{'sæləd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 色拉，凉拌菜 *sal (= salt)}
  \wordexample{chicken salad（鸡肉沙拉） fruit salad（水果色拉） salad days（少不更事的时期） salad-dressing（色拉调料（由油、醋、香料等混合在一起制成）） salad-oil（色拉油）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{salary}{'sæləri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 薪金，薪水 *sal (= salt)}
  \wordexample{salary of \$30,000 per annum（年薪 3 万美元）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{salaried}{'sælərid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordsense{a. 有薪水的 *sal (= salt)}
  \wordexample{salaried classes（工薪阶层） salaried posts（带薪职位）}
\end{word}

\chapter{VENG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 veng 意为 to punish。同义词根为 pun / pen。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{avenge}{ə'vendʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{对某人惩戒 a<ad (= to) + veng (= punish)}
  \wordsense{vt. 复仇，报仇}
  \wordphrase{avenger 复仇者，报复者}
  \wordphrase{avenging 报复的}
  \wordexample{I avenged my low grade by toilet papering my teacher's house.（我用手纸把我们老师家围了起来，作为对他给我低分的报复。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{revenge}{ri'vendʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VENG}
  \wordsense{n./v. 报仇，复仇 *re (= back)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{revengeful}{ri'vendʒfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VENG}
  \wordsense{a. 复仇的；报复的 *re (= back)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{vengeance}{'vendʒəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VENG}
  \wordsense{n. 报仇，报复}
  \wordexample{take vengeance on an enemy（向敌人报仇） with a vengeance（极度地；猛烈地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{vengeful}{'vendʒfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VENG}
  \wordsense{a. 复仇的，报复的}
\end{word}

\chapter{CEL, CELER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cel, celer 意为 swift。其反义词根为 slo, tard，意为 slow。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{accelerate}{ək'seləreit}
  \wordbreakdown{使某物更快 ac<ad (= to, add) + celer (= swift)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使加速；促进}
  \wordsense{vi. 加快}
  \wordphrase{acceleration 加速；促进}
  \wordphrase{accelerator 加速者，加速器}
  \wordexample{Global warming continues to accelerate more each year.（全球变暖每年持续加速。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{celerity}{si'lerəti}
  \wordbreakdown{快的状态 celer (= swift)}
  \wordsense{n. 迅速，敏捷}
  \wordexample{Our teenage son shows amazing celerity when we call him to dinner.（我们喊我那十几岁的儿子吃晚饭时，他的反应快得惊人。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{accelerando}{ækˌselə'rɑ:ndou}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordsense{a./ad. 渐快的（地） *ac<ad (= to)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{decelerate}{di:'seləreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordsense{v. 减速 *de (= down)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{excel}{ik'sel}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordsense{v. 胜过，优于 *ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{excellence}{'eksələns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordsense{n. 卓越，优秀；优点，长处 *ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{prize for excellence in furniture design（家具设计优秀奖） his excellence in all forms of sports（他在所有运动项目上的出色）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{Excellency}{'eksələnsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordsense{n. 阁下（对大使、总督等的尊称） *ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{Your Excellency（直接称呼：阁下） His / Her Excellency（间接提及时的称呼：阁下）}
\end{word}

\chapter{EQU[IQU]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 equ 意为 equal, same。其变形为 iqu。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 sembl, simil / simul, par, ident 等和来自希腊语的 tauto。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{adequate}{'ædikwət}
  \wordbreakdown{在相同的、同等的事物上添加的 ad (= to, add) + equ (= equal)}
  \wordsense{a. 适当的；足够的}
  \wordphrase{adequacy 适当；足够}
  \wordphrase{adequately 适当地；足够地}
  \wordexample{My mother always reminds me of taking an adequate supply of socks to camp.（我妈妈常常提醒我：去野营时一定要带够袜子。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{equitable}{'ekwitəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{使能够同等的 equ (= equal, same)}
  \wordsense{a. 公正的，公平的；【律】衡平法的}
  \wordphrase{equitably 公正地，公平地}
  \wordexample{equity 公正，公平；衡平法；（pl.）股息不定的普通股}
  \wordexample{The divorcing couple negotiated an equitable property settlement.（那对离婚的夫妇就公平的财产分配达成了协议。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{equivocate}{i'kwivəkeit}
  \wordbreakdown{发出同样的声音 equ (= equal, same) + voc (= call, voice)}
  \wordsense{vi. 支吾，含糊其辞}
  \wordphrase{equivocal 歧义的；暧昧的，模糊的}
  \wordphrase{equivocation 含糊其辞}
  \wordphrase{equivocally 含糊地}
  \wordexample{Stop equivocating and get to the point!（别兜圈子了，快说重点！）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{inadequate}{in'ædikwət}
  \wordbreakdown{不是往同样的东西上添加的 in (= not) + ad (= to, add) + equ (= equal)}
  \wordsense{a. 不充分的；不适当的}
  \wordphrase{inadequacy 不充分；不适当}
  \wordphrase{inadequately 不适当地；不充分地}
  \wordexample{His academic performance has been inadequate for several semesters running.（他的学术研究成绩连续几个学期都不合格。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{iniquity}{i'nikwəti}
  \wordbreakdown{不相同或不正确 in (= not) + iqu<equ (= equal, same)}
  \wordsense{n. 不公正；邪恶；（pl.）不公正的行为；邪恶的行为}
  \wordphrase{iniquitous 不公正的；邪恶的}
  \wordexample{God banished Satan to hell for his iniquity.（上帝因撒旦的邪恶行径而将他放逐至地狱。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{equable}{'ekwəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 变化小的，稳定的；平和的}
  \wordexample{equable climate / disposition / temper（变化很小的气候 / 平和的性格 / 温和的脾气）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{equal}{'i:kwəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 相同的，同等的；平等的；胜任的}
  \wordsense{n. 同等的人；可匹敌的事物}
  \wordsense{v. 跟……相同；比得上}
  \wordphrase{equally 同样地，同等地；平等地}
  \wordexample{equal opportunity（均等的机会） equal opportunity employer（提供公平机会的雇主）}
  \wordexample{She has no equal in cooking.（在烹饪方面没有谁能和她匹敌。）}
  \wordexample{Equal Rights Amendment（（美国宪法的）平等权利修正案）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{equalitarian}{ˌi:kwɔli'teəriən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 平等主义的}
  \wordsense{n. 平等主义者}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{equality}{i:'kwɒləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n. 平等；同等；均等}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{equalize}{'i:kwəlaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{v. 使相等；使平等 *ize (= make)}
  \wordexample{equalization（平等化；均等化）}
  \wordexample{equalize tax burdens（均摊捐税负担） equalize income（使收入均等）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{equate}{i:'kweit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{v. 等同；使相同 *ate (= make)}
  \wordexample{equate the possession of wealth to happiness（认为拥有财富就等于幸福） equate two quantities（使两个数量相同）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{equation}{i'kweiʒn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n.【数】方程式；等式；相等}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{equator}{i'kweitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n.（the ～）赤道}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{equatorial}{ˌekwə'tɔ:riəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 赤道的；赤道附近的}
  \wordexample{equatorial Africa（位于赤道附近的非洲） equatorial orbit（赤道轨道） Equatorial Guinea（赤道几内亚）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{equidistant}{ˌi:kwi'distənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 等距的 *di<dis (= away), st (= stand)}
  \wordexample{equidistant diplomacy（等距离外交）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{equilateral}{ˌi:kwi'lætərəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 等边的 *later (= side)}
  \wordexample{equilateral triangle（等边三角形）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{equilibrium}{ˌi:kwi'libriəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n. 平衡状态；均衡；（心情的）平静 *libr (= balance)}
  \wordexample{in a state of equilibrium（处于平衡状态的） scales in equilibrium（平衡的天平） maintain / lose equilibrium（保持 / 失去平衡）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{equinox}{'i:kwinɒks}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n. 昼夜平分时，春分或秋分 *nox (= night)}
  \wordexample{autumnal / spring equinox（秋 / 春分）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{equinoctial}{ˌi:kwi'nɒkʃəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 昼夜平分的，春分或秋分的 *noct (= night)}
  \wordexample{equinoctial circle / line（天（球）赤道） equinoctial point（二分点） equinoctial gales / tides（二分点风暴 / 分点潮）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{equipoise}{'ekwipɔiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n. 平衡，均衡；平衡物；秤锤 *pois<pos (= put)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{equivalent}{i'kwivələnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 同等的}
  \wordsense{n. 同等物；相当物；意义相同的词（或符号、表达法等） *val (= worth)}
  \wordexample{equivalent to an insult（等于辱骂的）}
  \wordexample{the Korean equivalent of “thank you”（和 “thank you” 相对应的韩语） noun equivalent（相当于名词的形式）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{equivalence/-ency}{i'kwivələns / -ənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n. 等同；等价 *val (= worth)}
  \wordexample{High School Equivalency Exam（中学同等学力考试）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{inequality}{ˌini'kwɒləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n. 不平等；不同；不均衡；不等式 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{the inequality between the rich and the poor（贫富间的不平等）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{inequitable}{in'ekwitəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 不公平的，不公正的 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{inequitable division of the profits（不公平的收益分配）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{inequity}{in'ekwəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n. 不公平，不公正 *in (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{unequal}{ʌn'i:kwəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 不同的；不公平的 *un (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{unequalled}{ʌn'i:kwəld}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 无与伦比的 *un (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{unequivocal}{ˌʌni'kwivəkl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 明确的，不含糊的 *un (= not), voc (= voice, call)}
\end{word}

\chapter{FLICT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 flict 意为 to strike。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 bat, cuss, fend / fens[fenc, fest], lid / lis, plaud / plaus[plod, plos, plex] 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 sla 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affliction}{ə'flikʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人一击 af<ad (= to) + flict (= strike)}
  \wordsense{n. 痛苦；苦事；痛苦的事由}
  \wordphrase{afflict 使痛苦}
  \wordexample{She has some affliction that causes her to walk with a limp.（她感到有些疼痛，走路一瘸一拐的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{inflict}{in'flikt}
  \wordbreakdown{瞄准打 in (= on, against) + flict (= strike)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + on + sb 给予打击；使痛苦；加以处罚等}
  \wordexample{infliction 施加的事物（如痛苦、处罚等）}
  \wordexample{The tornado inflicted severe damages on the small town.（龙卷风给这个小城镇造成了严重的破坏。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conflict}{'kɒnflikt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLICT}
  \wordsense{n. 斗争，战斗；冲突}
  \wordsense{v. [kən'flikt] 冲突；矛盾 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{a conflict of interest（利益冲突） wordy conflict（舌战） the conflict between duty and desire（义务和欲望的冲突）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conflicting}{kən'fliktiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLICT}
  \wordsense{a. 抵触的；冲突的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{conflicting views（相互矛盾的观点）}
\end{word}

\chapter{LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 lig 意为 to bind, to tie。变形有 li, leag, ly 等。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cinct, nect / nex, strain / strict / string / stress 等和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 bond。此外，意为 to join 的 junct / join / jug 也与此词根含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{alliance}{ə'laiəns}
  \wordbreakdown{捆得很近 al<ad (= to, near) + li<lig (= bind)}
  \wordsense{n. 同盟；联姻}
  \wordexample{ally 结盟；联姻；联合；（n.）同盟国，联盟国；姻亲 allied 结盟的}
  \wordexample{In alliance with my mother, we convinced my father to buy us kids a car.（我们和妈妈联合起来说服爸爸给我们小孩子买了一辆汽车。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{obligate}{'ɒbligeit}
  \wordbreakdown{用力地捆 ob (= intensive) + lig (= bind)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + to do 使负有责任、义务}
  \wordphrase{obligation 义务，责任}
  \wordphrase{obligatory 有义务的；必须的}
  \wordexample{A typical rental lease obligates the landlord to make any needed repairs.（典型的租赁合同上都规定房东有义务负责所有必要的维修。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{liaison}{li'eizɒn}
  \wordbreakdown{起到捆的作用 li<lig (= bind)}
  \wordsense{n. 联络；私通}
  \wordphrase{liaise 取得联系；充当联络人员}
  \wordexample{I work as a liaison between top management and the union leaders.（我负责最高管理层和工会领导人之间的联络工作。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{liability}{ˌlaiə'biləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 责任，义务；倾向；（pl.）债务；负债；不利（的事情） *li<lig (= bind)}
  \wordexample{liability insurance（责任保险） liability for military service（兵役义务） liability to disease（易于患病的倾向）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{liable}{'laiəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{a. 有责任的，有义务的；可能遭受……的；易患……的 *li<lig (= bind)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{league}{li:g}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 联盟，同盟；盟约；里格（长度单位，约为 3 英里、5 公里或 3 海里）}
  \wordsense{v.（使）结盟，联盟，（使）订立盟约 *leag<lig (= tie)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{ligament}{'ligəmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 系带，纽带；韧带}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{ligature}{'ligətʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 带子，绳索；绷带；结扎线}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{oblige}{ə'blaidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{v. 迫使；施恩于；答应……的请求 *ob (= to)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{obliging}{ə'blaidʒiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{a. 乐于助人的 *ob (= to)}
  \wordexample{obliging neighbors（乐于助人的邻居）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{rally}{'ræli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 大会，集会；（重新）集合}
  \wordsense{v. 重新集合、聚集；召集 *r<re (= again), al<ad (= near), ly<lig (= tie)}
  \wordexample{political rally（政治集会） peace rally（和平集会）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{reliable}{ri'laiəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{a. 可靠的；可依赖的 *re (= again), li<lig (= tie)}
  \wordexample{reliability（可靠性，可信赖性）}
  \wordexample{reliable information（可靠的信息）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{reliance}{ri'laiəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 信任，信赖；可依赖的人（或物） *re (= again), li<lig (= tie)}
  \wordexample{reliant（信任的；依赖的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{rely}{ri'lai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{v. 依靠；信赖 *re (= again), li<lig (= tie)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{religion}{ri'lidʒən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 宗教；信仰；信条 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{great religions of the world（世界主要宗教（Christianity, Judaism, Islam, Confucianism, Taoism, Buddhism, Hinduism, etc.）） freedom of religion（宗教信仰自由）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{religious}{ri'lidʒəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{a. 宗教的，宗教上的；虔诚的；修道的；认真的}
  \wordsense{n. 修道士；修女；和尚；尼姑 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{religious rites（宗教仪式） religious life（修行生活） religious man（虔诚的人）}
\end{word}

\chapter{VAL[VAIL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 val 意为 worth, strong。变形为 vail。此外，来自拉丁语的 rob, fort / forc, firm, bil 等也有 strong 的意思。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{available}{ə'veiləbl}
  \wordbreakdown{对某人有价值的 a<ad (= to) + vail<val (= worth)}
  \wordsense{a. 可利用的，可使用的；有用的；可入住的；有空的；可见面的；可获得的}
  \wordphrase{avail 有助于}
  \wordexample{availability 有用（性）}
  \wordexample{Many kinds of organically grown vegetables are available at this store.（在这家商店可以买到很多种有机蔬菜。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{ambivalent}{æm'bivələnt}
  \wordbreakdown{价值站在双方的 ambi (= both) + val (= worth)}
  \wordsense{a.（两种态度、意义等）对立的，矛盾的}
  \wordphrase{ambivalence 矛盾情绪；摇摆}
  \wordexample{I'm ambivalent about his films. They're brilliant, but they're too violent!（我对他的电影持矛盾的态度，电影很精彩，但过于暴力！）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{invalidate}{in'vælideit}
  \wordbreakdown{使没有价值 in (= not) + val (= strong, worth)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使无效}
  \wordphrase{invalidation 使无效 invalidity 无效 invalidism 病弱，虚弱}
  \wordexample{invalid 无效的；（n./a.）['invəlid] 病人；有病的}
  \wordexample{Her research findings were invalidated by her strong personal bias.（她的研究成果因她顽固的个人偏见而变得毫无用处。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{prevail}{pri'veil}
  \wordbreakdown{力量领先 pre (= before) + vail<val (= strength, worth)}
  \wordsense{vi. 占优势，战胜；普及，盛行；奏效}
  \wordphrase{prevailing 占优势的；普及的，流行的；有效的 prevalent 普及的，流行的}
  \wordphrase{prevalence 普及，流行}
  \wordexample{Although the team was down in the 3rd quarter, in the end they prevailed.（虽然那个队在第三局处于劣势，不过最终他们还是赢了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{convalesce}{ˌkɒnvə'les}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{v.（生病后渐渐地）恢复健康 *con<com (= with), esc (= becoming)}
  \wordexample{convalesce after a long illness（久病之后逐渐康复）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{convalescent}{ˌkɒnvə'lesnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{a.（病）渐愈的；恢复中的}
  \wordsense{n. 康复期病人}
  \wordexample{convalescence（渐愈；恢复期）}
  \wordexample{convalescent hospital（（为康复期病人提供的）康复院，疗养院）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{countervail}{ˌkauntə'veil}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{v. 与……势均力敌；抵消；补偿 *counter (= against), vail<val (= strong, strength)}
  \wordexample{countervailing duties（抵消关税）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{devaluate}{di:'væljueit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{v. 降低……的价值，（使）贬值 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{devalue（（使）贬值）}
  \wordexample{devaluate the dollar（使美元贬值）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{devaluation}{diˌvælju'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{n. 贬值 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{devaluation policy（贬值政策）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{equivalent}{i'kwivələnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{a. 同等的}
  \wordsense{n. 同等物；相当物；意义相同的词（或符号、表达法等） *equ (= equal)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{evaluate}{i'væljueit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{v. 评价；鉴定 *e<ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{evaluation}{iˌvælju'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{n. 评价 *e<ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{invalid}{'invəlid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{a. 无效的}
  \wordsense{n. 患者，病人}
  \wordsense{a. 病弱的；（因病或伤导致的）残疾的；供病人用的}
  \wordsense{v.（因病或伤而）使退役 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{invalidity（无效） invalidism（长期的病弱状态）}
  \wordexample{invalid excuses（无效的辩解） invalid will（无效遗嘱） be invalided out of the army（因伤病而退伍）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{invaluable}{in'væljuəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{a. 无法估价的，非常贵重的 *in (= intensive, not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{revalue}{ri:'vælju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{v. 对……再评价；对……再估价 *re (= again)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{revaluation}{ˌri:vælju'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{n. 再评价，再估价；币值的重新调整 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{revaluation of the German mark（重新调整德国马克的币值）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{unavailing}{ˌʌnə'veiliŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{a. 无效的；无益的；无用的 *un (= not), a<ad (= to), vail<val (= worth)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{undervalue}{ˌʌndə'value:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{v. 低估}
  \wordexample{overvalue（高估）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{valence**/valency**}{'veiləns / -si}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{n. 原子价，化合价}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{valetudinarian}{ˌvælitju:di'neəriən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{a. 体弱多病的；过分担心健康的}
  \wordsense{n. 体弱多病的人；过分担心健康的人}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{valiant}{'væliənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
