\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sensation}{senˈseiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{n. 引起轰动的事件，轰动效应；感觉（力）}
  \wordexample{create a great sensation（引起很大的轰动）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sensational}{senˈseiʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{a. 引起轰动的；《贬》耸人听闻的；企图激起感情的}
  \wordexample{sensationalism（追求轰动效应）}
  \wordexample{sensational murder（耸人听闻的谋杀事件）}
  \wordexample{sensational writer（采用耸人听闻手法的作家）}
  \wordexample{sensational newspaper（追求轰动效应的报纸）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{sentient}{ˈsentiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{a. 有感觉力的，有知觉力的；感觉敏锐的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sentiment}{ˈsentɪmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{n. 感情，情绪，心情；伤感情绪；（pl.）意见，见解}
  \wordexample{sentiment checked by reason（受理性压制的情感）}
  \wordexample{sentiment of pity（怜悯之情）}
  \wordexample{public sentiment（公众情绪）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SAT[SET]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 sat 意为 enough，元音弱化后变成 set。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{asset}{ˈæset}
  \wordbreakdown{在近处充分拥有 as<ad (= to, near) + set<sat (= enough)}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）资产，财产；宝贵的人（或物）}
  \wordexample{Harold has been a real asset to his company.（哈罗德一直是他们公司的骨干。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{insatiable}{ɪnˈseiʃəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不充分的 in (= not) + sat (= enough)}
  \wordsense{a. 不知足的；贪得无厌的}
  \wordexample{The addictive personality invariably has insatiable appetites.（上瘾的人都有贪得无厌的欲望。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{satiate}{ˈseiʃieit}
  \wordbreakdown{使充分 sat (= enough)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使饱足；（因为太多而）使厌腻}
  \wordphrase{satiable 可满足的}
  \wordexample{Just a salad and crackers tend to satiate me. I'm a light eater.（我要一份沙拉和饼干就够了，我的胃口不大。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{insatiate}{ɪnˈseiʃiət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordsense{a. 不知足的 *in (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{satisfaction}{ˌsætɪsˈfækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordsense{n. 满足；乐事；赔偿 *fact (= make)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{satisfactory}{ˌsætɪsˈfæktəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordsense{a. 令人满意的 *fact (= make)}
  \wordexample{satisfactorily（令人满意地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{satisfy}{ˈsætɪsfaɪ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordsense{v. 使满意，使满足；使确信}
  \wordexample{satisfying（令人满意的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{saturate}{ˈsætʃəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordsense{v. 浸透；（被湿气或潮湿的东西）浸湿；使饱享；充满；饱和}
  \wordexample{saturated with sunshine（尽情享受阳光的）}
  \wordexample{saturated with learning（专心于学问的）}
  \wordexample{saturated solution of salt（饱和盐溶液）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{saturation}{ˌsætʃəˈreiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordsense{n. 浸透；饱和（状态）}
  \wordexample{saturation bombing（【军】饱和轰炸）}
  \wordexample{saturation point（饱和点）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SED, SESS[SID]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 sed，sess 意为 to sit，元音弱化后变成 sid。反义词根有来自拉丁语的 st，stat，stit，sist 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{assiduous}{əˈsidjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{一直坐着 as<ad (= to, intensive) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordsense{a. 勤奋的；周到的，竭诚的；一丝不苟的}
  \wordphrase{assiduity 勤奋，刻苦}
  \wordphrase{assiduously 勤奋地，刻苦地}
  \wordexample{Her assiduous attention to detail makes her a good editor.（她对细节一丝不苟的关注使她成为一名优秀的编辑。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{dissident}{ˈdisidənt}
  \wordbreakdown{分开坐 dis (= apart) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordsense{n. 持不同政见者；不赞成者}
  \wordsense{a. 持不同政见的；不赞成的}
  \wordphrase{dissidence 异议}
  \wordexample{He isn't shy about expressing his habitually dissident views.（他从不怯于发表自己常持有的不同见解。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{insidious}{inˈsidjəs}
  \wordbreakdown{坐在里面的 in (= in) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordsense{a. 阴险的；狡猾的；（疾病等）不知不觉间加剧的；潜伏的}
  \wordexample{Ugly rumors are insidious and tend to acquire a life of their own.（可恶的谣言不知不觉间愈演愈烈，而且有愈演愈真的倾向。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{residue}{ˈrezidju:}
  \wordbreakdown{重新、继续坐着 re (= again) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordsense{n. 剩余，残余；残余物；【律】剩余财产}
  \wordphrase{residual 剩余的，残余的}
  \wordphrase{residuary 残余的；剩余财产的}
  \wordexample{The insecticide leaves a residue that keeps on killing 2 weeks.（杀虫剂的残余物在两周内将继续起到杀虫作用。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{subsidiary}{səbˈsidiəri}
  \wordbreakdown{坐在下面 sub (= under) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordsense{a. 辅助的；附属的；津贴的}
  \wordsense{n. 子公司}
  \wordphrase{subsidize 给……补贴}
  \wordphrase{subsidy 补贴；补助金}
  \wordexample{Multinational companies have a host of well-known subsidiaries.（跨国公司有许多著名的子公司。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{supersede}{ˌsu:pəˈsi:d}
  \wordbreakdown{从上往下坐下 super (= over) + sed (= sit)}
  \wordsense{vt. 代替；取代}
  \wordphrase{supersession 取代}
  \wordexample{Model AE-400X supersedes Model AE-200.（模型 AE-400X 取代 AE-200。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sedentary}{ˈsedntri}
  \wordbreakdown{与坐着的状态相关联的 sed (= sit) + tary (= relating to)}
  \wordsense{a. （工作等需要）坐着的；久坐的}
  \wordexample{People with a sedentary lifestyle tend to suffer from obesity.（有久坐习惯的人容易肥胖。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{assess}{əˈses}
  \wordbreakdown{靠近坐 as<ad (= to, near) + sess (= sit)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb/sth + at + sth 评估（财产）的价值；征（税）；处（罚金）}
  \wordphrase{assessment 评估}
  \wordphrase{assessor 评估员；陪审法官}
  \wordexample{I assess the worth of this pearl necklace at \$8,000.（这条珍珠项链我估价为 8000 美元。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{obsession}{əbˈseʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{妨碍地坐着 ob (= in the way) + sess (= sit)}
  \wordsense{n. 着魔；迷住}
  \wordphrase{obsessive 使人着迷的}
  \wordexample{obsess（妄想、固有观念等）萦绕心头，不停地困扰}
  \wordexample{Eating is my hobby. Shopping is my obsession!（吃东西是我的兴趣，购物让我着迷！）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preside}{priˈzaid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pre (= before)}
  \wordsense{v. 担任会议主席，主持；掌管，统辖}
  \wordexample{presider（主持者；主宰者）}
  \wordexample{presiding officer（主持会议的官员）}
  \wordexample{presiding judge（庭长，首席法官）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{presidential}{ˌpreziˈdenʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pre (= before)}
  \wordsense{a. 总统的；支配的，管辖的}
  \wordexample{president（（P\textasciitilde{}）总统；总裁；董事长）}
  \wordexample{president-elect（（尚未宣誓就职的）当选总统）}
  \wordexample{presidential election（总统选举）}
  \wordexample{presidential year（（美国的）总统选举年）}
  \wordexample{presidential government（总统制政体）}
  \wordexample{presidential primary（（美国各政党的）总统预选）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{presidency}{ˈprezidənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pre (= before)}
  \wordsense{n. 总统职位、职权、任期；统辖}
  \wordexample{during the presidency of Lincoln（林肯总统在任期间）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{presidium}{priˈsidjəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pre (= before)}
  \wordsense{n. （组织、团体的）主席团；常务委员会}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{reside}{riˈzaid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 re (= again)}
  \wordsense{v. 居住；（权力等）属于；存在于}
  \wordexample{The supreme authority resides in the president.（最高权力属于总统。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{residence}{ˈrezidəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 re (= again)}
  \wordsense{n. 住所，住宅；官邸；居住；驻在}
  \wordexample{residency（外交官等的官邸；住院医师的实习期）}
  \wordexample{this desirable family residence for sale（令人满意的待售的住宅）}
  \wordexample{official residence（官邸）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{resident}{ˈrezidənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 re (= again)}
  \wordsense{n. 居民；侨民；驻外官员}
  \wordsense{a. 居住的}
  \wordexample{South Korean residents in the United States（在美韩国人）}
  \wordexample{Resident Assistant（（美国大学宿舍的）常驻助理（缩写：RA））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{residential}{ˌreziˈdenʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 re (= again)}
  \wordsense{a. 住所的，居住的，住宅的}
  \wordexample{residential quarter（住宅地）}
  \wordexample{residential qualifications（（投票时必须具备的）居住资格）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{subside}{səbˈsaid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 sub (= under)}
  \wordsense{v. 下沉；（洪水、浮肿等）消退；（建筑向地下）沉降；（风或担忧等）平息，平静下来}
  \wordexample{subsidence（下沉，下陷）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sedan}{siˈdæn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{n. 箱式小客车（可容纳 4 人以上）；（17\textasciitilde{}18 世纪的）轿子}
  \wordexample{sedan chair（轿子）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{sedate}{siˈdeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{a. 沉着的；安静的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{sedation}{siˈdeiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{n. 镇静作用；镇静状态}
  \wordexample{The patient is under sedation.（病人处于镇静状态。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sedative}{ˈsedətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{n. 镇静剂}
  \wordsense{a. 使镇静的，使安静的}
  \wordexample{take a sedative（服用镇静剂）}
  \wordexample{sedative effect（镇静效果）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sediment}{ˈsedimənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{n. 沉淀；沉渣，沉淀物（如沉在江河底部的沙子、泥土、石子等；河水泛滥后留下的淤泥等）}
  \wordexample{sedimentation（沉淀（作用））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sedimentary}{ˌsediˈmentri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{a. 沉渣的，沉淀物的；由沉淀物生成的}
  \wordexample{sedimentary rocks（沉积岩（如粘板岩、石灰岩等））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sedulous}{ˈsedjuləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{a. 坚忍不拔的；勤勉的，刻苦的；小心周到的}
  \wordexample{pay him sedulous attention（密切地关注他）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{saddle}{ˈsædl}
  \wordbreakdown{sad<sed (= sit)}
  \wordsense{n. （马、自行车等的）鞍、车座；鞍状物}
  \wordsense{v. 给（马等）装鞍；赋予某人以重任}
  \wordexample{saddler（制造马具的人）}
  \wordexample{saddlery（马具；马具库）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{possess}{pəˈzes}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pos (= powerful, able)}
  \wordsense{v. 持有，拥有；控制；（恶魔、邪念等）缠住，迷住（某人）}
  \wordexample{possess a large fortune（拥有大量财产）}
  \wordexample{be possessed with/by devils（着魔的）}
  \wordexample{be possessed with the idea that someone is persecuting him（着魔地认为有人在迫害他）}
  \wordexample{be possessed by envy（内心充满嫉妒）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{possession}{pəˈzeʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pos (= powerful, able)}
  \wordsense{n. 所有；（pl.）所有物；财产}
  \wordexample{illegal possession of arms（非法持有武器）}
  \wordexample{a man of great possessions（富翁）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{possessive}{pəˈzesiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{a. 所有的；所有权的；【语】所有格的；占有欲强的}
  \wordexample{possessive manner（极具占有欲的态度）}
  \wordexample{possessive adjective/case/pronoun（所有格形容词/所有格/所有格代词）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{session}{ˈseʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SED, SESS[SID]}
  \wordsense{n. 开会；（法庭的）开庭；会期；学期；授课时间}
  \wordexample{sessional（开会的；开庭的；会期的）}
  \wordexample{the 30th session of the National Assembly（第 30 届国会）}
  \wordexample{summer session（暑期学习班）}
  \wordexample{at the next session of this class（在这门课的下节课上）}
  \wordexample{in session（会议中；开庭中）}
\end{word}

\chapter{BAS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 bas 意为 lower，bottom。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{abase}{əˈbeis}
  \wordbreakdown{降低到 a<ad (= to) + bas (= lower)}
  \wordsense{vt. 降低……的地位、身份、品格、名誉等}
  \wordphrase{abasement 屈尊}
  \wordexample{Don't abase yourself by begging to get your job back.（不要为了要回工作而卑躬屈膝。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{debase}{diˈbeis}
  \wordbreakdown{使完全走向底部 de (= down, intensive) + bas (= bottom, lower)}
  \wordsense{vt. 降低，贬低（价值、品质、人格等）}
  \wordexample{debasement（品格、品质的）降低}
  \wordexample{Many people feel that terrorists debase the Islamic faith.（许多人认为恐怖分子有损伊斯兰教信仰。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{base}{beis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordsense{n. 基部；基础；基地；底边，底面；（棒球）垒}
  \wordsense{v. 以……为基础；把……建立在某种基础上}
  \wordsense{a. 不值钱的；卑劣的；不纯的}
  \wordexample{air/naval base（空军/海军基地）}
  \wordexample{base of operations（作战基地）}
  \wordexample{base camp（探险队等的营地）}
  \wordexample{get to first base（取得初步成功）}
  \wordexample{base motives（卑劣的动机）}
  \wordexample{base metals（贱金属）}
  \wordexample{base coin（掺有贱金属的硬币）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{baseless}{ˈbeisləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 less (= without)}
  \wordsense{a. 没有理由的，没有根据的}
  \wordexample{baseless fears（莫名其妙的恐惧）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{basement}{ˈbeismənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordsense{n. 地下室}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{basic}{ˈbeisik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordsense{a. 基础的；基本的}
  \wordexample{basically（基本地；根本地）}
  \wordexample{basic principles（基本原则）}
  \wordexample{Basic English（基础英语）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{basis}{ˈbeisis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordsense{n. 基础，根基，基部；基本；主要成分；最重要的部分}
  \wordexample{on a solid basis（在稳固的基础上）}
  \wordexample{on an equal basis（在平等的基础之上）}
  \wordexample{on the basis of（以……为基础）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{basin}{ˈbeisn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordsense{n. 水罐；盆；（喷泉等的）水池；（被陆地包围的）内湾，内港；船坞；盆地；流域}
  \wordexample{a basin of water（一盆水）}
  \wordexample{yacht basin（游艇停泊处）}
  \wordexample{the Thames basin（泰晤士河流域）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{bastard}{ˈba:stəd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 ard (= person who)}
  \wordsense{n. 庶子；私生子；家伙，坏蛋}
  \wordsense{a. 假冒的}
  \wordexample{bastardy（私生）}
  \wordexample{bastardize（证明为私生子，判定为私生子；伪造，捏造）}
  \wordexample{bastard child/son/daughter（私生子/私生女）}
  \wordexample{You heartless bastard!（你这个无情的家伙！）}
  \wordexample{bastard charity（伪善）}
\end{word}

\chapter{CENT, CANT[CHANT, CHANS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 cent，cant 意为 to sing，song，元音弱化后变成 chant，chans。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{accentuate}{əkˈsentʃueit}
  \wordbreakdown{用非常大的声音歌唱 ac<ad (= to, intensive) + cent (= sing)}
  \wordsense{vt. 重读；强调；使突出}
  \wordphrase{accentuation 重读；强调}
  \wordexample{accent 重音；（v.）[əkˈsent] 加重音符号}
  \wordexample{Your earrings accentuate the deep blue of your eyes.（你的耳环更加突出了你深蓝色的眼睛。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{enchant}{inˈtʃa:nt}
  \wordbreakdown{在里边歌唱 en (= in) + chant<cant (= sing)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使陶醉；使悦；对……施魔法}
  \wordphrase{enchantment 着迷；着魔}
  \wordphrase{enchanter 魔法师；使人着迷的人}
  \wordphrase{enchantress 女巫；迷人的女人}
  \wordphrase{enchanting 令人着迷的}
  \wordphrase{enchanted 入迷的，着魔的}
  \wordphrase{enchantingly 入迷地}
  \wordexample{Fairy tales with trolls always enchant my children.（出现侏儒或巨人的童话总是能让我的孩子们很着迷。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{incentive}{inˈsentiv}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面唱歌 in (= in, on) + cent (= sing)}
  \wordsense{n. 刺激；鼓励；动机}
  \wordexample{Salespeople usually receive commissions as an incentive to sell more.（销售人员常常收取回扣，这能刺激他们卖得更多。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{recant}{riˈkænt}
  \wordbreakdown{向后、使消失地唱歌 re (= back, away) + cant (= sing)}
  \wordsense{v. 撤回；放弃}
  \wordphrase{recantation 撤回；放弃}
  \wordexample{After sessions with a deprogrammer, Iris recanted her allegiance to the cult.（和说服她放弃被灌输的思想的人见了几次面之后，艾里斯放弃了对异教的效忠。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{chanson}{ʃɔŋˈsɔŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{chans<cant (= sing)}
  \wordsense{n. 《法》歌曲}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{chant}{tʃa:nt}
  \wordbreakdown{chant<cant (= sing)}
  \wordsense{n. 歌曲；颂歌；吟唱}
  \wordsense{v. 唱（歌曲、颂歌）；吟唱}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{descant}{ˈdeskænt}
  \wordbreakdown{des<dis (= away, apart)}
  \wordsense{n. 高音部（歌唱或演奏中高于主旋律的装饰性曲调或对位音）；详述}
  \wordsense{v. 详谈，详述}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{incantation}{ˌinkænˈteiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 in (= in)}
  \wordsense{n. 咒语；魔法}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{precentor}{priˈsentə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pre (= before)}
  \wordsense{n. （教堂圣歌的）领唱人}
\end{word}

\chapter{CRE[CRU]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cre 具有 to grow，to increase 等含义，元音弱化后变成 cru。此外，意为 to separate，to observe 的 cret/creet/cre/cern 和此词根形态相似，需加以注意。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{accrue}{əˈkru:}
  \wordbreakdown{更加增多 ac<ad (= to, add) + cru<cre (= grow, increase)}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sb（通过自然增长、积累）产生；（利息等）自然积累而成}
  \wordphrase{accretion 增大，添加}
  \wordexample{Interest accrues, but just 4\% annually.（虽然也生息，但每年只有 4\%。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{decrease}{diˈkri:s}
  \wordbreakdown{对“增大、变大”的轻微否定 de (= reverse) + cre (= grow)}
  \wordsense{v. 减少；降低}
  \wordsense{n. [ˈdi:kri:s] 减少；降低}
  \wordphrase{decreasingly 逐渐减少地}
  \wordexample{The senate voted to decrease property tax rates yesterday.（参议院昨天投票决定降低财产税率。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{increment}{ˈinkrəmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面增长 in (= in, on) + cre (= grow)}
  \wordsense{n. 增加；增额}
  \wordexample{The street protest grew by increments until a mob had formed.（街上抗议的队伍不断扩大，最终演变成一场暴动。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{concrete}{ˈkɔnkri:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 con<com (= together)}
  \wordsense{a. 具体的；实在的；有形的}
  \wordsense{n. 混凝土}
  \wordsense{v. 凝结；使凝固；用混凝土浇筑}
  \wordexample{concretion（凝固，凝结；凝固物）}
  \wordexample{concrete proposals/evidence（具体的提案/确凿的证据）}
  \wordexample{concrete jungle（水泥丛林（指竞争激烈、弱肉强食的城市））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{crescent}{ˈkresnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 sc<esc (= becoming)}
  \wordsense{n. 新月}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{decrement}{ˈdekrimənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 de (= down)}
  \wordsense{n. 减少（量）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{increase}{ˈinkri:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 in (= in)}
  \wordsense{n. 增加}
  \wordsense{v. [inˈkri:s] 增加，增大，增长}
  \wordexample{increasingly（渐增地，愈加）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{recruit}{riˈkru:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 re (= again)}
  \wordsense{v. 征募新兵；招收新会员或新职员；补充；恢复（体力等）}
  \wordsense{n. 征募新兵；招收，招生；补充；恢复体力}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{recruitment}{riˈkru:tmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 re (= again)}
  \wordsense{n. 征募新兵；招收，招聘；补充}
  \wordexample{recruitment advertising（招聘广告）}
  \wordexample{recruitment training center（新兵训练所）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FA,FAM,FESS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 fa, fam, fess 意为 to say, to talk。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 dic / dict, locut / loqu, nounc / nunci, parl 等和来自希腊语的 phras, pha / phe / phu。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affable}{ˈæfəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{靠近说话的 af<ad (= to, near) + fa (= say)}
  \wordsense{a. 和蔼的，易于接近的，亲切的}
  \wordphrase{affability 易于接近，亲切}
  \wordexample{My grandfather is a very affable gentleman.（我爷爷是个非常和蔼可亲的人。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{ineffable}{in'efəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不能向外说的 in (= not) + ef<ex (= out) + fa (= say)}
  \wordsense{a. 说不出的；难以形容的}
  \wordexample{Nothing compares to the ineffable joy of listening to a Mozart symphony.（听莫扎特交响乐给人带来的愉悦不可言传，无与伦比。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{prefatory}{'prefətəri}
  \wordbreakdown{先说的 pre (= before) + fa (= say)}
  \wordsense{a. 序言的，前言的；位于前面的}
  \wordphrase{preface 序言，前言}
  \wordexample{Before giving my speech, I'd like to make a few prefatory remarks.（在开始演讲之前，我想先说几句开场白。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{defamation}{ˌdefə'meiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{贬低地说某人 de (= down) + fam (= say)}
  \wordsense{n. 诽谤，中伤}
  \wordphrase{defame 诽谤 defamatory 中伤的}
  \wordexample{Movie stars are often the victims of defamation in the tabloid press.（电影明星往往成为小报新闻中诽谤的对象。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{infamous}{'infəməs}
  \wordbreakdown{无法言说的 in (= not) + fa (= say)}
  \wordsense{a. 声名狼藉的，臭名昭著的，口碑不好的；邪恶的；无耻的}
  \wordphrase{infamy 声名狼藉；丑行，恶行}
  \wordexample{America will probably never forget Japan's infamous surprised attack on Pearl Harbor.（也许美国永远不会忘记臭名昭著的日本突袭珍珠港事件。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{profess}{prə'fes}
  \wordbreakdown{在众人面前吐露 pro (= forth) + fess (= say)}
  \wordsense{vt. 公开宣称，自称，冒充；把……作为职业}
  \wordexample{profession 职业；宣布 professionalism 专家气质；内行气派 professor 教授 professional 职业的；专家的；职业选手；专家；以特定职业谋生的人（如职业歌手、舞蹈家等） professed 公然的；冒充的 professionally 职业地，专业地 professedly 公然地；冒充地}
  \wordexample{The president professes confidence in the nation's economic recovery.（总统公开表示了对国家经济复苏的信心。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{confabulate}{kən'fæbjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{v. 谈论 *con<com (= together, with)}
  \wordexample{confabulation（谈论）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{fable}{'feibl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{n. 寓言；传说；神话}
  \wordexample{Aesop's Fables（《伊索寓言》）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{fabulous}{'fæbjələs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{a. 传说中的；难以置信的；惊人的}
  \wordexample{fabulous heroes（传说中的英雄） fabulous wealth（巨额财富）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{infancy}{'infənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{n. 婴儿期；（发展、成长的）初期 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{infancy of a nation（国家发展初期）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{infant}{'infənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{n. 婴儿 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{infant voices（婴儿的噪音） infant food（婴儿食品） infant industries（新兴 / 初创行业）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{infantile}{'infəntail}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{a. 婴儿的；幼稚的 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{infantile pastimes（婴儿游戏）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{infantry}{'infəntri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{n.【总称，用做单数】步兵 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{two regiments of infantry（两个步兵团）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{nefarious}{ni'feəriəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{a. 恶毒的，邪恶的 *ne (= not)}
  \wordexample{nefarious activities（恶毒的行为）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{fame}{feim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{n. 名誉；名声}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{confess}{kən'fes}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{v. 坦白，供认，承认；表明信仰 *con<com (= together, with)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{confession}{kən'feʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{n. 从实招认，供认；（信仰等的）告白 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{confession of faith（信仰告白）}
\end{word}

\chapter{GRAND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 grand 意为 great, large。同义词根有来自希腊语的 coloss, magn, maha, mega / megalo, metro 和来自拉丁语的 maj, max 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{aggrandize}{ə'grændaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{使更大 ag<ad (= to, add) + grand (= great)}
  \wordsense{vt. 增加，扩大，强化（权力、财产、地位等）；夸张}
  \wordphrase{aggrandizement 扩大；提高；强化；夸张}
  \wordexample{He seeks to aggrandize himself before his superiors to advance his career.（他为了晋升而在上司面前过分地夸大自己。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{grand}{grænd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAND}
  \wordsense{a. 雄伟的，壮丽的；高贵的；愉快的；总的；完全的；崇高的}
  \wordphrase{grandly 宏伟地；崇高地}
  \wordexample{grand jury（大陪审团） Grand Canyon（大峡谷（美国亚利桑那州科罗拉多河的大峡谷）） Grand Old Party（老大党，指美国共和党，缩写：GOP）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{grandeur}{'grændʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAND}
  \wordsense{n. 伟大，庄严；壮丽，壮观}
  \wordexample{the grandeur of Napoleon Bonaparte（拿破仑·波拿巴的伟大） the grandeur of the Swiss Alps（瑞士阿尔卑斯山的壮观） the grandeur of his nature（他高贵的气质）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{grandiloquent}{ˌgræn'diləkwənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAND}
  \wordsense{a. 夸张的，夸大的；大言不断的 *loqu (= say)}
  \wordphrase{grandiloquence 夸张之言；大言不断}
  \wordexample{grandiloquent speaker（夸夸其谈的人） written in a grandiloquent style（以夸张的风格写作）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{grandiose}{'grændiəus}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAND}
  \wordsense{a. 宏伟的；浮夸的}
\end{word}

\chapter{PROB, PROV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 prob, prov 意为 to test, to try, to examine, good, proper。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{approbation}{ˌæprə'beiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{让某人尝试 ap<ad (= to) + prob (= try, test, good)}
  \wordsense{n. 许可；认可；承认；赞赏}
  \wordphrase{approbate 认可；承认；赞赏}
  \wordphrase{Most children behave well after receiving approbation.（大多数孩子都会在受到表扬后变得很乖。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{probity}{ˈprəubiti}
  \wordbreakdown{经历考验的或优秀的性质 prob (= test, good, proper)}
  \wordsense{n. 清廉，高洁；诚实}
  \wordexample{My father is a man of unquestioned probity.（毫无疑问，我父亲是个诚实的人。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{reprove}{ri'pru:v}
  \wordbreakdown{重新进行试验 re (= again) + prov (= test)}
  \wordsense{vt. 责备，指责}
  \wordphrase{reproof 非难，指责，责备}
  \wordexample{The judge reproved Microsoft for its obviously monopolistic business practices.（法官指责微软有明显的行业垄断行为。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{reprobate}{'reprəbeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{v. 责备，非难}
  \wordsense{n. 恶棍，无赖 *re (= back, again, against)}
  \wordexample{reprobation（责备，非难）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{probe}{prəub}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{v. 探测；（用探针）探查；彻底调查（原因等）}
  \wordsense{n. 探针（医生为查明伤处等而使用的工具）；调查}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{probable}{'prɔbəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{a. 可能会发生的}
  \wordsense{n. 很可能会被选中的人，可能获胜的人}
  \wordexample{probably（也许）}
  \wordexample{probable winner（可能获胜的人） probable result（可能出现的结果）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{probability}{ˌprɔbə'biləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 可能性，或然性；可能出现的结果}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{improbable}{im'prɔbəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{a. 不可能的，不可能有（或发生）的 *im<in (= not)}
  \wordexample{improbability（不可能有（或发生），不可能发生的事）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{probate}{'prəubeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 遗嘱的验证；经验证的遗嘱}
  \wordsense{v. 验证（遗嘱）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{probation}{prə'beiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 试用，见习；见习期；试用期；鉴定；缓刑}
  \wordexample{probationary（试用的；见习的；缓刑的）}
  \wordexample{officer on probation（见习期公务员） three year's probation under suspended sentence of one year's imprisonment（一年徒刑，缓刑三年） probation system（缓刑制） probation officer（缓刑监护官）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{probationer}{prə'beiʃnə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 见习生；试用人员；缓刑犯}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{approve}{ə'pru:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{v. 认可，赞成，同意；批准；满意 *ap<ad (= to)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{approval}{ə'pru:vəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 认可，赞成；同意；满意 *ap<ad (= to)}
  \wordexample{goods on approval（供试用的货物（不满意可以退货））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{disprove}{dis'pru:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{v. 证明……不正确；反驳 *dis (= away, apart)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{disproof}{'dispru:f}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 反证；反驳 *dis (= away, apart), proof<prov (= test, try, examine)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{improve}{im'pru:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{v. 改善，改良；增进；利用 *im<in (= into)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{improvement}{im'pru:vmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 改良，改善；增进；改善的事物；利用 *im<in (= into)}
\end{word}

\chapter{SCRIB, SCRIPT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 scrib, script 意为 to write。同义词根有来自希腊语的 graph, gram。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{ascribe}{ə'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{将某事写下来向某人告知 a<ad (= to) + scrib (= write)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（于）}
  \wordphrase{ascription 归因，归属 ascribable 可归因于……的}
  \wordexample{I ascribe no malefic motive to his behavior.（我从他的行为里找不出任何恶意的动机。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{prescribe}{pri'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{事先写 pre (= before) + scrib (= write)}
  \wordsense{vt. 规定，命令；开处方}
  \wordphrase{prescription 处方；命令，指示；规定，法规 prescript 命令；规定，法令}
  \wordphrase{prescriptive 规定的，规范的；【律】依据时效的}
  \wordexample{The doctor prescribed some tranquilizers and a few days of rest.（医生开了一些镇静药，要求休息几天。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{proscribe}{prə'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{事先写 pro (= before) + scrib (= write)}
  \wordsense{vt. 禁止；剥夺公权；排斥；流放}
  \wordphrase{proscription 剥夺公权；流放；禁止}
  \wordexample{The state senate proscribed an expiration date on welfare benefits.（州议院禁止对福利救济金设置截止期限。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{transcribe}{træn'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{写得使人明白，达到期望状态 tran<trans (= across) + scrib (= write)}
  \wordsense{v. 抄写；（播放）录音，（播放）录像；编曲}
  \wordexample{transcript（学校的）成绩单；副本，誉本 transcription 抄写；复本，誉本；播放录音或录像}
  \wordexample{I record my professor's lectures and transcribe them at home.（教授讲课时我录下来，然后回家再听。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{circumscribe}{'sə:kəmskraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{v. 在……周围画线；限定，限制 *circum (= around)}
  \wordexample{circumscription（限定，限制；（硬币等边上的）回读铭文）}
  \wordexample{circumscribe one's interests（限制某人的爱好）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conscript}{kən'skript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{v. 征集，征召}
  \wordsense{n. ['kɔnskript] 应征入伍者}
  \wordsense{a. ['kɔnskript] 被征召的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{conscript solider（应征入伍的士兵）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conscription}{kən'skripʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n. 征兵；征用（制度） *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{conscription age（兵役年龄） conscription system（征兵制度） conscription of wealth（财产的征收；（战时向免服兵役者征收的）兵役税）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{describe}{di'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{v. 描述，描写；把……说成 *de (= down, intensive)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{description}{di'skripʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n. 描述；描写；种类 *de (= down, intensive)}
  \wordexample{description of the thief（对小偷的描述） vessels of every description（各种船舶）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{descriptive}{di'skriptiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{a. 叙述的；描写的 *de (= down, intensive)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{inscribe}{in'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{v. 题写；雕刻；题赠 *in (= in, into)}
  \wordexample{inscription（（纪念碑、货币等的）铭文）}
  \wordexample{inscribed stock（记名股票） inscribe a tomb with a name（在墓碑上刻名字）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{manuscript}{'mænjuskript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n.（书等的）原稿 *manu (= hand)}
  \wordexample{poems still in manuscript（还没有付印的诗） send a manuscript to the printers（把原稿发送给印刷工）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{nondescript}{'nɔndiskript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{a./n. 难以归类的（人或物），无明显特征的（人或物）；无趣的 *non (= not), de (= down)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{postscript}{'pəustskript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n.（信件的）附言（缩写：P.S.） *post (= after)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{rescript}{ri'skript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n. 公告；（政府的）布告，敕令，法令；（罗马教皇的）解答敕令 *re (= again, back)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{subscribe}{səb'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{v. 捐款；（在文件下面）署名；同意，赞成；订阅 *sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{subscriber（捐款者；订阅者）}
  \wordexample{subscribe one's name to a petition（在请愿书上签名）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{subscription}{səb'skripʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n. 捐款，认捐；订阅；签名；捐助金；订购款；会费 *sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{public subscription（公众捐款） subscription concert（联票音乐会（指预售联票本的系列音乐会中的一场））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{superscription}{ˌsu:pə'skripʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n. 刻字，题字 *super (= above, over)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{scribe}{skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n.（文牍、经文）抄写员 *-e (= person who)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{scribble}{'skribl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{v. 潦草地写，乱涂，乱画 *-le (= small, little)}
  \wordexample{scribbling block（拍纸簿，便条簿）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{scribbler}{'skriblə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n. 写字潦草的人；无足轻重的作家}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{script}{skript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n. 手迹，笔迹；手写体；脚本，底稿}
  \wordexample{scripted（照稿念的）}
  \wordexample{script writer（脚本作家，编剧）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{scripture}{'skriptʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n.（The (Holy) Scriptures）基督教《圣经》；（基督教以外的其他宗教的）圣典，经典；}
  \wordsense{a. 引自圣经的，与圣经有关的}
  \wordexample{scripture lesson（圣经课）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SPERS[SPARS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 spers 意为 to sprinkle, to strew。spars 是其变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{aspersion}{ə'spə:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人散播 a(ad (= to) + spers (= strew))}
  \wordsense{n. 洒圣水；诽谤，中伤}
  \wordphrase{asperse 洒圣水；诽谤，中伤}
  \wordexample{The movie star's autobiography cast aspersions on every director she'd worked with.（那个电影演员在自传里诽谤了所有与她合作过的导演。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{disperse}{di'spə:s}
  \wordbreakdown{分别散播 di<dis (= away, apart) + spers (= strew)}
  \wordsense{vt. 驱散，使散开}
  \wordphrase{dispersal 散布，分散；解散 dispersion 散布；分散}
  \wordexample{The crowd of protesters dispersed when the police arrived.（警察一到，示威者们就散开了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sparse}{spɑ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPERS[SPARS]}
  \wordsense{a. 稀少的，稀疏的 *spars<spers (= strew)}
  \wordexample{sparse population（稀少的人口） sparse beard（稀疏的胡须）}
\end{word}

\chapter{TRIBUT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 tribut 意为 to give, to pay。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{attribute}{ə'tribju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{给某人某物 at(ad (= to) + tribut (= give))}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……；认为……属于……}
  \wordsense{n. ['ætribju:t] 属性，特征；象征}
  \wordphrase{attributable 可归因于……的 attributive 属性的；【语】用做定语的}
  \wordphrase{attribution 归因，归属；属性}
  \wordexample{To what do you attribute her extreme anger?（你认为是什么让她这么生气？）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{retribution}{ˌretri'bju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{还、作为答谢还给 re (= again, back) + tribut (= give, pay)}
  \wordsense{n. 报答；报应}
  \wordphrase{retributive 报答的；报应的}
  \wordexample{Retribution will quickly follow.（报应将很快随之降临。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{contribute}{kən'tribju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordsense{v. 贡献，捐献；提供；投稿 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordexample{contributor（贡献者，捐献者；投稿者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{contribution}{ˌkɒntri'bju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordsense{n. 贡献，捐献；提供；投稿；捐助金；捐献的物品 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordexample{contributions to the relief fund（给救济基金的捐款） contributions for the July issue（给七月刊的投稿）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{contributory}{kən'tribjutəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordsense{a. 贡献的；有助于……的；捐献的，义捐的；分担的 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordexample{contributory negligence（【律】与有过失，共同过失）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{distribute}{di'stribju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordsense{v. 分配，分发；散布，分布；区分，分类 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordexample{distributor（分配者，配给者；批发商；配电盘）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{distribution}{ˌdistri'bju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordsense{n. 分配，分发；分布 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordexample{distribution of the profits（利益分配） distribution of wealth（财富的分配）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{distributive}{di'stribjutiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordsense{a. 分配的；分布的 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordexample{distributive trades（分配行业）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{tribute}{'tribju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordsense{n. 定期的贡物；（表达尊敬、称赞的）行动、言语、礼物}
  \wordexample{pay tribute to（向……进贡） floral tributes（献花）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{tributary}{'tribjutəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordsense{a. 进贡的；（河等）支流的}
  \wordsense{n. 进贡国，附庸国；支流}
\end{word}

\chapter{VER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 ver 意为 true。此外 ver 还有 to feel awe 和 to fear 的意思。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{aver}{ə'və:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{告诉某人是事实，没错 a<ad (= to) + ver (= true)}
  \wordsense{vt. 断言；证明……属实}
  \wordexample{The prosecuting attorney avers that the defendant murdered his mother.（检察官断言被告谋杀了自己的母亲。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{verify}{'verifai}
  \wordbreakdown{使成为真实 ver (= true)}
  \wordsense{vt. 核实（真实性或正确性）；证实，证明}
  \wordphrase{verification 证实，证明}
  \wordphrase{verifiable 能证实的}
  \wordexample{I can verify that Susan arrived late because her car had a flat tire.（我可以证明苏珊是因为车胎漏气才来晚的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{veritable}{'veritəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{使能够成为事实的 ver (= true)}
  \wordsense{a. 真正的，名副其实的}
  \wordphrase{verity 真正，真实；确实存在的事物；真理 verily 真实地；真正地}
  \wordexample{She's earned a veritable fortune selling her cakes and cookies.（她靠卖糕点成了真正的富翁。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{verily}{'verili}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER}
  \wordsense{ad. 真实地；真正地}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{verisimilitude}{ˌverisi'militju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER}
  \wordsense{n. 貌似真实；逼真（的事物） *simil (= same)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{verity}{'verəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER}
  \wordsense{n.（言语等的）真实性；真理}
\end{word}

\chapter{FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 fac, fact, fec / fect, fic / fict 意为 to make, to do。feas, feit, feat, fit 等是其变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affectation}{ˌæfek'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{使靠近 af<ad (= to, near) + fect (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{n. 装模作样；假装}
  \wordphrase{affect 假装；影响}
  \wordphrase{affectionately 充满深情地}
  \wordexample{affected 假装的；受影响的 cf. affection 影响；爱情；感情}
  \wordexample{His cigarette holder is merely an affectation.（他的烟斗只不过是装样子罢了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{defect}{di'fekt}
  \wordbreakdown{做某物时漏掉一定要放入的材料 de (= from) + fect (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{n. 缺点，缺陷，不足之处}
  \wordsense{vi. from + sth 脱离，逃跑}
  \wordphrase{defection 脱离，脱党；叛教，变节 defector 叛离者，变节者}
  \wordphrase{defective 不完美的，有缺陷的}
  \wordexample{Almost everyone has at least one major character defect.（几乎所有的人都至少有一个较大的性格缺陷。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{malefactor}{ˈmælifæktə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{做坏事的人 male<mal (= bad) + fact (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{n. 罪人；恶人}
  \wordphrase{malefaction 罪行，坏事 maleficent 有害的，作恶的}
  \wordexample{My father always jokingly asks what kind of malefactor I'm dating now.（我父亲总是开玩笑地问我，最近在和一个什么样的坏小子约会。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{factual}{'fæktʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{实际所做或所制作的 fact (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 事实上的；实际的}
  \wordexample{TV docudramas are a blend of both the factual and the fictional.（纪实电视剧是事实与虚构的结合体。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{facile}{'fæsail}
  \wordbreakdown{易于做或制作的 fac (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 容易的，易得到的；做事不花力气的；熟练的；流畅的}
  \wordexample{Class, don't give me facile responses. Really think about your answers.（同学们，不要给我那种很容易就能想到的答案，一定要好好思考一下再回答。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{beneficent}{bi'nefisnt}
  \wordbreakdown{做善事、亲切之事的 bene (= good) + fic (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 做善事的，仁慈的；有益的}
  \wordphrase{beneficence 善行，慈善 benefaction 善行，施舍 benefactor 捐助人，捐赠者；恩人}
  \wordexample{I generally find in life that beneficent acts are answered in kind.（我发现生活中总是善有善报。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{beneficial}{ˌbeni'fiʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{使有利的 bene (= good) + fic (= make, do)}
  \wordsense{a. 有益的，有帮助的}
  \wordexample{Taking a B-complex vitamin is proven to be beneficial for cardiac health.（已证实摄入复合维生素 B 有益心脏健康。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{efficacious}{ˌefi'keiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{做、制作得好的 ef<ex (= intensive) + fic (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 灵验的；有效的}
  \wordphrase{efficacy 效力，功效}
  \wordphrase{efficaciously 有效地}
  \wordexample{A high fiber diet is efficacious in preventing certain kinds of cancer.（高纤维饮食可以有效预防某些癌症。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{munificent}{mju:'nifisnt}
  \wordbreakdown{使成为礼物的 mun (= gift, service) + fic (= make, do)}
  \wordsense{a. 慷慨的，大方的；丰厚的}
  \wordphrase{munificence 慷慨；丰厚}
  \wordexample{The company made me a munificent offer, but I declined it.（那家公司的出价很慷慨，但我还是拒绝了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{officiate}{ə'fiʃieit}
  \wordbreakdown{好好做事 of<ob (= intensive) + fic (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{vi. at + sth 行使职权；主持}
  \wordphrase{office 事务所；办公室 officer 军官 officiator 主婚人}
  \wordexample{official 正式的；公务的；`(n.)` 公务员，官员}
  \wordexample{A Unitarian minister officiated at his funeral.（一位论教派的牧师主持了他的葬礼。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{officious}{ə'fiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{使妨碍的 of<ob (= in the way) + fic (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 好出主意的，多管闲事的}
  \wordexample{The government required us to use their somewhat officious translator.（政府要求我们用那个有点爱多管闲事的翻译官。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{proficient}{prə'fiʃnt}
  \wordbreakdown{首先做、制作 pro (= before) + fic (= make, do)}
  \wordsense{a. 精通的，熟练的}
  \wordsense{n. 能手；专家}
  \wordphrase{proficiency 精通，熟练}
  \wordexample{I wouldn't say I'm a great saxophone player. I'm just barely proficient.（我没有说我是萨克斯演奏家，只是比较精通而已。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{superficial}{ˌsu:pə'fiʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{使登到上边 super (= above, over) + fic (= make, do)}
  \wordsense{a. 表面的，外表的；肤浅的，没有深度的，浅薄的}
  \wordphrase{superficiality 表面性；肤浅，浅薄}
  \wordexample{I got a C-minus. The teacher said my essay was superficial.（我得了 C-，老师说我的论文太肤浅。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{surfeit}{'sɜ:fit}
  \wordbreakdown{使过度 sur (= over, excessive) + feit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{n. 过量；饮食过度；腻烦}
  \wordsense{v.（使）饮食过度；（使）腻烦}
  \wordexample{There's a surfeit of great recipes on the Internet.（网上的精美食谱多得让人烦。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{malfeasance}{mæl'fi:zəns}
  \wordbreakdown{做坏事 mal (= bad) + feas<fac (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{n. 违法行为；（特指公务员的）不正当行为，渎职}
  \wordexample{The mayor's well-publicized malfeasance cost him the election.（市长的渎职行为已公之于众，他为此付出了代价，在选举中失利。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{discomfit}{dis'kʌmfit}
  \wordbreakdown{使完全做不了 dis (= not) + com (= thoroughly) + fit<fact (= make, do)}
  \wordsense{vt. 挫败；扰乱}
  \wordphrase{discomfiture 挫败；狼狈}
  \wordexample{I'm discomfited by the criminal record of my daughter's fiance.（得知女儿未婚夫的前科后，我感到很惊慌。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{confection}{kən'fekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 糖果，点心；混合，合成，调制；（一般指女士服装）成衣 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{confectioner（糖果商）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{confectionery}{kən'fekʃənəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 糖果类；糖果制造（业）；糖果店 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{Lotte Confectionery（乐天糖果）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{effect}{i'fekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 结果；效果，效能，效力；影响；印象；（pl.）财产}
  \wordsense{v. 招致；达到（目的）；实现 *ef<ex (= out, intensive)}
  \wordexample{effect of heat upon metals（热对金属的影响） take effect（见效，生效，起作用） sound effects（音响效果） personal effects（个人财产） no effects（无存款（银行在空头支票上的批语，常写做 N/E）） effect a cure（治愈） effect an insurance policy（投保）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{effective}{i'fektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 有效的；给人深刻印象的；实际的，事实上的 *ef<ex (= out, intensive)}
  \wordexample{effective measures（有效的手段）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{effectual}{i'fektʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 奏效的，有效的 *ef<ex (= out, intensive)}
  \wordexample{effectual remedy（有效的治疗）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{infect}{in'fekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{v.【医】感染，传染；使受影响 *in (= into)}
  \wordexample{infected with cholera（染上霍乱）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{infection}{in'fekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（通过空气、水传播病菌的）感染；传染（病）；影响 *in (= into)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{infectious}{in'fekʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 传染的；（病）有传染性的；有感染力的；易传播的 *in (= into)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{perfect}{'pɜ:fikt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 完美的；娴熟的，熟练的}
  \wordsense{v. 使完美；完成 *per (= completely)}
  \wordexample{perfectly（圆满地，完美地）}
  \wordexample{perfectible（可完善的；可完成的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{perfection}{pə'fekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 完成；完美 *per (= completely)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{perfectionist}{pə'fekʃənist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 完美主义者，追求完美的人 *per (= completely)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{prefect}{'pri:fekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 地方行政长官；（法国等的）省长；（日本的）县长；（巴黎的）警察局局长 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{prefect of police（巴黎警察局局长）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{prefecture}{'pri:fektʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（法国、日本等的）省长（或县长等的）职位（或辖区、管辖区、任期、官邸）；县；专区；府 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{prefectural（县的；长官（巴黎警察局局长）的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{refection}{ri'fekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（靠食物）恢复体力；便餐，小吃 *re (= back)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{refectory}{ri'fektri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（修道院、大学等的）食堂 *re (= back), ory (= place where)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{unaffected}{ˌʌnə'fektid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 不矫揉造作的，自然的；未受影响的 *un (= not), af<ad (= near)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artifact}{'ɑ:tifækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{/ artefact**}
  \wordsense{n. 人造物品，人工制品 *art (= art, skill, craft)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{manufacture}{ˌmænju'fæktʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 制作，生产；捏造，虚构（证据、借口等）}
  \wordsense{n. 制造，制作，生产；（pl.）制品，产品 *manu (= hand)}
  \wordexample{manufacturer（生产者，制造者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{faction}{'fækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（政党的）派别，党派；派系斗争，内讧}
  \wordexample{factionalism（党派之争）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{factious}{'fækʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 好搞党派活动的；源于派别的}
  \wordexample{factious spirit（派性）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{factitious}{fæk'tiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 人为的；不自然的；虚假的}
  \wordexample{factitious enthusiasm（虚假的热情） factitious demand for goods（商品的虚假需求）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{factor}{'fæktə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n.【数】因子；因素；要因；代理人}
  \wordexample{factorize（将……分解为因子）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{factory}{'fæktri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 工厂，制造厂 *ory (= place where)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{factotum}{fæk'təutəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 勤杂工人}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{facilitate}{fə'siliteit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 使容易；促进，帮助}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{facility}{fə'siləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 能力，才能；（pl.）设施，设备；（供特别用途的）场所}
  \wordexample{great facility in learning English（学好英语的能力） facilities for study（研究室，学习室） sports facilities（运动设施）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{facsimile}{fæk'simili}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 誊写，复制；复制品 *simil (= same)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{faculty}{'fækəlti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 才能，本领；（大学的）系；院；（大学的）全体教职员}
  \wordexample{the faculty of making friends easily（容易结交朋友的能力） the Faculty of Science（理学院） faculty meeting（全体教职员会议）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{fiction}{'fikʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 虚构，想像的事；【总称】小说}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{fictitious}{fik'tiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 虚构的，假的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{beneficiary}{ˌbeni'fiʃəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（遗嘱、保险等的）受益人 *bene (= good), ary (= person who)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deficiency}{di'fiʃnsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 缺乏，不足；不足的数量、数额；缺点，不足之处 *de (= away)}
  \wordexample{deficiency diseases（【医】营养缺乏症） a deficiency of \$100（缺 100 美元）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deficient}{di'fiʃnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 缺乏的，不足的 *de (= away)}
  \wordexample{deficient in courage（勇气不足的） a mentally deficient person（有智力缺陷的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deficit}{'defisit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 不足的数额，赤字 *de (= away)}
  \wordexample{budget deficit（预算赤字）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{difficulty}{'difikəlti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 困难，艰难；难事；难题 *dif<dis (= away)}
  \wordexample{difficult（困难的，艰难的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{edifice}{'edifis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（宏伟的）建筑物；《喻》精神建设 *edi (= build)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{efficient}{i'fiʃnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 效率高的；有能力的，能胜任的；有效的 *ef<ex (= intensive, out)}
  \wordexample{efficiently（效率高地；有效地）}
  \wordexample{efficient methods of teaching（有效的教学法） efficient secretary（能干的秘书）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{efficiency}{i'fiʃnsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 效率；效力，效能 *ef<ex (= intensive, out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{magnificent}{mæg'nifisnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 极美的；壮丽的，宏伟的，宏大的；豪华的 *magn (= great)}
  \wordexample{magnificent house（豪华住宅）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suffice}{sə'fais}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 充足，足够；使满足 *suf<sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{Your word will suffice.（你来说就行了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sufficient}{sə'fiʃnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 充分的 *suf<sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{sufficiently（充分地）}
  \wordexample{sufficiency（充分，足量）}
  \wordexample{sufficient condition（充分条件）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{feasible}{'fi:zəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 可实行的；可能的；适用的 *feas<fac (= do)}
  \wordexample{feasibility（可行性）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{counterfeit}{'kauntəfit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 伪造}
  \wordsense{n. 赝品，伪造品}
  \wordsense{a. 伪造的，仿造的 *counter (= against), feit<fact (= make, do)}
  \wordexample{counterfeiter（伪造者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{forfeit}{'fɔ:fit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{v.（因犯罪、过失等）丧失（地位、财产、权力等）}
  \wordsense{n. 丧失的东西；没收物；罚款，罚金；罚物游戏的抵押物 *for (= out of doors), feit<fact (= make, do)}
  \wordexample{forfeiture（丧失；没收）}
  \wordexample{forfeit the good opinion of one's friends（丧失了朋友们的好感） forfeit one's property（没收某人的财产）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{feat}{fi:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 业绩，功绩；英勇事迹；技艺；武艺 *feat<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordexample{brilliant feats of engineering（工程学领域的辉煌业绩） feats of valor（英勇的壮举）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{feature}{'fi:tʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 特征；特色；（pl.）面貌，容貌；（报纸的）特写，特别报道；电影正片}
  \wordsense{v. 以……为特色 *feat<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordexample{featureless（平淡无奇的，无特色的）}
  \wordexample{unusual features in a political program（政治纲领与众不同的特点） a man of handsome features（美男子） newspaper that makes a feature of sport（以体育报道为特色的报纸） double features（【电影】两片连映）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{defeat}{di'fi:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 使失败，击败，使受挫；【律】使无效}
  \wordsense{n. 失败 *de (= down), feat<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordexample{defeatism（失败主义） defeatist（失败主义者）}
  \wordexample{four victories and two defeats（四胜两败）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{benefit}{'benifit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 益处；帮助；义演；义卖；补助费，救济金；（根据保险规定所得的）保险金}
  \wordsense{v. 有益于；得益 *bene (= good), fit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordexample{the benefits of a good education（优质教育的益处） benefit performance / concert（义演 / 义演音乐会） medical / unemployment benefit（医疗津贴 / 失业救济金）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{profit}{'prɔfit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 利益；收益；利润}
  \wordsense{v. 得益；有益于 *pro (= before), fit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordexample{profit margin（利润率） profit sharing（分红制）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{profitable}{'prɔfitəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{a. 有益的；有赢利的，有利的 *pro (= before), fit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordexample{profitably（有益地，有利地）}
  \wordexample{profitable investment（有利润的投资） profitable instruction（有益的教导）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{profiteer}{ˌprɔfi'tiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 牟取暴利}
  \wordsense{n. 奸商；牟取暴利者 *pro (= before), fit<fact (= do, make)}
\end{word}

\chapter{FRONT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 front 意为 forehead, face。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affront}{ə'frʌnt}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人凑过脸 af<ad (= to) + front (= face, forehead)}
  \wordsense{vt. 公然侮辱；勇敢地面对}
  \wordsense{n. 公然侮辱}
  \wordphrase{affrontive 侮辱性的}
  \wordexample{Priests who marry are an affront to the church.（结婚的牧师对教会来说是一种侮辱。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{confront}{kən'frʌnt}
  \wordbreakdown{一起对顶额头 con<com (= together) + front (= forehead, face)}
  \wordsense{vt. 面临；勇敢地面对；对抗}
  \wordphrase{confrontation 对抗}
  \wordexample{It's important to confront your fears rather than run from them.（重要的是，你应该勇敢地去面对你内心的恐惧，而不是逃避。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{effrontery}{i'frʌntəri}
  \wordbreakdown{将额头向外探 ef<ex (= out) + front (= forehead)}
  \wordsense{n. 厚颜无耻（的行为）}
  \wordexample{The doorman had the effrontery to deny me entrance to the hotel.（门卫厚颜无耻地阻止我进饭店。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{frontage}{'frʌntidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n.（建筑物等的）正面；临街地}
  \wordexample{frontage road（沿街道路（与高速公路平行的辅助道路））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{frontal}{'frʌntl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{a. 正面的}
  \wordsense{n. 额骨}
  \wordexample{frontal attack / assault（正面攻击） full frontal nudity（全正面裸体）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{frontier}{'frʌntiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n. 边境，国境；（探索活动的）新领域}
  \wordexample{town on the frontier（国境线上的城市） frontier disputes（边境纷争） frontier incidents（边境（冲突）事件） frontier spirit（开拓者精神）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{frontiersman}{'frʌntiəzmən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n. 边远地区的居民}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{frontispiece}{'frʌntispi:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n.（书的）卷首插画}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{frontlet}{'frʌntlit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n.（丝带等）额饰 *let (= small)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{forefront}{'fɔ:frʌnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n. 最前面，最前方；最重要的位置 *fore (= before)}
  \wordexample{in the forefront of the battle（在战斗的最前线） in the forefront of my mind（在我心中最重要）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SEVER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sever 意为 serious, earnest, to cut。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{asseverate}{ə'sevəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得非常深刻和严肃 as<ad (= to, intensive) + sever (= serious)}
  \wordsense{vt. 断言}
  \wordphrase{asseveration 断言}
  \wordexample{The professor asseverated that more than half the students had cheated.（教授断言一半以上的学生都作弊了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{persevere}{ˌpɜ:sɪ'viə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{从头到尾始终非常严肃 per (= through, throughout) + sever (= serious)}
  \wordsense{vi. in + sth 坚持不懈；（辩论中）固执己见}
  \wordphrase{perseverance 忍耐，坚持}
  \wordphrase{persevering 有毅力的，锲而不舍的；固执的}
  \wordexample{It's important to persevere, especially when you feel like quitting.（坚持不懈很重要，尤其是当你想放弃的时候。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{severe}{si'viə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEVER}
  \wordsense{a. 严重的；剧烈的；严格的，苛刻的 *sever (= serious)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{dissever}{di'sevə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEVER}
  \wordsense{v.（使）分离；（使）分裂 *dis (= away), sever (= cut)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sever}{'sevə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEVER}
  \wordsense{v. 切断，割断；中断；断绝；（使）分隔，（使）分离 *sever (= cut)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{severance}{'sevərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEVER}
  \wordsense{n. 割断，断绝；分离，隔离 *sever (= cut)}
  \wordexample{severance payment（解雇金，离职金） severance of diplomatic relations / of communications（断绝外交关系 / 通信中断）}
\end{word}

\chapter{TENU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 tenu 意为 thin。同义词根为 mac。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{attenuate}{ə'tenjuət}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得非常细 at<ad (= to, intensive) + tenu (= thin)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使减弱；使减少}
  \wordphrase{attenuation 减弱；减少}
  \wordexample{Aspirin normally attenuates the severity of a fever.（通常阿司匹林可以退高烧。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{extenuate}{ik'stenjueit}
  \wordbreakdown{脱离于罪，使罪变轻 ex (= out) + tenu (= thin)}
  \wordsense{vt. 企图减轻（罪行等）}
  \wordexample{extenuation（罪行等的）减轻}
  \wordexample{Her youth extenuates her error of judgment.（因为她年纪小，所以对她的判断失误减轻了处罚。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{tenuous}{'tenjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TENU}
  \wordsense{a. 稀薄的；纤细的；（区别）细微的，微妙的}
  \wordexample{tenuity（细；薄；稀薄；微弱）}
  \wordexample{the tenuous web of a spider（蜘蛛的细丝网）}
\end{word}

\chapter{COL[CUL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 col 意为 neck。另外，col 还有 to filter, large intestine, to till, glue 等含义。cul 是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{accolade}{'ækəleid}
  \wordbreakdown{挂在脖子附近 ac<ad (= to, near) + col (= neck)}
  \wordsense{n. 称赞；赞赏；（the \textasciitilde{}）骑士爵位的授予典礼}
  \wordexample{The poet received a prestigious accolade from the Literary Society.（那位诗人获得了文学协会的极高赞誉。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{collar}{'kɒlə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 领子；（狗、马等的）项圈；（连接两个管子、连杆、车轴等的）箍 *col (= neck)}
  \wordexample{blue / white collar workers（蓝领 / 白领工作者） collar stud（领扣） collar bone（锁骨）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{decollation}{di:kə'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 断头 *de (= down), col (= neck)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{décolleté*}{dei'kɒltei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{a.《法》（衣服）低领露肩的 *de (= down, away), col (= neck)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{percolate}{'pɜ:kəleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{v. 过滤；（使）渗出；（使）浸透 *per (= through), col (= filter)}
  \wordexample{percolator（（带有过滤器的）过滤式咖啡壶）}
  \wordexample{Water percolates through sand.（水从沙子里渗出来。） I make coffee by percolating boiling water through ground coffee.（我用沸水滤过咖啡粉的方法煮咖啡。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{colander}{'kʌləndə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 过滤器；（厨房用）滤水器（碗状，有孔，用来过滤或淘洗食物） *col (= filter)}
  \wordphrase{cullender (= colander)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{culvert}{'kʌlvət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 阴沟；地下电缆管道 *cul<col (= filter)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{colon}{'kəulən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 结肠 *col (= large intestine)}
  \wordexample{colon cleanse（肠清洗剂）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{colic}{'kɒlik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n.（无腹泻的剧烈的）腹痛 *col (= large intestine)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{colitis}{kəu'laitis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 大肠炎 *col (= large intestine), itis (= inflammation)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{colony}{'kɒləni}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 殖民地；侨民；群体；【生】群落 *col (= till)}
  \wordexample{colonist（殖民地开拓者）}
  \wordexample{the South Korean colony in New York（纽约的韩国侨民） a colony of artists（一群美术家） a colony of ants（一群蚂蚁）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{colonial}{kə'ləuniəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{a. 殖民地的}
  \wordsense{n. 殖民地居民（尤指开拓者的子孙） *col (= till)}
  \wordexample{colonialism（殖民政策；殖民主义） colonialist（殖民政策的支持者，殖民主义者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{colonize}{'kɒlənaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{v. 开拓殖民地 *col (= till)}
  \wordexample{colonization（殖民） colonizer（殖民地开拓者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{neocolonialism}{ˌni:əukə'ləuniəlizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 新殖民主义 *neo (= new), col (= till)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{cultivate}{'kʌltiveit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{v. 耕种（土地），耕作；栽培；培养 *cul<col (= till)}
  \wordexample{cultivated（有教养的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{cultivation}{ˌkʌlti'veiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 耕作；栽培；培养；修养 *cul<col (= till)}
  \wordexample{cultivator（耕作者，耕耘者，栽培者）}
  \wordexample{cultivation of the soil（耕种土地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{culture}{'kʌltʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 文化；教养；修养；栽培；（医学、科学研究用微生物的）培养}
  \wordsense{v. 培养（细菌等）；栽培，耕作 *cul<col (= till)}
  \wordexample{cultured（（人）有教养的，有修养的；（兴趣、爱好等）高雅的） cultural（文化的；教养的）}
  \wordexample{a man of culture（有修养的人） culture of the mind（心性修养） culture of cotton（棉花栽培） silk culture（养蚕） culture of cholera germs（霍乱菌的培养） cultured pearl（养殖珍珠）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{collage}{kə'lɑ:ʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 拼贴画（用纸、布、照片、金属等的小片粘贴拼合而成） *col (= glue)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{protocol}{'prəutəkɒl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordsense{n.（条约等的）草案；议定书；（外交）礼节 *proto (= first), col (= glue)}
\end{word}

\chapter{COST}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cost 意为 rib, side。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{accost}{ə'kɒst}
  \wordbreakdown{走到某人身边 ac<ad (= to) + cost (= rib, side)}
  \wordsense{vt. 走近与……搭话}
  \wordexample{My ex-wife accosted me in the driveway demanding an increase in alimony.（我的前妻从车道上过来跟我搭话，要求我提高赡养费。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{costalgia}{kɒs'tældʒiə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COST}
  \wordsense{n. 肋骨痛}
\end{word}

\chapter{JUD}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 jud 意为 to judge。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{adjudicate}{ə'dʒu:dikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{对某物进行判断 ad (= to) + jud (= judge)}
  \wordsense{v. 判决；宣告}
  \wordexample{My mother always adjudicates the fights I have with my sister.（每当我和我妹妹打架时，总是由妈妈来裁决。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{judicious}{dʒu:'diʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{具有判断力的 jud (= judge)}
  \wordsense{a. 有判断力的；明智的}
  \wordexample{The judicious use of antibiotics can prevent severe staphylococcus infections.（明智地使用抗生素可以预防葡萄球菌的严重感染。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{judge}{dʒʌdʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{n. 裁判员；鉴定人，鉴赏家}
  \wordsense{v. 判决，裁判；判断}
  \wordexample{judge advocate（军法官；军事检察官）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{judgment}{'dʒʌdʒmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{n. 裁判；审判；辨别力，判断力；意见}
  \wordexample{the Day of Judgment（（上帝的）最后审判日） error of judgment（判断错误） a man of judgment（有判断力的人，有辨别力的人） in my judgment（以我之见） judgment seat（法官席；法庭）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{judicature}{'dʒu:dikətʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{n. 司法；【总称】法官}
  \wordexample{the Supreme Court of Judicature（《英》最高法院）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{judicial}{dʒu:'diʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{a. 司法的；法庭的；法官的；审判的}
  \wordexample{the judicial bench（法官们） judicial separation（法定分居） judicial murder（合法但不公正的死刑判决） judicial power(s)（司法权） judicial precedent（司法判例） judicial review（《美》司法审查（权））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{judiciary}{dʒu:'diʃəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{n. 司法部；（国家的）司法系统；【总称】法官}
  \wordexample{the judiciary（司法部） cf. the executive（行政部门） the legislature（立法机关）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{extrajudicial}{ˌekstrədʒu:'diʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{a. 司法程序以外的；（正常）法律以外的 *extra (= outside)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{injudicious}{ˌindʒu:'diʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{a. 缺乏判断力的，不明智的 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{injudicious remarks（不明智的话）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{prejudge}{pri:'dʒʌdʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{v. 过早判断，预先判断；【律】不审而判 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{prejudgment（不审而判）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{prejudice}{'predʒudis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{n. 偏见；【律】侵害，损害}
  \wordsense{v. 使抱偏见；使怀成见；损害 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{prejudiced（抱有成见的，有偏见的） prejudicial（引起偏见的；有害的，不利的）}
  \wordexample{racial prejudice（种族偏见） without prejudice（对合法权利无损害） prejudiced opinion（偏见）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{unprejudiced}{ʌn'predʒudist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordsense{a. 无偏见的；公平的 *un (= not), pre (= before)}
\end{word}

\chapter{JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 junct, join 意为 to join, to bind。jug 是它们的变形。此外，还有意为 to bind, to tie 的来自拉丁语的 lig [li, leag, ly], cinct, nect / nex, strict / string / stress 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 bond，含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{adjunct}{'ædʒʌŋkt}
  \wordbreakdown{另外再捆绑 ad (= to, add) + junct (= join, bind)}
  \wordsense{n. 附属物；助手，副手}
  \wordsense{a. 附加的，辅助的}
  \wordexample{A direct mail promotion will be an adjunct to our print advertising campaign.（以印刷广告活动为主，直接邮寄产品促销是辅助手段。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{enjoin}{in'dʒɔin}
  \wordbreakdown{用力捆绑 en (= intensive) + join (= join, bind)}
  \wordsense{vt. 命令；吩咐；禁止}
  \wordexample{The store was enjoined from selling the inflammable children's clothing.（那家商店被禁止出售易燃性儿童服装。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{subjugate}{'sʌbdʒəgeit}
  \wordbreakdown{在下面捆 sub (= under) + jug (= bind, join)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使服从，征服；使从属；使屈从}
  \wordphrase{subjugation 征服；从属}
  \wordexample{Rapists subjugate women not only physically but also emotionally.（强奸犯不仅从身体上而且从精神上使女性屈从。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conjunction}{kən'dʒʌŋkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n. 结合，连接；【语】连接词 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{in conjunction with（与……协力） a coordinate / subordinate conjunction（并列连词 / 从属连词） an unusual conjunction of circumstances（各种情况的奇特巧合）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{conjunctive}{kən'dʒʌŋktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{a. 结合的；连接的；【语】连接词的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{conjunctiva（结膜） conjunctivitis（结膜炎）}
  \wordexample{conjunctive word（连接词）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{conjuncture}{kən'dʒʌŋktʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n.（事件的）共同发生；紧要关头 *con<com (= together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{disjunctive}{dis'dʒʌŋktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{a.【语】转折的}
  \wordsense{n.【语】转折连词 *dis (= away, apart)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{junction}{'dʒʌŋkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n. 连接，接合，汇合；（道路、铁道等的）会合处，交叉点；（铁路的）联轨站；（电路的）接点，中继}
  \wordexample{effect a junction with the allies（与联合军汇合） effect a junction with the Kyung-Bu Railway Line（与京釜铁路线汇合） junction circuit（中继电路）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{juncture}{'dʒʌŋktʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n. 形势，情况，事态；危急关头；联合，接合；汇合}
  \wordexample{at this juncture（在这个当口，在这个节骨眼上）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{junta}{'dʒʌntə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n.（西班牙、意大利等的）政务（审议）会；（通过军事政变掌握政权的）军人集团 *junt<junct (= join)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{junto}{'dʒʌntəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n. 秘密政治集团；派系 *junt<junct (= join)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{subjunctive}{səb'dʒʌŋktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{a.【语】虚拟的}
  \wordsense{n.【语】虚拟语气，虚拟式 *sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{subjunctive mood（虚拟语气）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{adjoin}{ə'dʒɔin}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{v. 贴近，毗邻 *ad (= near)}
  \wordexample{adjoining（贴近的，毗邻的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{conjoin}{kən'dʒɔin}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{v. 结合；联合 *con<com (= together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{conjoint}{kən'dʒɔint}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{a. 共同的；联合的 *con<com (= together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{disjoint}{dis'dʒɔint}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{v.（使）脱节，（使）脱臼；拆开；解体 *dis (= away, apart)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{disjointed}{dis'dʒɔintid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{a. 脱臼的；（谈话、文章等）无条理的 *dis (= away, apart)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{joint}{dʒɔint}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n. 接点，接线，接合处，接缝；关节；（下等的）小酒馆；大块肉；含有大麻的香烟}
  \wordsense{a. 共同的，共有的；合作的}
  \wordsense{v.（用接缝方式）连接；从关节处切开}
  \wordexample{jointly（联合地，共同地）}
  \wordexample{finger joints（手指关节） out of joint（脱臼） joint efforts（共同努力） joint communique（联合公报） joint statement（共同声明） joint account（联合存款账户） joint authors（合著者） Joint Chiefs of Staff（《美》参谋长联席会议（JCS）） joint resolution（《美》（国会两院的）共同决议） joint session（（议会、国会两院的）联席会议） joint venture（合作投资） clip joint（索价特别高昂的商店（或夜总会、酒吧等））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{jointure}{'dʒɔintʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n.（结婚时指定的）寡妇授予产}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{subjoin}{səb'dʒɔin}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{v. 附加 *sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{subjoin a postscript to a letter（在信件上加附言）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conjugal}{'kɒndʒəgl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{a. 结婚的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{conjugally（结婚地）}
  \wordexample{conjugality（婚姻；夫妇生活）}
  \wordexample{conjugal happiness（婚姻生活的幸福） conjugal affection（夫妻之间的恩爱）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conjugate}{'kɒndʒəgeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{v.【语】列举动词的词形变化；使结合 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{conjugation（词形变化；结合）}
\end{word}

\chapter{UMBR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 umbr 意为 shade, to shadow。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{adumbrate}{'ædʌmbreit}
  \wordbreakdown{使看起来非常模糊 ad (= to, intensive) + umbr (= shadow)}
  \wordsense{vt. 勾画轮廓；预示}
  \wordphrase{adumbration 轮廓；预示}
  \wordexample{The reduced quarterly earnings adumbrate layoffs down the road.（季度收益削减预示着将要裁员。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{umbrage}{'ʌmbridʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UMBR}
  \wordsense{n. 不快，恼火；树荫}
  \wordexample{give / take umbrage at（因……使感到不愉快）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{umbrella}{ʌm'brelə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UMBR}
  \wordsense{n. 雨伞；《喻》（政治、经济界等的）保护伞}
  \wordexample{under the Conservative umbrella（在保守党的保护下） umbrella stand（伞架）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FIN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 fin 意为 end, limit。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 termin, ultim 和来自希腊语的 bys。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affinity}{ə'finəti}
  \wordbreakdown{末端在近处 af<ad (= to, near) + fin (= end)}
  \wordsense{n. 亲密关系；相似；（异性间的）亲密感，吸引力；喜好，迷恋；【化】亲合性}
  \wordexample{I feel a greater affinity for dogs than for cats.（相比猫，我更喜欢狗。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{definitive}{di'finitiv}
  \wordbreakdown{完全见尾 de (= intensive) + fin (= end)}
  \wordsense{a. 最终的；决定性的；限定性的}
  \wordsense{n.【语】限定词}
  \wordexample{The appeals court offered the definitive ruling in the case.（上诉法庭对那一事件作出了最终判决。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{infinite}{'infinət}
  \wordbreakdown{没有结尾 in (= not) + fin (= end)}
  \wordsense{a. 无穷的，无限的，无数的；极大的}
  \wordsense{n.（the \textasciitilde{}）上帝}
  \wordphrase{infinitely 无限地，无穷地}
  \wordphrase{infinitesimal【数】无穷小的；极小的}
  \wordexample{Some mystics who experience God say they feel infinite love and peace.（一些感受到过上帝的神秘主义者说他们体会到无穷的爱和安宁。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{confine}{kən'fain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{v. 限制，限定；禁闭，监禁}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）范围，界限，边界 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{confined（受限制的；狭窄的）}
  \wordexample{beyond the confines of human knowledge（超出人类的知识范围） within the confines of this valley（在这个山谷的范围之内）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{confinement}{kən'fainmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 限制，局限；幽禁，监禁；分娩 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{be placed in confinement（被监禁） six months solitary confinement（六个月的单独监禁）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{define}{di'fain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{v. 给……下定义；阐明 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{definable（可定义的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{definite}{'definit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{a. 清楚的；明确的 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{definitely（明确地，确切地；（用于回答）肯定地，无疑地）}
  \wordexample{definite time and place（明确的时间和地点） definite article（定冠词）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{definition}{ˌdefi'niʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 定义；限定；（轮廓的）清晰；（镜头的）分辨率 *de (= down)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{infinitive}{in'finitiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n.【语】不定式}
  \wordsense{a.【语】不定式的 *in (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{infinitude}{in'finitju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 无限，无穷 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{infinitude of small particles（无数小微粒）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{infinity}{in'finiti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 无限，无穷 *in (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{refine}{ri'fain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{v. 精制；精炼；使文雅；推敲 *re (= again)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{refined}{ri'faind}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{a. 精炼的；文雅的；精制的 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{refined manners / language（文雅的态度 / 言语）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{refinement}{ri'fainmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 精炼，精制；文雅；精巧，精细 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{a lady of refinement（文雅的女子）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{refiner}{ri'fainə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 精制者，精炼者；精制机，精炼机 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{sugar refiners（制糖厂厂主）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{refinery}{ri'fainəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 精炼厂 *re (= again), ery (= place where)}
  \wordexample{sugar refinery（制糖厂） oil refinery（炼油厂）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{indefinite}{in'definit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{a. 含糊的，模糊的；不明确的 *in (= not), de (= down)}
  \wordexample{indefinite answer（含糊的回答）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{indefinable}{ˌindi'fainəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{a. 不确定的，难以说明的 *in (= not), de (= down)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{final}{'fainl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{a. 最后的，最终的}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）决赛；期末考试；（当天报纸的）末版}
  \wordexample{finalist（决赛参赛选手） finally（最后，最终）}
  \wordexample{tennis finals（网球决赛） take his finals（参加期末考试）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{finale}{fi'nɑ:li}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 结尾；（戏剧的）终场；（交响乐等的）末乐章，终曲}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{finality}{fai'næləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 结局；决定性}
  \wordexample{speak with an air of finality（断言）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{finalize}{'fainəlaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{v. 完成；结束}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{finance}{fai'næns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 财政（学）；（pl.）（政府或公司的）财源}
  \wordsense{v.（为计划等）筹措、供给资金}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{financial}{fai'nænʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{a. 财政的；财务的；金融上的}
  \wordexample{financially（财政地）}
  \wordexample{financial year（财政年度） financial center（金融中心） financial condition（财政状况） financial crisis（金融危机） financial difficulties（财政困难） financial resources（财源）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{financier}{fai'nænsiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIN}
  \wordsense{n. 财政家；金融家}
\end{word}

\chapter{POS, POST, PON, POUND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 pos, post, pon, pound 意为 to put, to place。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 loc, sert, stal 和来自希腊语的 thes / thet / them, top 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{apposite}{'æpəzit}
  \wordbreakdown{放在近处的 ap<ad (= to, near) + pos (= put)}
  \wordsense{a. 适当的，合适的}
  \wordphrase{appositeness 适当，合适 appositely 适当地，合适地}
  \wordexample{Her jokes and smiles were not apposite behavior for her father's funeral.（她在她爸爸的葬礼上开玩笑和微笑不合适。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{depose}{di'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{将某人分开放 de (= from, away) + pos (= put)}
  \wordsense{vt. 罢免，废黜；【律】宣誓作证}
  \wordphrase{deposition 废黜；宣誓作证}
  \wordexample{A popular liberation force deposed the monarch and took power.（一支民众解放势力罢黜国王夺取了政权。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{exposition}{ˌekspə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{展开放在外边 ex (= out) + pos (= put)}
  \wordsense{n. 博览会，展览会；阐述；（剧本等的）提示部分}
  \wordexample{Novice screenwriters often deploy a film's exposition clumsily.（新编剧往往对电影提示部分的设置很生疏。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{repose}{ri'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{放在背后 re (= back) + pos (= put)}
  \wordsense{n. 休息，睡眠；平静}
  \wordsense{vi. 休息，歇息；长眠；放置；基于}
  \wordphrase{reposeful 平稳的，安详的}
  \wordexample{Is there anything so beautiful as a young child in repose?（还有什么比安然熟睡的婴儿更美丽的吗？）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{proponent}{prəu'pəunənt}
  \wordbreakdown{向前放、提出 pro (= forward, forth) + pon (= put)}
  \wordsense{n. 提议者，发起者}
  \wordexample{I've always been a proponent of gun control.（我一直都提倡管制枪支。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{imposter}{im'pɒstə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{放在某物上面 im<in (= on) + post (= put)}
  \wordsense{n. 冒名顶替者；骗子}
  \wordexample{I've read of several cases where respected physicians turned out to be impostors.（我阅读过几宗关于受尊敬的内科医生最终被查明是骗子的案例。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{expound}{ik'spaund}
  \wordbreakdown{放在外边，彻底说明 ex (= out, intensive) + pound (= put)}
  \wordsense{vt. 详细说明}
  \wordsense{vi. on + sth 详细说明}
  \wordexample{He loves to expound on any topic; he's such a blowhard.（他对每个话题都爱长篇大论，他真是个吹牛专家。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{indisposed}{ˌindi'spauzd}
  \wordbreakdown{不想分开放的 in (= not) + dis (= apart) + pos (= put)}
  \wordsense{a. 身体不舒服的；厌恶的，不情愿的}
  \wordphrase{indisposition 不愿意，不舒服}
  \wordexample{Mrs. Barnes can't attend the luncheon because she is indisposed.（巴恩斯夫人因身体不适而不能参加午宴。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{supposition}{ˌsʌpə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{放在下面 sup<sub (= under) + pos (= put)}
  \wordsense{n. 想像；假定}
  \wordphrase{suppose 假定，推测 supposed 假定的；想像的 supposedly 根据想像，根据推测 supposing 如果}
  \wordexample{My supposition is that next year's Cadillac model will look basically like this year's.（我推测明年卡迪拉克的样式基本上会和今年一样。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{apposition}{ˌæpə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 并置；【语】同位 *ap<ad (= near)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{compose}{kəm'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 构成；写作，作曲；排字，排版；使平静，使镇静 *com (= together, completely)}
  \wordexample{be composed of（由……组成）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{composed}{kəm'pauzd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 镇静的，沉着的 *com (= together, completely)}
  \wordexample{composedly（镇定地，沉着地）}
  \wordexample{composure（沉着，泰然自若）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{composer}{kəm'pauzə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 作曲家 *com (= completely)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{composite}{'kɒmpəzit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 混成的，合成的；综合的 *com (= together)}
  \wordexample{composite illustration（综合图解）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{composition}{ˌkɒmpə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 排版，排字；构成，成分；写作；作曲；作品；合成物 *com (= together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{compositor}{kəm'pɒzitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 排字工人 *com (= together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{compos mentis}{ˌkɒmpɒs'mentis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 心智健全的 *com (= together), ment (= mind)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{deposit}{di'pɒzit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 储蓄；寄存；预付订金；使沉淀，使沉积；放下；放置}
  \wordsense{n. 押金；存款保证金；沉淀物；矿床；寄存物；仓库，储藏所 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{deposit account（《英》定期存款） safe-deposit（（银行的）贵重物品保管库） deposit slip（存款单） money on deposit（存款） valuable new deposits of tin（新的有价值的锡矿床）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{depositor}{di'pɒzitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 存款人 *de (= down)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{depository}{di'pɒzitri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 存放处，仓库 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{depository of learning（知识宝库）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{dispose}{di'spauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 处理，处置；布置，排列；使愿意 *dis (= away)}
  \wordexample{dispose of rubbish（处理垃圾） Man proposes, God disposes.（谋事在人，成事在天。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{disposed}{di'spauzd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 愿意的；有倾向的 *dis (= away)}
  \wordexample{well- / ill-disposed（友好的 / 心存恶意的） indisposed（不舒服的，不愿意的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{disposable}{di'spauzəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a.（使用后）可随便处理的}
  \wordsense{n. 一次性物品（如纸杯等） *dis (= away)}
  \wordexample{disposable chopsticks / diapers / syringes / panties（一次性筷子 / 一次性尿布 / 一次性注射器 / 一次性内裤） disposable income（（工资的）可支配收入，可支配所得）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{disposal}{di'spauzl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 处理，处置；布置，排列；控制；出售 *dis (= away)}
  \wordexample{disposal bag（（飞机、饭店准备的）呕吐袋，废物袋） disposal sale（变卖处理） at one's disposal（由某人随意支配）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{disposition}{ˌdispə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 性情，脾气；倾向，意向；排列，布置；处置；支配（权）；天命，（神的）安排 *dis (= away)}
  \wordexample{disposition of Providence（上帝的安排）}
  \wordexample{God has the supreme disposition of all things.（上帝拥有支配万物的最高权力。） a man of a cheerful disposition（性格开朗的人） disposition of furniture in a room（房间里家具的布置）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{expose}{ik'spauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 使暴露；展示，陈列；揭露，揭发；使曝光 *ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{exposed goods（陈列的商品）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{exposé***}{ek'spauzei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n.（丑闻等的）揭露，揭发 *ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{exposed}{ik'spauzd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 暴露的；易受攻击的 *ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{be exposed to the wind and rain（暴露在风雨之中） be exposed to ridicule（遭受奚落）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{exposure}{ik'spauʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 暴露；陈列；揭露，揭发；（房间等的）方位，朝向 *ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{exposure meter（曝光计） exposure index（曝光指数） exposure to the rain（受雨淋） house with a southern exposure（朝南的房子）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{impose}{im'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 征收；把……强加于；（用欺骗手段）把……硬塞给 *im<in (= on)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{imposing}{im'pauziŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a.（因规模、外观、特征而）给人深刻印象的；雄伟的，庄严的 *im<in (= on)}
  \wordexample{imposing old lady（威严的老妇人） imposing air（威严的气势）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{imposition}{ˌimpə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n.（征收的）税 *im<in (= on)}
  \wordexample{imposition of new taxes（新设税项的征收）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{interpose}{ˌintə'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 提出（异议）；干涉，插入，介入 *inter (= between)}
  \wordexample{interposition（提出（异议）；干涉；插入（物））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{juxtapose}{ˌdʒʌkstə'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 并置，并列 *juxta (= side by side)}
  \wordexample{juxtaposition（并置，并列）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{oppose}{ə'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 反对，反抗，对抗；使相对 *op<ob (= against)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{opposed}{ə'pauzd}
