  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 反对的，敌对的 *op<ob (= against)}
  \wordexample{be opposed to（反对……） as opposed to（（表示对比）而，相对于……）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{opposable}{ə'pauzəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 可反对的；可与……相对的 *op<ob (= against)}
  \wordexample{opposable thumb（可以与其他手指相对的拇指）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{opposite}{'ɒpəzit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 对面的，正相反的；对立的}
  \wordsense{n. 反义词；对立物 *op<ob (= against)}
  \wordexample{one's opposite number（另一个单位内与自己职位相等的人） on the opposite side of the road（在路的对面） in the opposite direction（在相反方向）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{opposition}{ˌɒpə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 反对；（the O\textasciitilde{}）反对党 *op<ob (= against)}
  \wordexample{opposition party（反对党） leader of the Opposition（反对党的领导人） Opposition benches（反对党（议员）席） in opposition（（政党）在野的） in opposition to（反对……）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{pose}{pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n.（为画像或拍照片等而摆的）姿势，姿态；造作之态，装腔作势；伪装}
  \wordsense{v.（使）摆姿势；提出；引起，造成；伪装}
  \wordexample{poser（摆姿势的人；装腔作势的人；难以处理的问题，难题） poseur（装模作样的人，装腔作势的人）}
  \wordexample{pose many problems（提出很多问题）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{posit}{'pɒzit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 假设；安置}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{position}{pə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 职位；地位，身份；位置；态度；条件，情况}
  \wordsense{v. 把……摆正位置}
  \wordexample{high position in society（高的社会地位）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{positive}{'pɒzətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 确定的，确实的；肯定的，断定的，确信的；积极的；建设性的；《口》完全的；阳性的；正号的}
  \wordexample{positive knowledge（确切无误的知识） positive suggestion（建设性的提议） positive answer（肯定的回答）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{positively}{'pɒzətivli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{ad. 明确地；坚决地；确实；肯定地}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{positivism}{'pɒzətivizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n.【哲】实证主义，实证论}
  \wordexample{logical positivism（逻辑实证主义）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preposition}{ˌprepə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n.【语】前置词，介词 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{prepositional（前置词的，介词的）}
  \wordexample{prepositional phrase（介词短语） prepositional adverb（介词性副词）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{propose}{prə'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 提议，建议；求婚；推荐，提名 *pro (= forth)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{proposal}{prə'pauzl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 提案，提议；建议；求婚 *pro (= forth)}
  \wordexample{proposer（提议者）}
  \wordexample{proposal for peace（和平提议） girl who had five proposals in one week（一周内有五个人向她求婚的女孩）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{proposition}{ˌprɒpə'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 提案，建议；陈述，主张；命题；《口》要处理的事；《口》（向某人）提出（非法的、不道德的）提议 *pro (= forth)}
  \wordexample{tough proposition（棘手的人（或事））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{purpose}{'pɜ:pəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 目的，意志；用途；（讨论中的）议题}
  \wordsense{v. 打算 *pur<pro (= forth)}
  \wordexample{purpose-built（为特定目的建造的） of set purpose（故意地，有目的地） on purpose（故意地，有意地） to the purpose（适当（地）；与本题有关（地））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{purposeful}{'pɜ:pəsfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 意味深长的；有目的的 *pur<pro (= forth)}
  \wordexample{purposeless（无意义的；漫无目的的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{purposive}{'pɜ:pəsiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 有目的的 *pur<pro (= forth)}
  \wordexample{purposive movements（有目的的动作）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{repository}{ri'pɒzitri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 容器；贮藏室，仓库；（知识等的）宝库 *re (= back)}
  \wordexample{Korean Repository（《韩国的宝库》（杂志名））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{transpose}{træns'pauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 转换 *trans (= change)}
  \wordexample{transposition（转换）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{component}{kəm'pəunənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{a. 组成的，构成（成品）的}
  \wordsense{n. 成分，构成要素 *com (= together)}
  \wordexample{component parts（组成部分，零件） the components of a camera lens（照相机镜头的各个组成部分）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{deponent}{di'pəunənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n.【律】证人 *de (= down)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{exponent}{ik'spəunənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 说明者，解释者；（思想等的）拥护者；代表者；【数】指数 *ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{exponent of Darwin's theory of evolution（达尔文进化论的解说者（指赫胥黎））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{opponent}{ə'pəunənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n.（比赛、争论等的）对手，对方；对抗者，反对者 *op<ob (= against)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{postpone}{pəust'pəun}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 延期，推迟 *post (= after)}
  \wordexample{postponement（延期）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{compost}{'kɒmpɒst}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 混合肥料，堆肥}
  \wordsense{v. 将……做成堆肥 *com (= together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{expostulate}{ik'spɒstjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 告诫，劝告 *ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{expostulation（告诫，劝告）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{outpost}{'autpaust}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 前哨基地；边远居民点；边境}
  \wordexample{outpost of the Roman Empire（罗马帝国的边境）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{postulate}{'pɒstjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n. 假设；基本原理；【数】【逻】公设}
  \wordsense{v. 假定……存在；视……为当然}
  \wordexample{the postulates of Euclidean geometry（欧几里得几何公设）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{posture}{'pɒstʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{n.（身体的）姿势，状态；心境，态度}
  \wordsense{v. 使摆姿势；装模作样，故作姿态}
  \wordexample{reclining posture（斜靠着的姿势） in the present posture of public affairs（在公共事务目前的这种情况下） posture a model（让模特摆姿势）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{compound}{kəm'paund}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 合成，混合；妥协；加重；【经】以复利计算（利息）}
  \wordsense{a. 复合的，混合的，合成的}
  \wordsense{n. 混合物，合成物 *com (= together)}
  \wordexample{compound sentence（复合句） compound interest（复利） compound fracture（有创骨折）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{impound}{im'paund}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 扣押（证物等），没收；将（牲畜等）关在圈中 *im<in (= into)}
  \wordexample{impoundment（扣押；没收）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{propound}{prə'paund}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POS, POST, PON, POUND}
  \wordsense{v. 提议 *pro (= forth)}
\end{word}

\chapter{TRI, TRIT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 tri, trit 意为 to rub。同义词根为 fric。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{attrition}{ə'triʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{用力擦 at<ad (= to, intensive) + trit (= rub)}
  \wordsense{n. 消耗，磨损；（由于死亡、退休等造成的）人员自然缩减}
  \wordphrase{attrit 消耗；削弱 attrited 磨损的}
  \wordexample{The company is downsizing its staff by attrition rather than layoffs.（公司减员是通过人员自然缩减实现的，而非通过裁员。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{contrite}{'kɒntrait}
  \wordbreakdown{用力擦的 con<com (= intensive, together) + trit (= rub)}
  \wordsense{a. 悔罪的，深感懊悔的}
  \wordphrase{contrition 懊悔，忏悔}
  \wordexample{My nephew seemed genuinely contrite about having taken money from my purse.（我侄子看起来是在真心懊悔从我钱包里拿钱的事。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{detriment}{'detrimənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRI, TRIT}
  \wordsense{n. 损害，损伤 *de (= away, intensive)}
  \wordexample{to the detriment of his health（对他的健康有害）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{detrimental}{ˌdetri'mentl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRI, TRIT}
  \wordsense{a. 有害的 *de (= away, intensive)}
  \wordexample{detrimental to our interests（有损我们利益的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{detritus}{di'traitəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRI, TRIT}
  \wordsense{n.【地】岩屑（岩石因风化作用掉下来的石粒或碎屑），碎石 *de (= away, intensive)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{trite}{trait}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRI, TRIT}
  \wordsense{a.（话、想法、意见等）陈腐的；无新意的}
\end{word}

\chapter{DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 dot, don 意为 to give。dow, dos 是其变形。此外，与此相似的还有 dat 及其变形 dit。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{anecdote}{'ænikdəut}
  \wordbreakdown{不向外给予 an (= not) + ec<ex (= out) + dot (= give)}
  \wordsense{n. 轶事，奇闻；（pl.）秘史}
  \wordexample{Many public speakers begin their address with an amusing anecdote.（许多演说家都是以趣闻轶事开始演说的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{condone}{kən'dəun}
  \wordbreakdown{完全给予 con<com (= intensive, completely) + don (= give)}
  \wordsense{vt. 原谅，宽恕；不咎（过错）}
  \wordexample{condonation 宽恕；不咎（过错）}
  \wordexample{In some countries, spousal physical abuse is condoned.（有些国家容许对配偶身体的虐待行为。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{endow}{in'dau}
  \wordbreakdown{向里边给予 en (= in) + dow<don (= give)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + with + sth（向医院、学校等）捐赠；（天生）赋有（某种资质）}
  \wordexample{endowment 捐赠，捐助（款）；（pl.）天赋，天资}
  \wordexample{The business tycoon endowed a professorship in the university's business school.（那位商业大亨向该大学商学院捐赠了教授基金。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{render}{'rendə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{还给 ren<re (= back) + der<dit (= give)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使……成为；以……回报；提出，提供，交付；演出，演奏；翻译；表达，描写}
  \wordphrase{rendition 翻译；演出，演奏 rendering 翻译；演出，演奏}
  \wordexample{A tranquilizer dart rendered the lion harmless.（狮子中了麻醉针剂后就不会伤人了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{antidote}{'æntidəut}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 解毒剂；矫正方法 *anti (= against)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{donate}{dəu'neit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 捐赠}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{donation}{dəu'neiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 捐献，赠与；捐款；捐赠品}
  \wordexample{donations to the Red Cross（给红十字会的捐款）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{donor}{'dəunə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 捐赠者}
  \wordexample{blood donor（献血者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{pardon}{'pɑ:dn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{v./n. 原谅，宽恕；赦免 *par<per (= completely)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dowager}{'dauədʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 受有亡夫爵位（或遗产）的寡妇；老年贵妇人}
  \wordexample{dowager duchess（公爵遗孀） queen dowager（国王遗孀） Empress Dowager Hsi（（中国清朝的）西太后）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dower}{'dauə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（寡妇应分得的）亡夫遗产；嫁妆；天赋，天资}
  \wordsense{v. 给……亡夫遗产；赋予（才能等）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dowry}{'dauri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 嫁妆}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{dose}{dəus}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（药物等的）（一次）剂量，一服；《口》（尤指苦事的）一次，一番；《俚》花柳病}
  \wordsense{v.（按剂量）给……服药}
  \wordexample{six doses（服用六次的药量） give him a dose（给他服一剂药）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dosage}{'dəusidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（药的）剂量；（按剂量的）给药}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{antedate}{ˌænti'deit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 倒填（在信件、文件等的）日期；（时间上）先发生 *ante (= before)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{postdate}{ˌpəust'deit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{v.（在信件、文件等上）写上比实际日期晚的日期；（时间上）后于 *post (= after)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{predate}{pri:'deit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{v. (= antedate) *pre (= before)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{data}{'deitə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 资料，材料；数据}
  \wordexample{data bank（数据库） data processing（数据处理）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{date}{deit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 日期；（尤指与异性的）约会；时代，年代；约会对象；海枣；枣椰树}
  \wordsense{v. 在……上注明日期；源自；（\textasciitilde{} from / back to）追溯至……；与……约会}
  \wordexample{dated（过时的） datable（可确定年代的） dateless（无期限的；古老得无法确定日期的） dateline（国际日期变更线） blind date（不相识的男女初次会面）}
  \wordexample{The castle dates back to the 14th century.（那座城堡早在 14 世纪就建成了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{dative}{'deitiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{a.【语】与格的}
  \wordsense{n. 与格}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{edit}{'edit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{v. 编辑 *e<ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{edition}{i'diʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n. 版本 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{cheap edition（廉价版） pocket edition（袖珍本） first edition（初版） revised edition（修订版） popular edition（普及版） deluxe edition（精装版）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{editor}{'editə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOT, DON[DOW, DOS], DAT[DIT]}
  \wordsense{n.（书、报纸、期刊、广播节目等的）编辑；（报刊的）主编；（报纸专栏）主笔 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordexample{chief editor / editor-in-chief（总编辑，主编） city editor（本地新闻主编） financial editor（财经编辑） managing editor（总编辑）}
\end{word}

\chapter{ONYM[ONOM]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自希腊语的 onym 意为 name, word。onom 是其变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{anonymous}{ə'nɒniməs}
  \wordbreakdown{没有姓名的 an (= not, without) + onym (= name, word)}
  \wordsense{a. 作者未详的；匿名的，不具名的}
  \wordphrase{anonymity 匿名；作者不详}
  \wordexample{The funds for the new library were given by an anonymous donor.（新图书馆的建设资金是一位匿名捐助者赞助的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{pseudonym}{'sju:dənim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{n. 假名；（著者的）雅号，笔名 *pseud<pseudo (= false), onym (= name)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{antonym}{'æntənim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{n. 反义词 *ant<anti (= against, opposite), onym (= word)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{synonym}{'sinənim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{n. 同义词 *syn (= alike, together), onym (= word)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{synonymous}{si'nɒniməs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{a. 同义词的，同义的 *syn (= alike, together), onym (= word)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{homonym}{'hɒmənim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{n. 同音异义词 *hom<homo (= same), onym (= word)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{heteronym}{'hetərənim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{n. 同形异音异义词 *heter<hetero (= other), onym (= word)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{metonymy}{me'tɒnimi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{n.【语】转喻（如用 crown 代替 king） *met<meta (= change), onym (= word)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{acronym}{'ækrənim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{n. 首字母缩略词 *acr<acro (= high), onym (= word)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{onomatopoeia}{ˌɒnəˌmætə'pi:ə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ONYM[ONOM]}
  \wordsense{n.【语】拟声词 *onom<onym (= word), poeia (= make)}
\end{word}

\chapter{BYS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自希腊语的 bys 意为 end, bottom。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 fin, termin, ultim 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{abyss}{ə'bis}
  \wordbreakdown{没有尽头 a<an (= not, without) + bys (= bottom, end)}
  \wordsense{n. 深渊；地狱}
  \wordexample{The abyss of space is infinite so far as we know.（就我们所知，宇宙是无限深邃的深渊。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{abysmal}{ə'bizməl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BYS}
  \wordsense{a. 深渊的，深不可测的 *a<an (= not, without)}
  \wordexample{abysmal ignorance（极度的无知） abysmal night（深夜）}
\end{word}

\chapter{ARCH}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自希腊语的 arch 意为 government, to rule。arch 还有 chief, first, old 的含义。同义词根有来自希腊语的 cracy / crat 和来自拉丁语的 reg。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{anarchy}{'ænəki}
  \wordbreakdown{无政府状态 an (= not, without) + arch (= government)}
  \wordsense{n. 无政府状态；混乱，无秩序}
  \wordphrase{anarchism 无政府主义 anarchist 无政府主义者}
  \wordexample{Without a federal government a nation devolves into anarchy.（若没有联邦政府，国家就会陷入无政府状态。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{autarchy}{'ɔ:tɑ:ki}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 独裁，专制 *aut<auto (= self), arch (= rule)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{autarky}{'ɔ:tɑ:ki}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 国家经济的自给自足 *aut<auto (= self), ark<arch (= rule)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{diarchy}{'daiɑ:ki}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 两头政治 *di (= two), arch (= rule)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{matriarch}{'meitriɑ:k}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 女家长，女族长；女统治者；女负责人 *matri (= mother), arch (= rule)}
  \wordexample{matriarchy（母系氏族制）}
  \wordexample{matriarchal（母系氏族制的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{monarchy}{'mɒnəki}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 君主政治，君主制；君主国 *mon<mono (= one), arch (= rule)}
  \wordexample{monarchism（君主制，君主主义） monarchist（君主主义者）}
  \wordexample{absolute / despotic monarchy（专制君主制） constitutional / limited monarchy（君主立宪制（政体））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{monarch}{'mɒnək}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 君主 *mon<mono (= one), arch (= rule)}
  \wordexample{monarchic（君主的，君主政体的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{oligarchy}{'ɒligɑ:ki}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 寡头政治（→ polyarchy 多头政治）；寡头统治的国家 *olig (= few), arch (= rule)}
  \wordexample{oligarch（寡头统治集团成员）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{patriarch}{'peitriɑ:k}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 家长，族长；元老，长老；最年长者 *patri (= father), arch (= rule)}
  \wordexample{patriarchy（父权制）}
  \wordexample{patriarchal（家长的，族长的；德高望重的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{archangel}{'ɑ:keindʒəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 大天使 *arch (= chief)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{archbishop}{ɑ:tʃ'biʃəp}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 大主教，主教长 *arch (= chief)}
  \wordexample{archbishopric（大主教的职务、辖区、管辖权）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{archdeacon}{ɑ:tʃ'di:kən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 英国国教的会吏总监 *arch (= chief)}
  \wordexample{archdeaconry（会吏总监的职位、住宅）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{archdiocese}{ˌɑ:tʃ'daiəsis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 大主教（管辖）区 *arch (= chief), di<dia (= through), oce<eco (= house)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{archenemy}{ˌɑ:tʃ'enəmi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 不共戴天的敌人，主要敌人 *arch (= chief)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{archetype}{'ɑ:kitaip}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 原型 *arch (= chief)}
  \wordexample{archetypal（原型）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{archipelago}{ˌɑ:ki'peləgəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 群岛；有很多岛屿的海；（the A\textasciitilde{}）爱琴海 *arch (= chief), pelago (= sea)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{architect}{'ɑ:kitekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 设计师；建筑师；《喻》缔造者 *arch (= chief), tect (= build, carpenter)}
  \wordexample{naval architect（造船工程师） the (Great) Architect（造物主） the architect of one's own fortunes（自我命运的创造者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{architecture}{'ɑ:kitektʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 建筑术；建筑学；建筑风格；结构，构造 *arch (= chief), tect (= build, carpenter)}
  \wordexample{Romanesque architecture（罗马式的建筑风格） architecture of a novel（小说的结构）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{architectural}{ˌɑ:ki'tektʃərəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{a. 建筑学的；建筑术的；建筑的 *arch (= chief), tect (= build, carpenter)}
  \wordexample{the architectural beauties of a city（城市的建筑美） architectural engineer（建筑工程师）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{archaeology}{ˌɑ:ki'ɒlədʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 考古学 *arch (= old), ology (= science)}
  \wordexample{archaeological（考古学的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{archaeologist}{ˌɑ:ki'ɒlədʒist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n. 考古学者 *arch (= old)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{archaic}{ɑ:'keiik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{a. 古老的；古式的；古体的 *arch (= old)}
  \wordexample{archaism（古语，古体；古风）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{archives}{'ɑ:kaivz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARCH}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）档案室，案卷保管处；档案 *arch (= old)}
  \wordexample{archivist（档案保管员）}
\end{word}

\chapter{PATH, PAT, PASS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自希腊语的 path 意为 to feel, to suffer。来自拉丁语的 pat, pass 与其形态相似，含义相同。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 sent / sens 和来自希腊语的 (a)esthes / (a)esthet。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{apathy}{'æpəθi}
  \wordbreakdown{没有感觉 a<an (= not, without) + path (= feel, suffer)}
  \wordsense{n. 冷淡；漠不关心}
  \wordphrase{apathetic 冷淡的，漠不关心的}
  \wordexample{Many teachers complain of the apathy their students exhibit.（许多教师都对学生们表现出的冷漠表示不满。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{antipathy}{æn'tipəθi}
  \wordbreakdown{反对的感情 anti (= against, opposite) + path (= feel)}
  \wordsense{n.（强烈的）反感；引起反感的事物}
  \wordphrase{antipathetic 生来厌恶的；本性不相容的}
  \wordexample{The Vietnamese immigrants were victims of racial antipathy.（越南移民是种族反感的牺牲品。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{empathy}{'empəθi}
  \wordbreakdown{向里边吹入情感 em<en (= in) + path (= feel, suffer)}
  \wordsense{n. 共鸣；移情；同情}
  \wordexample{I feel a lot of empathy for her plight as a poor single mother.（我非常同情她这样一个贫穷的单身母亲遭遇的困境。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{compassion}{kəm'pæʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{一起感受痛苦 com (= together) + pass (= feel, suffer)}
  \wordsense{n. 怜悯，同情}
  \wordphrase{compassionate 慈悲的；有同情心的}
  \wordexample{It's impossible to see images of starving children on television without compassion.（看到电视上那些饥饿的孩子，你不可能不产生同情心。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{compatible}{kəm'pætəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{一起想的 com (= together) + pat (= feel, suffer)}
  \wordsense{a. 一致的；可并立的；适合的}
  \wordphrase{compatibility 一致；适合性}
  \wordphrase{compatibly 协调地；适合地}
  \wordexample{My wife and I don't have compatible tastes in food.（我妻子和我的饮食口味不一致。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{dispassionate}{dis'pæʃənət}
  \wordbreakdown{不偏重感情的 dis (= not) + pass (= feel, suffer)}
  \wordsense{a. 冷静的；不偏袒的，公平的}
  \wordphrase{dispassion 冷静；公平}
  \wordphrase{dispassionately 冷静地；公平地}
  \wordexample{Ideally, a judge will be dispassionate in his rulings.（理想地来说，法官在作出判决时应冷静客观。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{impassive}{im'pæsiv}
  \wordbreakdown{无感情的 im<in (= not) + pass (= feel, suffer)}
  \wordsense{a. 无感情的；无感觉的}
  \wordphrase{impassivity 冷淡；无知觉}
  \wordphrase{impassively 无感情地}
  \wordexample{The policeman remained impassive as I tried to talk myself out of the ticket.（我试图说服警察不给我开罚单，但他一点儿也不为我所动。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sympathy}{'simpəθi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 同情，怜悯；赞同；支持 *sym<syn (= together)}
  \wordexample{a man of wide sympathies（富有同情心的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sympathetic}{ˌsimpə'θetik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 同情的，有同情心的；赞同的；支持的 *sym<syn (= together)}
  \wordexample{sympathetic words（同情的话语） sympathetic mind（同情心） sympathetic strike（（声援其他罢工的）同情罢工） sympathetic audience（表示支持的听众）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sympathize}{'simpəθaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{v. 同情；支持 *sym<syn (= together)}
  \wordexample{sympathizer（同情者，（某种主义、政党等的）支持者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{telepathy}{ti'lepəθi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 心灵感应；传心术 *tele (= far)}
  \wordexample{telepathic（心灵感应的；传心术的）}
  \wordexample{telepathist（传心术学者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{homeopathy}{ˌhəumi'ɒpəθi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 顺势疗法（一种疾病治疗体系，以小剂量运用一种药物为基础，这种药物的大量服用可在健康人体内产生一种类似于疾病的症状） *homeo<homo (= same)}
  \wordexample{homeopath（顺势疗法医师）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{psychopath}{'saikəpæθ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 精神病患者 *psycho (= mind)}
  \wordexample{psychopathic（精神病的，患有精神病的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pathetic}{pə'θetik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 可怜的；情感的}
  \wordexample{pathetic fallacy（感情误置（通过想像赋予无生命物以生命或人的情感）） pathetic story（悲伤的故事） pathetic scene（哀伤的场面） pathetic ignorance（可怜的无知）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pathos}{'peiθɒs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n.（言语、作品中）引起怜悯的因素；怜悯}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pathology}{pə'θɒlədʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 病理学 *ology (= science)}
  \wordexample{pathologist（病理学者）}
  \wordexample{pathological（病理学的；病态的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{incompatible}{ˌinkəm'pætəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 不能同时成立的，不相容的，矛盾的 *in (= not), com (= together)}
  \wordexample{incompatibility（不相容）}
  \wordexample{sexually incompatible（性生活不和谐的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{impassioned}{im'pæʃnd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 充满激情的；激烈的 *im<in (= into)}
  \wordexample{impassioned speech（慷慨激昂的演讲）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{impatient}{im'peiʃnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 没有耐心的，焦急的，性急的 *im<in (= not)}
  \wordexample{impatiently（性急地，焦急地，焦躁地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{impatience}{im'peiʃns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 急躁，无耐心；焦急，焦躁 *im<in (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{passion}{'pæʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 热情；（爱情、憎恶、愤怒等）强烈情感；激情；情欲；（the P\textasciitilde{}）耶稣的受难记}
  \wordexample{passionless（缺乏热情的；冷静的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{passionate}{'pæʃənət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 热情的；热烈的}
  \wordexample{passionately（热情地）}
  \wordexample{passionate nature（热情的天性） passionate advocate of democracy（民主主义的强烈拥护者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{passive}{'pæsiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 被动的，消极的；顺从的}
  \wordexample{passivity（被动，消极（性）；不抵抗）}
  \wordexample{passive obedience（无条件服从） passive resistance（消极的抵抗） passive resister（消极的抵抗者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{patience}{'peiʃns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 耐心；忍耐（力）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{patient}{'peiʃnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PATH, PAT, PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 有耐心的；有忍耐力的}
  \wordexample{patiently（有耐心地，有毅力地）}
\end{word}

\chapter{MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自希腊语的 mne, mnes 意为 to remember。此外，来自拉丁语的 mem(or) 也有相似的含义。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{amnesty}{'æmnəsti}
  \wordbreakdown{不记得犯过什么罪 a<an (= not, without) + mne (= remember)}
  \wordsense{n. 特赦，大赦}
  \wordexample{Vietnam War draft dodgers were granted an amnesty by President Carter.（卡特总统赦免了越南战争期间逃避兵役的人。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{amnesia}{æm'ni:zjə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n. 遗忘（症），记忆缺失 *a<an (= without, not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{mnemonic}{ni:'mɒnik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{a. 有助于记忆的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{mnemonics}{ni:'mɒniks}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n. 记忆术}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{commemorate}{kə'meməreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{v. 纪念（人、事件）；（物品等）成为……的纪念 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordexample{monument to commemorate the victory（纪念胜利的纪念碑）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{commemoration}{kəˌmemə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n. 纪念；纪念仪式 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordexample{in commemoration of（纪念……）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{commemorative}{kə'memərətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{a. 纪念的 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordexample{commemorative stamps / medals（纪念邮票 / 纪念奖章）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{memento}{mə'mentəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n. 纪念物，引起回忆的东西}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{memo}{'meməu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n.（memorandum 的简略形式）备忘录，便笺}
  \wordsense{v. 记录，向……提交备忘录}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{memoir}{'memwɑ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n. 传记，传略；（pl.）自传，回忆录}
  \wordexample{war memoirs（战争回忆录）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{memorable}{'memərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{a. 值得怀念的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{memorandum}{ˌmemə'rændəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n. 备忘录，便笺；外交备忘录；【商】代销委托书；【律】交易备忘录}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{memorial}{mə'mɔ:riəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n. 纪念碑；纪念馆；纪念物；（pl.）（历史的）记录；编年史}
  \wordsense{a. 纪念的；追悼的}
  \wordexample{memorialize（纪念；向……请愿）}
  \wordexample{Lincoln Memorial（林肯纪念馆） memorial to the dead（纪念死者的纪念碑） war memorial（战争纪念碑） Memorial Day（《韩》显忠日；《美》阵亡将士纪念日） memorial park（陵园） memorial tablet（（为追悼死者而挂在教会墙上的）纪念匾） memorial service（追悼仪式）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{memorize}{'meməraiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{v. 背诵，记忆}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{memory}{'meməri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MNE, MNES, MEM(OR)}
  \wordsense{n. 记忆，记忆力；回忆，回想}
  \wordexample{have a good / bad (poor) memory（记忆力好 / 坏） memories of childhood（童年的回忆） memory bank（存储体，数据总库）}
\end{word}

\chapter{MORPH}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自希腊语的 morph 意为 form。同义词根为 form。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{amorphous}{ə'mɔ:fəs}
  \wordbreakdown{没有形状的 a<an (= not, without) + morph (= form)}
  \wordsense{a. 无定形的；无一定方向（或目的）的；无组织的}
  \wordexample{A nebula (exploding star) often leaves an amorphous cloud of dust and gas.（星云（爆炸的恒星）往往留下由灰尘和气体组成的不定形云体。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{anthropomorphism}{ˌænθrəpə'mɔ:fizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORPH}
  \wordsense{n. 拟人论，拟人观 *anthrop (= man)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{metamorphose}{ˌmetə'mɔ:fəuz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORPH}
  \wordsense{v.（使）变形；【地】（使）变质 *meta (= change)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{metamorphosis}{ˌmetə'mɔ:fəsis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORPH}
  \wordsense{n. 变形；质变；【生】变态；（外貌、性格或环境等的）彻底（或显著）的变化 *meta (= change)}
  \wordexample{the metamorphosis in the life of an insect（昆虫的变态） the social metamorphosis that has occurred in South Korea（在韩国发生的显著的社会变化）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{morpheme}{'mɔ:fi:m}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORPH}
  \wordsense{n.【语】语素，词素}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{morphology}{mɔ:'fɒlədʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORPH}
  \wordsense{n.【生】形态学；【语】形态学；形态}
\end{word}

\chapter{TROPH}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 troph 意为 to nourish。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 nutri, al。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{atrophy}{'ætrəfi}
  \wordbreakdown{没有营养 a<an (= not, without) + troph (= nourishment)}
  \wordsense{n. 萎缩；衰退}
  \wordsense{v.（使）萎缩}
  \wordexample{Quadriplegics often experience muscular atrophy after their paralysis.（四肢瘫痪者在瘫痪以后肌肉往往也会萎缩。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{dystrophy}{'distrəfi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TROPH}
  \wordsense{n. 营养失调 *dys (= bad, badly)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{hypertrophy}{hai'pə:trəfi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TROPH}
  \wordsense{n.（因营养过剩等而引起的）【生】肥大 *hyper (= excessive)}
\end{word}

\chapter{GNO[GN, N]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 gno 意为 to know。其变形为 gn, n。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 not, sci。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{agnostic}{æg'nɒstik}
  \wordbreakdown{认为无法了解神是否存在的人 a<an (= not) + gno (= know)}
  \wordsense{n. 不可知论者}
  \wordsense{a. 不可知论的}
  \wordphrase{agnosticism 不可知论}
  \wordexample{Many currently religious people were once agnostics.（现在有宗教信仰的人中有很多曾经是不可知论者。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{ignoble}{ig'nəubl}
  \wordbreakdown{无法了解的 i<in (= not) + gno (= know)}
  \wordsense{a. 卑劣的，不光彩的；（出身或身份）卑微的}
  \wordphrase{ignobly 卑贱地；卑鄙地}
  \wordexample{The Taliban in Afghanistan met an ignoble end at the hands of the USA.（阿富汗塔利班组织在美国手里落了个不光彩的下场。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{ignominious}{ˌignə'miniəs}
  \wordbreakdown{无法了解的 i<in (= not) + gno (= know)}
  \wordsense{a. 可耻的；不光彩的}
  \wordphrase{ignominy 耻辱}
  \wordexample{I grew up in an ignominious hovel in Haiti.（我在海地的一间简陋的小屋里长大。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{cognizant}{'kɒgnizənt}
  \wordbreakdown{完全知道的 co<com (= thoroughly) + gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordsense{a. 认识到的}
  \wordphrase{cognizance 察觉；认识}
  \wordexample{The mayor was cognizant of police corruption, but did nothing.（市长明知警察腐败，却没有采取任何措施。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{incognito}{ˌinkɒg'ni:təu}
  \wordbreakdown{使完全不知道 in (= not) + co<com (= thoroughly) + gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordsense{a. 隐姓埋名的，化名的；秘密的}
  \wordsense{ad. 隐姓埋名地；化名地}
  \wordexample{Famous movie stars often travel incognito on their vacations.（电影明星们在休假时常常化名旅行。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{connoisseur}{ˌkɒnə'sɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{完全知道的人 con<com (= thoroughly) + n<gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordsense{n. 鉴定人，鉴赏家；专家}
  \wordexample{connoisseurship 鉴赏力；鉴赏家（身份）}
  \wordexample{As a certified chocoholic, I consider myself a connoisseur of chocolate.（大家都知道我酷爱巧克力，我认为自己是个巧克力专家。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{reconnaissance}{ri'kɒnisəns}
  \wordbreakdown{再一次完整地知道 re (= again) + con<com (= thoroughly) + n<gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordsense{n. 侦查；勘察，事先考察}
  \wordphrase{reconnoiter 侦查；勘察}
  \wordexample{I served with a reconnaissance squadron in the Vietnam War.（越战期间我在侦查队服役。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{prognosticate}{prɒg'nɒstikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{事先、提前知道 pro (= before) + gno (= know)}
  \wordsense{vt. 预言，预知，预测}
  \wordexample{prognosis 预知；【医】预后（对疾病发展状况的预见） prognostication 预测，预言，预知；前兆，征候 prognosticator 预言家，预报者，占卜师}
  \wordexample{prognostic 预后的；表现出征兆的；（n.）前兆；预测；预后}
  \wordexample{The meteorologists are prognosticating the hurricane's arrival for tomorrow morning.（气象学家预测明天早晨台风会到达。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{diagnose}{'daiəgnəuz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{v. 诊断疾病 *dia (= through)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{diagnosis}{ˌdaiəg'nəusis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{n. 诊断；诊断的结果，诊断书 *dia (= through)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{diagnostic}{ˌdaiəg'nɒstik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{a. 诊断的 *dia (= through)}
  \wordexample{diagnostic test（诊断测试） symptoms that were of little diagnostic value（对诊断没什么帮助的症状）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{physiognomy}{ˌfizi'ɒnəmi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{n. 观相术；面相；外貌，地貌 *physi (= nature)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ignorance}{'ignərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{n. 无知，不学无术 *i<in (= not)}
  \wordexample{Ignorance is bliss.（无知便是福。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ignorant}{'ignərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{a. 无知的，不学无术的；不知道的 *i<in (= not)}
  \wordexample{ignorantly（无知地；不知道地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ignore}{ig'nɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{v.（有意地）忽视，不理会；【律】（因证据不足而）驳回诉讼 *i<in (= not)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{cognition}{kɒg'niʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{n. 认识，认知；知识 *co<com (= together), gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordexample{cognitive（认识的，认知的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{cognomen}{kɒg'nəumen}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{n. 姓；绰号 *co<com (= together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{precognition}{ˌpri:kɒg'niʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{n. 预知，先知 *pre (= before), co<com (= together), gn<gno (= know)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{recognize}{'rekəgnaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{v. 认出，看出；认可，承认 *re (= again), co<com (= together), gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordexample{recognizable（可认可的，可承认的；可辨认出的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{recognition}{ˌrekəg'niʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{n. 认可，承认；认识；报偿 *re (= again), co<com (= together), gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordexample{receive recognition（得到（世人）认可） in recognition of his services（给他的服务以报酬） recognition of the new state（承认这个新国家）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{recognizance}{ri'kɒgnizəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GNO[GN, N]}
  \wordsense{n.【律】具结；保证金 *re (= again), co<com (= together), gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordexample{enter into recognizances（具结）}
\end{word}

\chapter{(A)ESTHET, (A)ESTHES}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自希腊语的 (a)esthet, (a)esthes 意为 to feel。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 sent / sens, pat / pass 和来自希腊语的 path。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{anesthesiologist}{ˌænəsθizi'ɒlədʒist}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得没有感觉的人 an (= not, without) + esthes (= feel)}
  \wordsense{n. 麻醉师}
  \wordsense{n. 唯美主义者，审美家 (a)esthetic** [i:s'θetik]}
  \wordsense{a. 审美的；（人）有审美感的}
  \wordsense{n. 美学}
  \wordphrase{anesthetize 使麻醉}
  \wordphrase{anesthesia 麻醉 anesthetist 麻醉师 anesthesiology 麻醉学}
  \wordphrase{(a)esthetical (=(a)esthetic)}
  \wordexample{anesthetic 麻醉的；（n.）麻醉剂}
  \wordexample{Anesthesiologists prevent surgical patients from feeling discomfort.（麻醉科医生使手术患者避免感到疼痛。） (a)esthete* ['i:sθi:t]}
  \wordexample{aesthetic person（有审美眼光的人） aesthetic standards（审美标准） (a)esthetics* [i:s'θetiks]}
  \wordexample{(a)estheticism（唯美主义）}
\end{word}

\chapter{MENT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ment 意为 mind。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{commentary}{ˈkɒməntri}
  \wordbreakdown{带有意见 com (= with) + ment (= mind)}
  \wordsense{n. 评论，批评；意见，见解；注释}
  \wordphrase{comment 注释，给……做注解；评论，提出意见}
  \wordphrase{commentator 注释者；时事评论者；实况播音员}
  \wordexample{After reading the book, please give your commentary to the class.（读完这本书之后，跟同学们谈谈你的见解。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{demented}{diˈmentid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MENT}
  \wordsense{a. 疯狂的，狂乱的 *de (= away)}
  \wordexample{poor, demented creature（可怜又疯狂的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{mental}{ˈmentl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MENT}
  \wordsense{a. 内心的，精神的；精神病的}
  \wordexample{mental age（智力年龄） mental arithmetic（心算） mental deficiency（智力缺陷）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{mentally}{ˈmentəli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MENT}
  \wordsense{ad. 精神地，内心地}
  \wordexample{mentally deficient / defective（智力缺陷的） mentally deranged（疯狂的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{mentality}{menˈtæləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MENT}
  \wordsense{n. 智力；精神；心理}
  \wordexample{persons of average mentality（智力一般的人） war mentality（战争心理） good-guy-bad-guy mentality（认为世上只存在善恶之分而不存在半善半恶的思考方式）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{mentor}{ˈmentɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MENT}
  \wordsense{n. 私人教师；良师益友；（M\textasciitilde{}）奥德修斯（Odysseus）儿子的良师}
\end{word}

\chapter{PACT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 pact 意为 to fasten, to agree。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 fix。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{compact}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PACT}
  \wordsense{[用力抽绷、勒紧 com (= intensive) + pact (= fasten, agree)]}
  \wordexample{[kəmˈpækt] a.（汽车）小型的；（房子）小的；紧凑的；简洁的}
  \wordexample{[kəmˈpækt] vt. 压紧；塞满}
  \wordexample{[ˈkɒmpækt] n. 契约；小粉盒}
  \wordexample{It's a compact but comfortable studio apartment.（这是一处小而舒适的一室公寓。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{impact}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PACT}
  \wordsense{[使劲儿系紧、抽紧 im<in (= intensive) + pact (= fasten, agree)]}
  \wordexample{[ˈimpækt] n. 影响；冲突，冲击}
  \wordexample{[imˈpækt] vt. 填满；冲击}
  \wordexample{The appeal had such an impact on me that I contributed 100 dollars.（该呼吁给我以强烈的冲击，我捐献了100美元。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pact}{pækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PACT}
  \wordsense{n. 契约，协定，条约}
  \wordexample{nonaggression pact（不侵犯条约） the Pact of Locarno（洛迦诺公约）}
\end{word}

\chapter{PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ple, plet, pli, ply, plen 意为 to fill, full。此外，意为 to fold 的 plic, ply, pli, plex, plo 等与这些词根形态相似，注意不要混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{compliant}{kəmˈplaiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{完全满足对方的心情的 com (= thoroughly) + pli (= fill)}
  \wordsense{a. 服从的，顺从的}
  \wordphrase{compliance / compliancy 顺从}
  \wordphrase{comply 依从，顺从}
  \wordexample{She has quite a compliant personality.（她的个性很温顺。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{complement}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{[完全添满 com (= intensive) + ple (= fill)]}
  \wordphrase{complementary 补充的}
  \wordexample{[ˈkɒmplimənt] n. 补充（物）；【语】补语}
  \wordexample{[ˈkɒmpliment] vt. 补充，补足}
  \wordexample{The belief that white wine invariably complements seafood is outdated.（认为吃海鲜总是要配上白葡萄酒的想法已经过时了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{implement}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{[把……里面填满 im<in (= in) + ple (= fill)]}
  \wordphrase{implementation 履行；实行}
  \wordexample{[ˈimplimənt] n. 工具；手段}
  \wordexample{[ˈimpliment] vt. 履行（协定、约定等）；实行}
  \wordexample{The plan will be implemented in stages, starting next week.（此计划下周开始分阶段实行。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{supplement}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{[在下面填满 sup<sub (= under) + ple (= fill)]}
  \wordphrase{supplementary 补充的，追加的}
  \wordexample{[ˈsʌplimənt] n. 补充，增补；补充物；附录；增刊}
  \wordexample{[ˈsʌpliment] vt. sth + with + sth 补充}
  \wordexample{Most breakfast cereals contain a host of vitamin and mineral supplements.（大部分的早餐谷物食品中都含有大量的维生素和矿物质添加剂。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deplete}{diˈpli:t}
  \wordbreakdown{对“装满”的轻微否定 de (= reverse) + plet (= fill)}
  \wordsense{vt. 用尽；使枯竭}
  \wordphrase{depletion 消耗；枯竭}
  \wordexample{Some cancer medications severely deplete calcium in bones.（一些治疗癌症的药物会使骨骼中钙的含量迅速减少。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{replete}{riˈpli:t}
  \wordbreakdown{完全填满的 re (= intensive) + plet (= fill)}
  \wordsense{a. 充满的；充实的；饱食的}
  \wordphrase{repletion 充满；饱食}
  \wordexample{The shelter gave the men a Christmas dinner replete with all the trimmings.（那个避难所用各种食物拼配而成的丰盛的圣诞晚餐招待了那些人。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{replenish}{riˈpleniʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{重新填满 re (= again) + plen (= fill)}
  \wordsense{vt. 再装满；重新补充}
  \wordexample{It's your responsibility to inventory and replenish supplies.（你的责任是盘点和补充库存。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{plenary}{ˈpli:nəri}
  \wordbreakdown{与填充的事物相关的 plen (= fill)}
  \wordsense{a. 完全的，充分的；绝对的；（会议）全体出席的}
  \wordphrase{plenarily 完全地}
  \wordexample{A vote was held at the plenary session of the city council.（在市议会的全体会议上进行了投票。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{compliment}{ˈkɒmplimənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{n. 称赞，赞扬；敬意；表示敬意；（pl.）祝词}
  \wordsense{v. [ˈkɒmpliment] 恭维，称赞；向……赠送（某物）以表示敬意 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordexample{pay a compliment（称赞） give / present / send my compliments to your father（向你父亲致意） With the compliments of the season（祝贺佳节〈圣诞节、新年等节日时的问候语〉） With the author's compliments（著者谨赠，惠存） compliment her on her new snowboard（称赞她的新滑雪板） compliment her with Vocabulary Workshop（向她赠送 Vocabulary Workshop 一书）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{complimentary}{ˌkɒmpliˈmentri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{a. 称赞的，赞美的；表示敬意的；免费赠送的 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordexample{complimentary address（祝词） complimentary copy / ticket / dinner（赠送本 / 优待券、招待券 / （飞机上为顾客提供的）免费餐）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{supply}{səˈplai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{v. 供给（所需或所要求的物品），提供；满足（需求）}
  \wordsense{n. 供给，供应；供应品；军粮补给品；储备物资；（个人的）生活费；代课教师 *sup<sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{supplier（物资等的）供应者}
  \wordexample{supply and demand（供求） supplies in our reservoir（我们水库里的储备水） medical supplies（医药用品） supply teacher（代课教师）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{complete}{kəmˈpli:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{a. 完全的，全部的，完整的；彻底的}
  \wordsense{v. 结束，完成；使完整 *com (= together)}
  \wordexample{completely（完全地，彻底地）}
  \wordexample{complete failure（彻底失败） complete stranger（完全不认识的人） complete works of Shakespeare（莎士比亚全集） complete the puzzle（做填字游戏）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{completion}{kəmˈpli:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{n. 完成，结束；（目的的）达成；【经】（销售契约手续的）正式完成 *com (= together)}
  \wordexample{on completion of contract（完成合同手续后） sentence completion（完成句子）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{expletive}{ikˈspli:tiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{a. 附加的，填补的 *ex (= out)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{plethora}{ˈpleθərə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{n. 过多，过剩；【医】多血症}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{plenipotentiary}{ˌplenipəˈtenʃəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{n. 全权大使}
  \wordsense{a. 有全权的 *pot (= capable of, powerful)}
  \wordexample{ambassador extraordinary and plenipotentiary（特命全权大使） minister plenipotentiary（全权公使）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{plenitude}{ˈplenitju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLE, PLET, PLI, PLY, PLEN}
  \wordsense{n. 充分；丰富}
  \wordexample{in the plenitude of his powers（在他权力鼎盛之时）}
\end{word}

\chapter{PRESS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 press 意为 to press。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{compress}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{[一起按 com (= together) + press (= press)]}
  \wordphrase{compression 压缩 compressor 压缩机}
  \wordphrase{compressed 压缩的 compressible 可压缩的}
  \wordexample{[ˈkɒmpres] n. 湿敷布，压布}
  \wordexample{[kəmˈpres] vt. 压缩；压紧；概括}
  \wordexample{You can compress a flower between the pages of a book.（可以把花夹压在书页中间。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{expressly}{ikˈspresli}
  \wordbreakdown{挤压出外边 ex (= out) + press (= press)}
  \wordsense{ad. 明白地，清楚地；特别地，特意地}
  \wordphrase{expression 表达，措辞；表情 expressionism 表现主义}
  \wordphrase{expressive 表现的 expressionless 无表情的}
  \wordexample{express 表达，表示；快递；（a.）明白的，明示的；快速的；（n.）快车；快运；快递公司}
  \wordexample{The web site was expressly created to support recently widowed women.（那个网站是为支持那些刚刚失去丈夫的女性们特别制作的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{oppress}{əˈpres}
  \wordbreakdown{逆着某物压 op<ob (= against, intensive) + press (= press)}
  \wordsense{vt. 压迫，压制；使（心情）沉重}
  \wordphrase{oppression 压制，压抑 oppressor 压制者}
  \wordphrase{oppressive 压制的，压迫的；烦闷的}
  \wordexample{Organized religion is often used to oppress people, in my view.（依我看，组织性的宗教往往被用来压制人民。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{repress}{riˈpres}
  \wordbreakdown{向后按压 re (= back) + press (= press)}
  \wordsense{vt. 压抑，抑制；镇压}
  \wordphrase{repression 压抑；镇压}
  \wordphrase{repressive 压抑的 repressed 被压抑的}
  \wordexample{I've tried to repress my memories of being raped, but without success.（我努力压抑着那段被强奸的记忆，不过却没什么用。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suppress}{səˈpres}
  \wordbreakdown{向下压 sup<sub (= under) + press (= press)}
  \wordsense{vt. 镇压，压制；隐蔽，隐藏；抑制}
  \wordphrase{suppression 镇压，压制；隐藏；抑制 suppressor 压制者，镇压者}
  \wordphrase{suppressive 压制的，镇压的}
  \wordexample{She was struggling to suppress her sobs.（她拼命不让自己哭出声。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{depress}{diˈpres}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{v. 压下；使沮丧，使忧郁；使萧条 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{depressed classes（印度的最下层人民） depressed area（不景气地区）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{depression}{diˈpreʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{n. 意志消沉，忧郁；坑，洼地；萧条，不景气；低气压 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{Great Depression（大萧条） in a slight depression（在较浅的低洼处） depression over Iceland（冰岛上空的低气压）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{depressive}{diˈpresiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{a. 压下的；抑郁的，忧郁的}
  \wordsense{n. 经常忧郁、沮丧、消沉的人 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{depressive financial measures（为抑制贸易活动而采取的财政措施） depressive fit（忧郁症）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{impress}{imˈpres}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{v. 盖印；使感动，留下印象}
  \wordsense{n. 印记 *im<in (= on)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{impression}{imˈpreʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{n. 压痕；印刷；印次；印数；印象；（模糊的）观念 *im<in (= on)}
  \wordexample{impressionism（印象主义） impressionist（印象主义者）}
  \wordexample{impressionistic（印象主义的）}
  \wordexample{impression of a seal on wax（蜡上面的〔?〕印） first impression of 5,000 copies（第一次印刷5000册）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{impressionable}{imˈpreʃənəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{a. 敏感的，易受影响的 *im<in (= on)}
  \wordexample{children who are at the impressionable age（处于敏感年龄的孩子们） impressionable young lady（很容易受感动的年轻女子）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{impressive}{imˈpresiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{a. 给人深刻印象的 *im<in (= on)}
  \wordexample{impressive ceremony（给人深刻印象的仪式）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{pressing}{ˈpresiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{a. 紧急的，迫切的，需要立即处理的；恳切的，再三要求的}
  \wordsense{n.（同一批压制的）唱片}
  \wordexample{pressing invitation（恳切的邀请） 100 pressings of a symphony（100张交响乐唱片） pressing business（紧急事务）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{pressure}{ˈpreʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{n. 压；压力；压迫；困扰；艰难；紧迫}
  \wordexample{atmospheric / blood pressure（气压 / 血压） pressure cooker（高压锅） pressure gauge（压力计） pressure of work（工作压力） under strong pressure（承受巨大压力的） pressure group（为影响政策或舆论而组织的压力集团）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{pressurized}{ˈpreʃəraizd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRESS}
  \wordsense{a.（飞机、潜水艇等）增压的}
\end{word}

\chapter{CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cept, cap, capt, ceiv 意为 to take, to seize。变形为 ceit, cip, cup。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 prehend / prehens, pris, empt / em, rap / rav, sum / sumpt, lab / lep 等。其中 cap [cip] 还有 head 的含义。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{concept}{ˈkɒnsept}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together) + cept (= take) 一起抓、取}
  \wordsense{n. 概念}
  \wordexample{Dr. Jameson's theory about personality is not a new concept.（詹姆森博士有关人格的理论不是一种新概念。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{inception}{inˈsepʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{带到里面去 in (= in, on) + cept (= take)}
  \wordsense{n. 开始，起初，发端}
  \wordexample{At its inception, our company had just 2 employees.（在创立初期，我们公司只有两名员工。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{intercept}{ˌintəˈsept}
  \wordbreakdown{在中间抓住、夺取 inter (= between) + cept (= take)}
  \wordsense{vt. 拦截}
  \wordphrase{interception 拦截 interceptor 拦截者；拦截机}
  \wordexample{Could you please intercept Ms. Barnes on her way out and give her this message?（你能去拦住巴恩斯女士告诉她这个消息吗？）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{perceive}{pəˈsi:v}
  \wordbreakdown{通过某种事情抓住或理解 per (= through) + ceiv (= take)}
  \wordsense{vt. 察觉，感知；认识到；理解}
  \wordphrase{perception 察觉，感知}
  \wordphrase{perceptible 可感觉到的 perceptive 感知的；敏锐的}
  \wordexample{perceptibly 可察觉地，可感觉地 cf. imperceptibly 察觉不到地，细微地}
  \wordexample{Studies show that two witnesses can perceive the same event quite differently.（研究表明，两名证人对同一事件的理解可能有很大的不同。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{precept}{ˈpri:sept}
  \wordbreakdown{事先抓住 pre (= before) + cept (= take)}
  \wordsense{n. 训诫；戒律}
  \wordphrase{preceptor 教师，训诫者}
  \wordexample{One of the main precepts of the Boy Scouts is to be “morally straight and clean”.（童子军的主要训诫之一是“品德端正纯洁”。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{susceptible}{səˈseptəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{容易被捕捉的 sus<sub (= under, intensive) + cept (= take)}
  \wordsense{a. 易受影响的，易动感情的；敏感的；允许的；可以……的}
  \wordphrase{susceptibility 多情，善感；敏感}
  \wordexample{Don't bother flirting with me. I'm not susceptible to your dubious charms.（不要向我卖弄风情，我对你的风情不感冒。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{capacious}{kəˈpeiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 容积大的，宽敞的，广阔的}
  \wordphrase{capacity 接受能力；容积；能力；资格}
  \wordexample{I love flying first class for the capacious seats.（我喜欢坐头等舱是因为头等舱坐椅宽敞。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{caption}{ˈkæpʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n.（杂志等报道的）标题，题目；附在照片、插图上的说明文字；（电影的）字幕}
  \wordsense{vt. 为……添加标题}
  \wordexample{The caption under the picture was wrong. The editor made a mistake.（那张图下面的说明文字不对，编辑弄错了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{captious}{ˈkæpʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 吹毛求疵的；强词夺理的}
  \wordexample{He's a captious sort〔?〕impossible to please.（他是那种爱挑剔的人，很难让他满意。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{recipient}{riˈsipiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 接受者，受领者}
  \wordsense{a. 接受的，受领的}
  \wordexample{The award recipient was so nervous he tripped and fell off the stage!（那个获奖者太紧张了，以至于绊倒摔下了舞台！）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{receptive}{riˈseptiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 易于接受的；接受的}
  \wordphrase{receive 接受；接待}
  \wordphrase{reception 接受；接待；欢迎会}
  \wordphrase{receptionist 招待员}
  \wordphrase{receipt 收条，收据}
  \wordphrase{receiver 收取人；接收器}
  \wordphrase{receivable 可以接受的}
  \wordphrase{received 被认可的，被认为标准的}
  \wordexample{The mechanics union officials seem to be receptive to the new offer.（技工工会的干部似乎接受了新提议。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{accept}{əkˈsept}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 接受；答应；认可；【商】承兑（汇票）}
  \wordexample{accept an invitation（接受邀请）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{acceptable}{əkˈseptəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 可接受的；令人满意的}
  \wordexample{acceptability（可接受）}
  \wordexample{acceptable present（令人满意的礼物）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{acceptance}{əkˈseptəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 接受；承认；承兑，认付；承兑汇票}
  \wordexample{acceptance speech（《美》总统候选人接受提名时所作的演讲）}
  \wordexample{acceptance payable（应付承兑汇票）}
  \wordexample{acceptance receivable（应收承兑汇票）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{accepted}{əkˈseptid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 公认的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conceive}{kənˈsi:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 抱有（想法等）；构想，构思，想象；认为；怀孕}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conceivable}{kənˈsi:vəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 可想到的，可想像的}
  \wordexample{conceivably（想得到地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conception}{kənˈsepʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 想法，构想；概念；怀孕}
  \wordexample{conceptual（概念上的）}
  \wordexample{conception control（避孕）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conceit}{kənˈsi:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 自满，自负；奇想，幻想}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conceited}{kənˈsi:tid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 骄傲自满的，自以为是的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{contraception}{ˌkɒntrəˈsepʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 避孕（法）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{contraceptive}{ˌkɒntrəˈseptiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 避孕工具，避孕药}
  \wordsense{a. 避孕（用）的}
  \wordexample{contraceptive pills / devices（避孕药 / 避孕用具）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{deceive}{diˈsi:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 欺骗，行骗}
  \wordexample{deceiver（骗子）}
  \wordexample{deceivingly（欺骗地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deception}{diˈsepʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 欺骗，行骗；受骗；诈术，诡计}
  \wordexample{gross deception（大骗局）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deceptive}{diˈseptiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 欺骗的；虚伪的；迷惑（人）的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{deceit}{diˈsi:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 欺骗，欺诈；谎言，欺诈手段}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deceitful}{diˈsi:tfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 骗人的，欺诈的}
  \wordexample{deceitfully（欺骗地，骗人地）}
  \wordexample{deceitful behavior（欺诈的行为）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{except}{ikˈsept}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 把……除外}
  \wordsense{prep. 除……之外}
  \wordexample{excepting（除……之外）}
  \wordexample{present company excepted（不计这里的几个人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{exception}{ikˈsepʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 除外；例外；例外的人（或事物）；异议}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{exceptional}{ikˈsepʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 例外的；特别的，特殊的}
  \wordexample{exceptionally（例外地；特别地）}
  \wordexample{exceptionable（可反对的，可提出异议的）}
  \wordexample{exceptional child（特殊 / 异常儿童）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{receptacle}{riˈseptəkl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 容器}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{capable}{ˈkeipəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 有能力的，有才能的；（人）有能力做……的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{capability}{ˌkeipəˈbiləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 能力；（pl.）潜能}
  \wordexample{nuclear capability（核能力）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{captivate}{ˈkæptiveit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. （使）迷惑}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{captive}{ˈkæptiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a.（人、兽）被俘的；受监禁的}
  \wordexample{captive audience（受制听众）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{captivity}{kæpˈtivəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 被俘；囚禁}
  \wordexample{in captivity（被俘；被囚禁）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{captor}{ˈkæptə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 捕捉者，捕获者}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{capture}{ˈkæptʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 捕获，俘虏；夺取；获得（奖项），赢得}
  \wordsense{n. 捕获，俘虏；捕获物}
  \wordexample{capture of a thief（捕获小偷）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{incapacitate}{ˌinkəˈpæsiteit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 使无能力，使不能}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{incapable}{inˈkeipəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 不能的；无能的；不能正常行动的}
  \wordexample{incapability（无能）}
  \wordexample{drunk and incapable（醉得不能动的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{recapture}{ˌri:ˈkæptʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 夺回}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{anticipate}{ænˈtisipeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 预期，期待}
  \wordexample{anticipatory（预期的，预先的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{anticipation}{ænˌtisiˈpeiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 预期，预料；预料的事物}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{incipient}{inˈsipiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 起初的，初期的}
  \wordexample{incipient decay of the teeth（早期龋齿）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{participate}{pa:ˈtisipeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 参加，参与}
  \wordexample{participation（参加，参与）}
  \wordexample{participant（参加者，参与者）}
  \wordexample{participatory（参与的）}
  \wordexample{participatory democracy（分享民主制）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{participle}{ˈpa:tisipl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n.【语】分词}
  \wordexample{participial（分词的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{principal}{ˈprinsəpl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 主要的，首要的，最重要的}
  \wordsense{n. 校长，（组织的）负责人；（经纪人或代理人所代表的）委托人，本人；主要屋架；本金，资本；主犯}
  \wordexample{principally（主要地）}
  \wordexample{principal and accessory（主犯与从犯）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{principle}{ˈprinsəpl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 原理，原则；信条，主义}
  \wordexample{moral principles（道义）}
  \wordexample{low / high principled woman（品格低下的 / 高尚的女人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{prince}{prins}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n.（小国的）君主，诸侯；王子；……公；巨头；名家}
  \wordexample{princedom（小国君主的地位或领地）}
  \wordexample{princess（王妃；公主）}
  \wordexample{On Prince（《君主论》）}
  \wordexample{the Crown Prince / the Prince Imperial（王储 / 太子）}
  \wordexample{the Prince of Wales（威尔士王储）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{princely}{ˈprinsli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 王子般的；高贵的；慷慨的；豪华的；庄严的}
  \wordexample{princely gift（丰厚的礼物）}
  \wordexample{princely mansion（豪华住宅）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{principality}{ˌprinsiˈpæləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 公国；侯国；封邑；（the P\textasciitilde{}）威尔士的别称}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{recipe}{ˈresəpi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 烹饪法；（药剂等的）处方（符号 R）；诀窍}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{occupy}{ˈɒkjupai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 居住；占用（时间、场所）；占领，占据；使（某人）从事；担任（职务）}
  \wordexample{“Occupied”（使用中）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{occupation}{ˌɒkjuˈpeiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 职业；居住；占有，占领}
  \wordexample{army of occupation（占领军）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{occupational}{ˌɒkjuˈpeiʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 职业的}
  \wordexample{occupational experience（工作经验，工作经历）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{occupant}{ˈɒkjupənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n.（职位的）占有者，（房屋等的）现居住者}
  \wordexample{occupancy（房屋、土地等的）占有；占用期}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{occupier}{ˈɒkjupaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 占有者；（土地或房屋的临时的）占有者}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preoccupy}{priˈɒkjupai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 使（某人）全神贯注；抢先占有，抢先占据}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preoccupied}{priˈɒkjupaid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{a. 全神贯注的；热衷的；抢先占有的}
  \wordexample{preoccupied by family troubles（一心只挂念家中麻烦的）}
  \wordexample{preoccupied with thoughts of the coming holidays（一心只想着即将到来的假期的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preoccupation}{priˌɒkjuˈpeiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{n. 全神贯注，专注；偏见；关注的事物；抢先占有}
  \wordexample{her greatest preoccupation（她最关注的事）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{recuperate}{riˈku:pəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEPT, CAP, CAPT, CEIV}
  \wordsense{v. 恢复（健康）；挽回（损失等）}
  \wordexample{recuperation（恢复；找回）}
  \wordexample{recuperative（恢复的；有恢复力的）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 fer 意为 to carry, to bear。词根 lat 与 fer 都来自拉丁语的 ferre，lat 来自该词的不规则过去分词，两个词根可以一起学习。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 ger / gest, port, veh / vect 等和来自希腊语的 phor / pher 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{confer}{kənˈfɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{带着某物搬运或一起搬运 con<com (= with, together) + fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + on + sb 授予，赠与}
  \wordsense{vi. with + sb; about + sth 协商；比较，参看（缩写：cf.）}
  \wordphrase{conference 协商，商谈 conferment 授予}
  \wordexample{Please confer with your academic advisor before enrolling in the course.（在选修这门课之前，请先向你们的导师咨询一下。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deference}{ˈdefərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{向下搬运 de (= down) + fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{n. 顺从，服从；尊敬，敬意}
  \wordphrase{defer 顺从，服从；表示敬意；延期 deferential 恭敬的 deferentially 恭敬地 deferment 延期}
  \wordexample{The judge gets cranky if the attorneys don't show her proper deference.（如果律师没有表现出诚挚的敬意，法官就会变得很苛刻。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{infer}{inˈfɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面搬运 in (= in) + fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{vt.（根据事实）推理，推断，推定；暗示；意味着}
  \wordphrase{inference 推论；推断 inferential 推论的，推理的}
  \wordexample{I infer from your dour expression that you're angry with me.（你脸色阴沉，可以推断你生我的气了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{opprobrium}{əˈprəubriəm}
  \wordbreakdown{和某人敌对，向前搬运 op<ob (= against) + pro (= forth) + br<fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{n. 耻辱，不名誉；蔑视；责难}
  \wordphrase{opprobrious 侮辱的，辱骂的；丢脸的，羞耻的}
  \wordexample{Many Republicans said that Clinton brought unprecedented opprobrium to his office.（很多共和党人说，克林顿给他的职位带来了前所未有的耻辱。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preferment}{priˈfɜ:mənt}
  \wordbreakdown{向前搬运 pre (= before) + fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{n. 晋升，升级；（尤指教会中的）美差，高位}
  \wordexample{He is a gentleman who never sought preferment at the cost of personal integrity.（他是一位绝不会牺牲自己的正直而谋求晋升的绅士。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{proffer}{ˈprɔfə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{朝着某个东西向前输送 pr<pro (= forward) + of<ob (= toward) + fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{vt. 提供；提出}
  \wordsense{n. 提供；提议}
  \wordexample{We proffered our services as house painters to all our neighbors that summer.（那年夏天我们帮所有的邻居粉刷了房子。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{vociferous}{vəuˈsifərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{出声的 voc (= call, voice) + fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{a. 嘈杂的，大声叫喊的}
  \wordexample{vociferate 叫喊 vociferation 〔?〕喊，高喊}
  \wordexample{The protestors made a vociferous but unsuccessful demonstration in the street.（抗议者们在街上大喊着抗议示威，但却没有取得成功。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{fertile}{ˈfɜ:tail}
  \wordbreakdown{可以搬运的，可带来的 fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{a.（土地等）肥沃的，（植物、动物）多产的；想像力丰富的，创造力丰富的}
  \wordexample{fertilize 使肥沃；使受精 fertility 肥沃，多产性；生殖力；丰富 fertilizer（化学）肥料 fertilization 施肥；肥沃}
  \wordexample{Some people seem to have been born with a fertile imagination.（有些人似乎天生就具有丰富的想像力。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{indifference}{inˈdifrəns}
  \wordbreakdown{搬运时注意使不脱离 in (= not) + dif<dis (= away) + fer (= carry)}
  \wordsense{n. 不关心，冷淡；不重要}
  \wordphrase{indifferent 不关心的；无关紧要的；平凡的}
  \wordexample{It's hard to be in love with you when you show me only indifference.（你对我如此冷淡，我很难爱上你。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{afferent}{ˈæfərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{a.【解】传入的 *af<ad (= to, toward)}
  \wordexample{efferent（传出的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{circumference}{səˈkʌmfərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n. 周围，周边；圆周；周长 *circum (= around)}
  \wordexample{the circumference of the earth（地球的周长）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{differ}{ˈdifə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{v. 不同，区别；意见不同 *dif<dis (= away, apart)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{difference}{ˈdifrəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n. 差异，相异，不同，（量、程度、方式等的）差别；（意见的）分歧，不和 *dif<dis (= away, apart)}
  \wordexample{make a difference（区别对待；起重要作用） make no difference（没有差异；无关紧要）}
  \wordexample{That makes all the difference.（那使情况截然不同。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{different}{ˈdifrənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{a. 不同的，相异的；各种的；个别的；与众不同的，独特的 *dif<dis (= away, apart)}
  \wordexample{differently（不同地） at different times（在不同的时期）}
  \wordexample{Different nations have different customs.（不同的民族有不同的风俗。） It's a different matter.（那是另一码事。） tobacco that is different（特别的烟草）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{differentiate}{ˌdifəˈrenʃieit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{v. 区别，区分 *dif<dis (= away, apart)}
  \wordexample{differentiation（区别）}
  \wordexample{differentiate varieties of plants（区分各种植物） differentiate between pupils（对学生区别对待）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{differential}{ˌdifəˈrenʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{a. 区别的，差别的}
  \wordsense{n.（在相同行业里依据熟练程度而产生的）工资级差 *dif<dis (= away, apart)}
  \wordexample{differential tariffs（差别关税） differential diagnosis（【医】鉴别诊断） differential gear（差动齿轮） differential calculus（【数】微分学） differential coefficient（导数） differential equation（微分方程） wage differential（工资差异）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{interfere}{ˌintəˈfiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{v. 干涉，干预；（工作、事情等）相冲突；打扰，妨碍 *inter (= between)}
  \wordexample{interfering old lady（爱管闲事的老太婆）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{interference}{ˌintəˈfiərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n. 干涉；妨碍 *inter (= between)}
  \wordexample{interference in internal affairs（干涉内政）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{offer}{ˈɔfə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{v. 提供，提出；提议；备有……出售；出价，报价；施加；贡献，奉献}
  \wordsense{n. 提议；提供；开价；报价；奉献 *of<ob (= to, toward)}
  \wordexample{offer an opinion（提出意见） offer \$500,000 for a house（房子要价50万美元） You offer.（你出个价吧。） accept / decline an offer（接受 / 拒绝一项提议） special offer（特价优惠）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{offering}{ˈɔfəriŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n. 提议；提供；（向神）贡奉；祭品，供品；捐款 *of<ob (= to, toward)}
  \wordexample{offering of bribes（行贿） offering plate（（教会的）捐款盘）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{offertory}{ˈɔfətəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n.（礼拜时的）献金 *of<ob (= to, toward)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{prefer}{priˈfɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{v. 宁愿（选择），更喜欢；提出（控告等）；《古》提升，提拔 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{prefer a charge against a motorist（控告汽车驾驶人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preferable}{ˈprefrəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{a. 更好的，更可取的 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{preferably（宁愿，宁可）}
  \wordexample{Poverty is preferable to ill health.（贫穷总比得病强。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{preference}{ˈprefrəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n. 更喜爱，偏爱；偏爱的事物，优选物；优先（权）；（关税的）特惠 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{have a preference for French novels（更喜欢法国小说） What are your preferences?（你更喜欢什么？）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{preferential}{ˌprefəˈrenʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{a. 优先的，优先提供（或接受）的；（关税等）特惠的 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{get preferential treatment（获得优待） preferential voting（选择选举制）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{refer}{riˈfɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{v. 谈及；查询；参考；把……归因于 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{referable（可归因于……的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{reference}{ˈrefrəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n. 查阅；参照，参考；谈及；委托；推荐信；参考文献，参考资料 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{referential（参照的，参考用的；与……有关的）}
  \wordexample{reference book（参考书（如词典、百科全书等）） excellent references from former employers（前雇主的特别推荐）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{referendum}{ˌrefəˈrendəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n. 公民投票 *re (= again)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{referee}{ˌrefəˈri:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n.（比赛的）裁判员}
  \wordsense{v. 裁判 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{referee a football match（担任足球赛的裁判）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suffer}{ˈsʌfə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{v. 承受（痛苦、损害等），经受，经历（不快的事），遭受；忍受 *suf<sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{sufferer（承受痛苦的人；患者；受难者） sufferable（可忍受的，可容忍的）}
  \wordexample{suffer from headaches（头疼）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suffering}{ˈsʌfəriŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n.（身心的）痛苦；（pl.）苦难；令人痛苦的事 *suf<sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{the sufferings of the Jews（犹太民族的苦难）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sufferance}{ˈsʌfərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{n. 忍受，忍耐 *suf<sub (= under)}
  \wordexample{on sufferance（勉强的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{transfer}{trænsˈfɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{v. 搬运，使转移；改变，转换；调动；转让，让与（财产、权利、头衔等）；转乘}
  \wordsense{n. 迁移，转移；移交，转让；转账；转运；（职务等的）调动，调任；传递（或转换等）的方法；运送工具，调动手段；汇兑，汇划（尤指电汇）；转车（或船、飞机）票，转车（或船、飞机）处，（汽车或火车的）摆渡处，（汽车或火车的）渡轮；转让证书；转学证书；转队证书；调职者，调任者；转学生；调换部队者；（股票等的）过户；过户凭单 *trans (= over, through)}
  \wordexample{transference（转移，传递，传输；（职务等的）调动，调任；（财产等的）转让，让与；【心】移情）}
  \wordexample{transfer fee（搬运费） transfer ticket（渡轮票）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{transferable}{trænsˈfɜ:rəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{a. 可转移的；可流通的；可转让的，可转换的 *trans (= over, through)}
  \wordexample{transferable accounts（可转拨账户） Railway tickets are not transferable.（火车票是不可转让的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{infertile}{inˈfɜ:tail}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FER}
  \wordsense{a. 不肥沃的，贫瘠的，不毛的；不结果实的，不生育的 *in (= not)}
  \wordexample{infertile eggs（未受精卵）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FISC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 fisc 意为 chest, money basket。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{confiscate}{ˈkɔnfiskeit}
  \wordbreakdown{使和金库分开 con<com (= with) + fisc (= chest)}
  \wordsense{vt. 没收，充公}
  \wordphrase{confiscation 没收，充公}
  \wordexample{The dorm director will confiscate any illegal drugs.（宿舍管理员将没收所有非法药品。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{fiscal}{ˈfiskl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FISC}
  \wordsense{a. 国库的；财政的；会计的}
  \wordexample{fiscal year（财政年度（美国的财政年度于9月30日结束）） fiscal stamp（印花税票）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SEQU, SECUT[SU]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sequ, secut 意为 to follow，su 为其变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{consequence}{ˈkɔnsikwəns}
  \wordbreakdown{一起跟来 con<com (= with) + sequ (= follow)}
  \wordsense{n. 结果；重要性}
  \wordphrase{consequent 作为结果发生的 consequential 作为结果发生的；重大的 consequently 所以，结果}
  \wordexample{Fifty-three people lost their homes as a consequence of the tornado.（那次龙卷风使53人失去了家园。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{inconsequential}{ˌinkɔnsiˈkwenʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{不一起跟随的 in (= not) + con<com (= with) + sequ (= follow)}
  \wordsense{a. 不合逻辑的，不合伦理的；不重要的，细小的}
  \wordexample{An inconsequential number of people actually read my book.（只有极少数人认真地阅读了我的书。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{obsequious}{əbˈsi:kwiəs}
  \wordbreakdown{追随得让人感到碍手碍脚的 ob (= in the way) + sequ (= follow)}
  \wordsense{a. 奉承的；拍马屁的}
  \wordexample{It's difficult for me to trust obsequious people.（我很难信任阿谀奉承的人。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{subsequent}{ˈsʌbsikwənt}
  \wordbreakdown{在下面或尾部接着发生的 sub (= under) + sequ (= follow)}
  \wordsense{a. 后续的；以后的}
  \wordphrase{subsequently 此后，其后，随后 subsequence 后继}
  \wordexample{Subsequent tests revealed that she was suffering from pleurisy.（随后的检查结果表明她患上了肋膜炎。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{persecute}{ˈpɜ:sikju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{坏事一直跟着 per (= through, throughout) + secut (= follow)}
  \wordsense{vt. 迫害；纠缠；为难}
  \wordphrase{persecution 迫害；纠缠 persecutor 迫害者}
  \wordexample{I was persecuted for months by an IRS representative who said I owed more taxes.（几个月以来，我一直被一位美国国内收入署职员所纠缠，他说我欠缴更多税金。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{prosecute}{ˈprɔsikju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{事先追踪行迹 pro (= before) + secut (= follow)}
  \wordsense{vt. 起诉，告发；从事，经营}
  \wordphrase{prosecution 起诉，告发；从事，经营；控方，原告；检察当局 prosecutor 检察官；起诉人}
  \wordexample{The DA decided not to prosecute the young man, as it was his first offense.（鉴于那个年轻人是初犯，地方检察官决定不起诉他了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ensue}{inˈsju:}
  \wordbreakdown{紧接在某事物的后边发生 en (= intensive) + su<sequ (= follow)}
  \wordsense{vi. 随后发生；接着发生；因而发生}
  \wordphrase{ensuing 接下来的，接着发生的}
  \wordexample{Widespread looting ensued after the race riot.（种族暴乱之后紧接着发生了大规模的掠夺。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{inconsequent}{inˈkɔnsikwənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{a. 不一致的；不切题的 *in (= not), con<com (= with)}
  \wordexample{inconsequent remark（不切题的话）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sequel}{ˈsi:kwəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{n.（前面发生的事的）继续，后续；结果，结局；（故事、电影等的）续集，续篇}
  \wordexample{sequel to the novel（小说的续篇） in the sequel（后来）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sequence}{ˈsi:kwəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{n. 接连，连续；（事件等的）顺序，序列；结果}
  \wordexample{sequent（连续的；继起的；作为结果而产生的） sequential（连续的，相继的，随之而来的；作为结果发生的）}
  \wordexample{in regular sequence（按次序地，有条不紊地） in historical sequence（按照历史顺序） a sequence of bad harvests（连续歉收） in alphabetical sequence（按照字母顺序）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{consecutive}{kənˈsekjutiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{a. 连续的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordexample{consecutive holidays（连休） on five consecutive days（连续五天）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{execute}{ˈeksikju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{v. 实行（要求、命令等），履行，实施，执行；处死；演奏；（按计划）制成 *ex (= intensive), ecut<secut (= follow)}
  \wordexample{executant（实行者，执行者；演奏者）}
  \wordexample{execute a plan（执行计划） execute a will（执行遗嘱） execute a murderer（处死杀人犯）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{execution}{ˌeksiˈkju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{n.（计划等的）实行，执行，履行；（作品等的）制作，完成；（音乐等的）演奏；技巧；（武器的）杀伤力，破坏效果；执行死刑，处决 *ex (= intensive), ecut<secut (= follow)}
  \wordexample{executioner（死刑执行人） executor（遗嘱执行人）}
  \wordexample{execution by hanging（处以绞刑） a writ of execution（执行令）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{executive}{igˈzekjutiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{a. 执行的；实行的；有执行权的；行政（上）的，行政性的}
  \wordsense{n.（the \textasciitilde{}）（政府的）行政部门；执行官；（企业的）经理；主管领导 *ex (= intensive), ecut<secut (= follow)}
  \wordexample{executive committee（执行委员会） executive director（执行董事） Chief Executive（最高行政长官；《美》总统；州长） County Executive（县长） executive agreement（和他国政府间的行政协定） executive officer（行政长官） executive order（《美》总统的行政命令，一般写成 E.O.） executive privilege（为保护机密而享有的行政特权，总统特权） executive secretary（行政秘书，执行秘书）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{pursue}{pəˈsju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{v. 追赶，追踪，追击；纠缠；进行，执行；以……为目标，追求 *pur<pro (= forth, before)}
  \wordexample{pursuer（追赶者；追求者）}
  \wordexample{pursue a robber（追赶强盗） pursue pleasure（追求快乐） pursued by misfortune（被厄运纠缠）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{pursuit}{pəˈsju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{n. 追赶，追击；追求；职业；消遣 *pur<pro (= forth, before)}
  \wordexample{a fox with the hounds in hot pursuit（被猎狗猛追的狐狸）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{pursuant}{pəˈsjuənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{a. 遵循的}
  \wordsense{ad. 相符地，一致地 *pur<pro (= forth, before)}
  \wordexample{pursuance（追赶，追求；进行；遵循）}
  \wordexample{pursuant to your instructions（依照您的指示） pursuant to Article 12（依据第12条）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sue}{su:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{v. 控告，对……提出诉讼；恳请，请求}
  \wordexample{sue for a divorce / peace（提出离婚诉讼 / 请求和解）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suit}{su:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{n. 控告，诉讼；恳求；一套外衣}
  \wordsense{v. 适合；适当；合……的心意；相配}
  \wordexample{suiting（西装料） suitable（合适的，适当的）}
  \wordexample{bring a suit against somebody（对某人提出诉讼） criminal / civil suit（刑事诉讼 / 民事诉讼）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{suitor}{ˈsu:tə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{n. 起诉者，原告；（男性）求婚者}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{suite}{swi:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEQU, SECUT[SU]}
  \wordsense{n.（重要人物的）随员；（由几部分组成的）一套家具；套房（如宾馆里除卧室以外还带有客厅、会客室的房间）；（物品的）一套；系列}
  \wordexample{dining-room suite（一套餐厅家具） lounge / bedroom suite（一套休息室 / 卧室家具） computer suite（计算机套装软件）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SERV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 serv 意为 to serve, to keep。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{conserve}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{v. [kənˈsɜ:v]}
  \wordsense{n. [ˈkɔnsɜ:v] [用某物来维持 con<com (= with) + serv (= serve, keep)]}
  \wordsense{vt. 保存，维护}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）水果蜜饯；果酱}
  \wordexample{conservation 保护，保存；管理 conservancy（河道；港口的）管理局；保护 conservatism 保守主义 conservatory 保守性的；（n.）温室；音乐（或美术、话剧）学校 conservative 保守的；（n.）保守主义者，保守党人}
  \wordexample{The primary goal of historical societies is to conserve landmarks.（历史学会的首要目标是保存文化古迹。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{subservient}{səbˈsɜ:viənt}
  \wordbreakdown{在下面侍奉、有帮助的 sub (= under) + serv (= serve)}
  \wordsense{a. 有益的；屈从的；从属的}
  \wordphrase{subserve 有利于；促进，推动 subservience 屈从；有益}
  \wordexample{Australia's subservient relationship with England gave way to an equal relationship.（澳大利亚和英国恢复了平等，取代了澳大利亚从属于英国的关系。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{servile}{ˈsɜ:vail}
  \wordbreakdown{侍奉的 serv (= serve)}
  \wordsense{a. 奴隶的，奴隶般的；不独立的；阿谀的，屈从的}
  \wordphrase{servility 奴性；卑屈，屈从}
  \wordexample{I think his promotion owes more to his servile manner than to talent.（我认为他之所以能够升迁，更多是因为他善于奉迎而非有才能。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conservatory}{kənˈsɜ:vətəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 温室；音乐学校 *con<com (= with, together)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{deserve}{diˈzɜ:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{v. 应受；值得 *de (= down)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{deserved}{diˈzɜ:vd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{a. 当然的，应得的 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{deservedly（理所应当地，应得地）}
  \wordexample{deserved reward / praise / punishment（应得的补偿 / 称赞 / 惩罚）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{deserving}{diˈzɜ:viŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{a. 应得的，值得的；值得帮助的 *de (= down)}
  \wordexample{deserving cause（值得赞助的事业） deserving of sympathy（应该受到同情的） deserving of praise（值得获得称赞的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{observe}{əbˈzɜ:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{v. 奉行（规则等），遵守；庆祝（节日、纪念日、生日等）；察觉；观察；监视；发表意见 *ob (= to, intensive)}
  \wordexample{observing（观察力敏锐的，注意观察的；从事观察的） observable（看得见的，可观察到的，值得注意的）}
  \wordexample{observe Christmas（庆祝圣诞节） He observed that the plan would work well.（他说那个计划会顺利进行。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{observation}{ˌɔbzəˈveiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 观察，注意，监视；观察力；（pl.）观察资料；观察数据 *ob (= to, intensive)}
  \wordexample{observation car（瞭望车） a man of little observation（观察力极差的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{observatory}{əbˈzɜ:vətəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 瞭望台；天文台；气象台；观测所 *ob (= to, intensive), ory (= place where)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{observer}{əbˈzɜ:və(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 观察员（列席会议但没有投票权）；观察者，观测者，监视者；遵守者 *ob (= to, intensive)}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{observance}{əbˈzɜ:vəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n.（法律、规则、习惯等的）遵守，奉行；仪式 *ob (= to, intensive)}
  \wordexample{the observance of the Sabbath（守安息日） the observance of the Queen's birthday（庆祝女王诞辰）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{observant}{əbˈzɜ:vənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{a. 观察力敏锐的，遵守（法律、习惯等）的 *ob (= to, intensive)}
  \wordexample{observant boy（观察力敏锐的孩子） observant of the rules（遵守规则的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{preserve}{priˈzɜ:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{v. 保护，保存；防腐，保藏；保持，维持；禁止他人捕猎}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）果酱，蜜饯；禁猎地 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{preservable（可保存的；可保护的） preserver（保存者，保护者；起保护作用的东西）}
  \wordexample{God preserve us!（上帝保佑我们！）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{preservation}{ˌprezəˈveiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 保存，保藏；保护 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{in good preservation（保存得好的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{preservative}{priˈzɜ:vətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{a. 有保存能力的；有利于保藏的}
  \wordsense{n. 防腐剂 *pre (= before)}
  \wordexample{fresh cream free from preservatives（没有添加防腐剂的新鲜奶油）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{reserve}{riˈzɜ:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{v.（为未来或者某种目的而）保留，储备；（为某个特定的人）准备，预备；预约，保留（权利）；预订}
  \wordsense{n. 储备（物），备用（物）；预备队；保留地；严肃；节制；谨慎 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{reservist（预备役军人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{reserved}{riˈzɜ:vd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{a. 保留的，留作专用的；预约的，预备的；有节制的；内向的，沉默寡言的 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{reservedly（拘谨地；有节制地）}
  \wordexample{reserved for special guests（留给贵宾的） All seats reserved.（所有席位都已预订。） reserved seat（预约席，指定席，包席） All rights reserved.（版权所有。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{reservation}{ˌrezəˈveiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 预约，保留；专用地；（印第安人的）居留区 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{make reservations（预约） without reservation（无条件地；无保留地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{reservoir}{ˈrezəvwɑ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 蓄水池；储藏所；（知识等的）储备 *re (= again)}
  \wordexample{reservoir of a fountain pen / an oil lamp（钢笔里的墨水囊 / 油灯里的油盒）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{serve}{sɜ:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{v. 服务；供职，任职；（商店）接待（客人）；端上，提出（食物）；有助于……，对……有用；使满足；对待；服役；送达（令状等）；发球；（在弥撒中）充当助祭}
  \wordsense{n.（网球等的）发球}
  \wordexample{Dinner is served.（晚饭都摆好了。） It serves him right.（他活该。） serve ten years of sentence（服刑十年） serve a summons / writ / warrant on somebody（向某人送达传票 / 令状 / 逮捕〈或搜查〉令） serve a ball（发球） First come, first served.（先到先接待。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{servant}{ˈsɜ:vənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 仆人，佣人；雇工；事务员}
  \wordexample{public servant（公务员，公仆） civil servant（文职人员）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{server}{ˈsɜ:və(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 服务者，侍者；餐具}
  \wordexample{salad-servers（色拉盘）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{serving}{ˈsɜ:viŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n.（食物等的）一份}
  \wordexample{four servings（四人份）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{service}{ˈsɜ:vis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 贡献；（为客人提供的）服务；（交通机关的）公共设施；（政府的）部门；宗教仪式，礼拜式；（商品售出后提供的）维修服务；（需要专业知识的）服务}
  \wordsense{v.（车、机器等售出后）维修，保养}
  \wordexample{serviceman（军人） service charge（服务费） have the car serviced regularly（定期维修汽车）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{serviceable}{ˈsɜ:visəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{a. 适于日常穿戴的；耐用的；有用的}
  \wordexample{serviceable clothes for school children（适于学生穿的衣服）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{servitude}{ˈsɜ:vitju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERV}
  \wordsense{n. 奴役（状态）；苦役}
  \wordexample{servitor（侍从，仆人；（牛津大学的）工读生）}
\end{word}

\chapter{STRAIN, STRICT, STRING, STRESS[STRANG]}

\begin{itemize}
