  \wordexample{diameter of a tree-trunk（树干的直径）a lens that magnifies 20 diameters（放大 20 倍的透镜）in diameter（直径上）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{diametrically}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 METER[METR]}
  \wordsense{ad.完全相反地；直径地 *dia（= across, through）}
  \wordsense{[ˌdaiə'metrikli]}
  \wordexample{diametrically opposed view（完全相反的观点）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{geometry}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 METER[METR]}
  \wordsense{n.几何学 *geo（= earth）}
  \wordsense{[dʒi'ɔmitri]}
  \wordexample{geometric / geometrical（几何学（上）的；（线、条纹等）几何图案的）}
  \wordexample{geometrical patterns（几何图案（纹样））geometrical progression（【数】几何级数）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{perimeter}{pə'rimitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 METER[METR]}
  \wordsense{n.【数】周；周边 *peri（= around）}
\end{word}

\chapter{JUR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根JUR 来自拉丁语的 jur 意为 to swear, law。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{perjury}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{【虚假地宣誓 per（= falsely）+ jur（= swear, law）】 ['pə:dʒəri] n.【律】伪证（罪）；背信弃义}
  \wordphrase{perjure 使作伪证 perjurer 作伪证者}
  \wordexample{The witness was convicted of perjury and fined 2,000 dollars.（那个证人被判犯有伪证罪，并被处以 2000 美元的罚金。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{abjure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{v.发誓放弃；公开放弃（权利、信仰等） *ab（= away）, jur（= swear）}
  \wordsense{[əb'dʒuə(r)]}
  \wordexample{abjure one's religion（抛弃信仰）abjure the realm（放弃领土）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{abjuration}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{n.发誓放弃；公开放弃 *ab（= away）, jur（= swear）}
  \wordsense{[ˌæbdʒuə'reiʃn]}
  \wordexample{abjuration of faith（信仰的抛弃）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{adjure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{v.恳求；严令 *ad（= to）, jur（= swear）}
  \wordsense{[ə'dʒuə(r)]}
  \wordexample{adjuration（恳求；严令）}
  \wordexample{I adjure you to tell the truth.（我命令你说实话。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conjure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{v.念咒语召唤；魔法变出；使人想起 *con<com（= together）, jur（= swear）}
  \wordsense{['kʌndʒə(r)]}
  \wordexample{conjuration（召唤；咒语；魔法）conjurer / conjuror（魔术师，变戏法的人；魔法师；巫师）}
  \wordexample{conjuring trick（魔术）conjure a rabbit out of a hat（从帽子里变出一只兔子）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{injure}{'indʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{v.使受伤，损害；伤害（感情、名誉等） *in（= not）, jur（= law）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{injured}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{a.（感情）受伤的；（名誉）被损害的 *in（= not）, jur（= law）}
  \wordsense{['indʒəd]}
  \wordexample{the dead and the injured（（事故等的）死伤者）in an injured voice（用受伤的声音）injured look（受伤的表情）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{injurious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{a.有害的；致伤的；诽谤的，中伤的 *in（= not）, jur（= law）}
  \wordexample{behavior that is injurious to social order（对社会秩序有害的行为）injurious to health（有损健康的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{injury}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{n.（对人、动物、精神等的）伤害；（对物的）损伤；（对健康的）损害；伤口 *in（= not）, jur（= law）}
  \wordsense{['indʒəri]}
  \wordexample{injury to the head（头上的伤）severe injuries（重伤）do a person an injury（让人蒙受损害）injury to my pride（对我自尊心的伤害）severe injury to his reputation（对他名誉的严重损害）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{jurisprudence}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{n.法学 *pr<pro（= before）, ud<vid（= look）, jur（= law）}
  \wordsense{[ˌdʒuəris'pru:dəns]}
  \wordexample{Doctor of Jurisprudence（法学博士，缩写：J.D.）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{juridical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{a.司法（上）的；法律（上）的}
  \wordsense{[dʒuə'ridikl]}
  \wordexample{juridical days（开庭日期）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{jurist}{'dʒuərist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{n.法学家；法律学者}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{juror}{'dʒuərə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{n.陪审员；（竞赛等的）评委}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{jury}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUR}
  \wordsense{n.陪审团；评审委员会}
  \wordsense{['dʒuəri]}
  \wordexample{juryman（陪审员）}
  \wordexample{common jury（普通陪审团）grand jury（大陪审团（特别指由 12 名到 23 名人员组成，审理上诉的案件，在认为证据充足的时候作判定））jury box（陪审（团）席）jury room（陪审团议事室）jury of public opinion（公众舆论的评判）}
\end{word}

\chapter{VAD, VAS[WAD]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根VAD, VAS[WAD] 来自拉丁语的 vad, vas 意为 to go。变形为 wad。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 ced/cess[ceed, ceas]，grad/gred/gress，it 和来自希腊语的 bat[bet, bit]，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 fare。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{pervade}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAD, VAS[WAD]}
  \wordsense{【这儿、那儿，一一走遍 per（= through, throughout）+ vad（= go）】 [pə'veid] vt.遍及；弥漫于，渗透于}
  \wordphrase{pervasion 弥漫，渗透；普及 pervasive 遍及的，普遍深入的；渗透的}
  \wordexample{According to conservatives, harmful violence pervades a lot of television programming.（保守主义者认为，有害的暴力行为渗透到了很多电视节目中。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{invade}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAD, VAS[WAD]}
  \wordsense{v.入侵，侵略；侵害；大批进入 *in（= into）}
  \wordsense{[in'veid]}
  \wordexample{invasion（入侵，侵略）invader（侵略者，入侵者）}
  \wordexample{city invaded by tourists（游客大批涌入的城市）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{vade-mecum}{ˌveidi'mi:kəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAD, VAS[WAD]}
  \wordsense{n.随身携带品；袖珍指南手册 *mecum（= together with me）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{wade}{weid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAD, VAS[WAD]}
  \wordsense{v.涉水而过（江、河等） *wad<vad（= go）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{waddle}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAD, VAS[WAD]}
  \wordsense{v.蹒跚而行}
  \wordsense{n.蹒跚，摇摆的步子 *wad<vad（= go） ['wɔdl]}
\end{word}

\chapter{ANN[ENN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根ANN[ENN] 来自拉丁语的 ann 意为 year，其元音弱化后变成 enn。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{perennial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{【持续整整一年 per（= through, throughout）+ enn<ann（= year）】 [pə'reniəl] a.终年不断的；长期的；（植物）多年生的}
  \wordsense{n.多年生植物}
  \wordexample{The perennial star Julie Andrews no longer sings, but still acts.（老牌歌星朱莉·安德鲁斯不再演唱，但还继续演戏。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{annals}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{n.编年史；历史记载；学会的年报}
  \wordsense{['ænəlz]}
  \wordexample{annalist（编年史的编者；年报的作者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{anniversary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{n.周年纪念日；结婚周年 *vers（= turn）}
  \wordsense{[ˌæni'və:səri]}
  \wordexample{my wedding anniversary（我的结婚纪念日）anniversary of Shakespeare's birth（莎士比亚的诞辰纪念日）anniversary dinner（周年纪念宴会）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{Anno Domini}{'ænəu'dɔminai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{n.纪元；公元（简称 AD） *domin（= lord, master）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{annual}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{a.每年的；一年一次的；一年生的}
  \wordsense{n.一年生植物；年鉴，年报}
  \wordsense{['ænjuəl]}
  \wordexample{annually（每年）}
  \wordexample{his annual income（他的年收入）annual production（年产量）annual message（年度咨文）annual enrollment in high schools（高中每年的入学新生）annual ring（树木的年轮）hardy annual（耐寒一年生植物）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{annuity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{n.年金}
  \wordsense{[ə'nju:əti]}
  \wordexample{annuitant（年金受领人）life annuity（终身年金）annuity bond（年金债券）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{biennial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{a.两年一次的，每两年的}
  \wordsense{n.两年生植物 *bi（= two）, enn<ann（= year） [bai'eniəl]}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{millennium}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{n.一千年；千年王国，千禧年；未来的黄金时代 *mil（= thousand）, enn<ann（= year）}
  \wordsense{[mi'leniəm]}
  \wordexample{millenarian（相信千禧年的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{centennial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{n.一百周年；一百周年纪念日}
  \wordsense{a.一百年一次的；一百周年的；一百岁的 *cent（= hundred）, enn<ann（= year）}
  \wordsense{[sen'teniəl]}
  \wordexample{centenary（= centennial）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{bicentennial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{n.两百周年；两百周年纪念日}
  \wordsense{a.两百年一次的；两百周年的；两百岁的 *bi（= two）, cent（= hundred）, enn<ann（= year）}
  \wordsense{[ˌbaisen'teniəl]}
  \wordexample{bicentenary（= bicentennial）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{quincentennial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ANN[ENN]}
  \wordsense{n.五百周年；五百周年纪念日}
  \wordsense{a.五百年一次的；五百周年的；五百岁的 *quin（= five）, cent（= hundred）, enn<ann（= year）}
  \wordsense{[ˌkwinsen'teniəl]}
  \wordexample{quincentenary（= quincentennial）}
\end{word}

\chapter{MEA[ME]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MEA[ME] 来自拉丁语的 mea 意为 to pass，其变形为 me。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{permeate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEA[ME]}
  \wordsense{【这里、那里，一一经过、流过 per（= through）+ me<mea（= pass, flow）】 ['pə:mieit] vt.渗透，浸透；弥漫}
  \wordphrase{permeation 渗透，漫透；弥漫 permeability 浸透性，渗透性}
  \wordphrase{permeable 可以渗透的}
  \wordexample{The smell of fish permeated the kitchen for days.（连续几天厨房里一直弥漫着鱼腥味。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{impermeable}{im'pə:miəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEA[ME]}
  \wordsense{a.不透水的 *im<in（= not）, per（= through）, me<mea（= pass）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FUNCT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FUNCT 来自拉丁语的 funct 意为 to perform。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{perfunctory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUNCT}
  \wordsense{【假装做事的 per（= falsely）+ funct（= perform）】 [pə'fʌŋktəri] a.敷衍的，马马虎虎的}
  \wordexample{perfunctorily（敷衍地，马马虎虎地）}
  \wordexample{The acquisitions editor only gave a perfunctory glance at the volume of poetry.（组稿编辑只是马马虎虎地看了一眼那本诗集。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{defunct}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUNCT}
  \wordsense{【停止、中断做事的 de（= off, from）+ funct（= perform）】 [di'fʌŋkt] a.死的；（法律等）废止的}
  \wordexample{The movie theater in my small town has been defunct for a decade.（我们小城镇的剧场关门已有十年了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{function}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUNCT}
  \wordsense{n.功能，作用；职务，职能；宗教仪式，庆典活动；【数】函数}
  \wordsense{v.起作用；运转}
  \wordsense{['fʌŋkʃn]}
  \wordexample{functionalism（功能主义）functionalist（功能主义者）}
  \wordexample{function of the heart（心脏的功能）the numerous functions that the Queen must attend（女王不得不参加的各种仪式）My new typewriter doesn't function very well.（我的新打字机不太好用。）He functioned as boss.（他起到了首领的作用。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{functional}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUNCT}
  \wordsense{a.机能的；作用的；职务（上）的；（建筑等）从使用的观点设计（或构成）的}
  \wordsense{['fʌŋkʃənl]}
  \wordexample{functional disorder（机能紊乱）functional representation（【医】功能表达法）functional furniture（实用的家具）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{functionary}{'fʌŋkʃənəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUNCT}
  \wordsense{n.（尤指政府机关、政党的）官员；公务员}
\end{word}

\chapter{CAU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CAU 来自拉丁语的 cau 意为 to take care。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{precaution}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CAU}
  \wordsense{【事先小心或注意 pre（= before）+ cau（= take care）】 [pri'kɔ:ʃn] n.谨慎；警惕；预防}
  \wordexample{precautionary（警惕的；预防的）}
  \wordexample{As a precaution, please fasten your seat belt before starting the car.（作为一种预防措施，请在发动车之前系好安全带。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{caution}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CAU}
  \wordsense{n.小心，慎重；警告；（外貌、行动或言语等）令人发笑的人}
  \wordsense{v.使某人小心；警告}
  \wordsense{['kɔ:ʃn]}
  \wordexample{cautionary（警告的；告诫的）cautionary tales（警世故事）}
  \wordexample{We were cautioned not to drive fast.（我们被警告不能开快车。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{cautious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CAU}
  \wordsense{a.谨慎的，小心的}
  \wordsense{['kɔ:ʃəs]}
  \wordexample{cautiously（谨慎地，小心地）}
  \wordexample{cautious about giving offense（唯恐让对方生气而小心翼翼的）}
\end{word}

\chapter{MAT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MAT 来自拉丁语的 mat 意为 ripe。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{premature}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAT}
  \wordsense{【在成熟之前的 pre（= before）+ mat（= ripe）】 ['premətʃə(r)] a.（做法等）不成熟的；太早的，不到期的；早产的}
  \wordexample{It would be premature to fire her; she's still learning her job.（解雇她时机尚早，她还在熟悉工作。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{immature}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAT}
  \wordsense{a.未成熟的，发育未全的 *im<in（= not）}
  \wordsense{[ˌimə'tjuə(r)]}
  \wordexample{immaturity（未成熟（状态）；未成年）}
  \wordexample{immature girl（尚年幼的少女）immature minds of young children（儿童未成熟的心智）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{mature}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAT}
  \wordsense{a.成熟的；酿熟的；慎重的；周到的；（票据）到支付日期的}
  \wordsense{v.使成熟；使长成；（票据）到期；酿成}
  \wordsense{[mə'tʃuə(r)]}
  \wordexample{mature woman（成熟的女人）mature plans（慎重的计划）This wine has not matured properly.（葡萄酒还没有酿好。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{maturity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAT}
  \wordsense{n.成熟；（票据的）到期}
  \wordsense{[mə'tʃuərəti]}
  \wordexample{come to / reach maturity（成熟）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{maturate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAT}
  \wordsense{v.成熟；化脓}
  \wordsense{['mætʃureit]}
  \wordexample{maturation（成熟；化脓）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SUMPT, SUM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SUMPT, SUM 来自拉丁语的 sum, sumpt 意为 to take, to use, to waste。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 prehend/prehens，pris，cap/capt/cept/ceiv[cip, cup, ceit]，empt/em，rap/rav，lab/lep 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{presume}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{【事先抓住、思考 pre（= before）+ sum（= take）】 [pri'zju:m] v.推断；擅自（做），冒昧（做）}
  \wordphrase{presumption 推断；放肆；专横}
  \wordphrase{presumptuous 放肆的；专横的 presumable 可以推断的 presuming 放肆的 presumptive 推断的}
  \wordphrase{presumably 据推测；可能}
  \wordexample{I presume you'll be flying, not driving, right?（我猜你会坐飞机，而不是自己开车，对吧？）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sumptuous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{【用得太多或者浪费的 sumpt（= take, use, waste）】 ['sʌmptʃuəs] a.昂贵的，奢侈的；豪华的，华丽的}
  \wordexample{Thank you for the sumptuous meal. You're a wonderful host.（感谢您的盛宴，您真是一位盛情的主人。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{assume}{ə'sju:m}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{v.假定；采取；承担；就任；假装 *as<ad（= near）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{assumption}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{n.假定，假说；担任；伪装 *as<ad（= near）}
  \wordsense{[ə'sʌmpʃn]}
  \wordexample{his assumption of power / the presidency（他掌权 / 任职总统）with an assumption of indifference（装作漠不关心）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{consume}{kən'sju:m}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{v.消费；浪费；吃光，喝光；耗尽；使全神贯注 *con<com（= together, completely）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{consuming}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{a.强烈的；消费的 *con<com（= together, completely）}
  \wordsense{[kən'sju:miŋ]}
  \wordexample{consuming ambition（强烈的野心）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{consumer}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{n.消费者 *con<com（= together, completely）}
  \wordsense{[kən'sju:mə(r)]}
  \wordexample{consumer goods（消费品；生活资料）consumer price index（消费者物价指数，缩写：CPI）consumer research（消费者调查）consumer resistance（消费者抵抗）consumer strike（（消费者的）罢购）consumer science（消费学）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{consumption}{kən'sʌmpʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{n.消费；消耗；消费量；肺痨 *con<com（= together, completely）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{consumptive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{a.消费的；消耗性的；肺痨的}
  \wordsense{n.肺痨患者 *con<com（= together, completely） [kən'sʌmptiv]}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{resume}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{v.重新开始；继续；重新占用；重返；收回 *re（= again, back）}
  \wordsense{[ri'zju:m]}
  \wordexample{resumé（简历；摘要，概要）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{resumption}{ri'zʌmpʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{n.重新开始 *re（= again, back）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{subsume}{səb'sju:m}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{v.使包含；把……纳入 *sub（= under）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sumpter}{'sʌmptə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{n.驮马，驮骡}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{sumptuary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMPT, SUM}
  \wordsense{a.限制费用的；禁止奢侈的}
  \wordsense{['sʌmptʃuəri]}
  \wordexample{sumptuary law（禁止奢侈法）}
\end{word}

\chapter{DOMIN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根DOMIN 来自拉丁语的 domin 意为 master, lord。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{predominant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOMIN}
  \wordsense{【提前征服、熟练、精通的 pre（= before）+ domin（= master）】 [pri'dɔminənt] a.突出的；主要的，支配的；占优势的；显著的}
  \wordphrase{predominate 支配；占优势 predominance 支配；优势；显著}
  \wordphrase{predominantly 占优势地；显著地}
  \wordexample{The predominant color in her decorating scheme is taupe.（她的装饰方案的主色是褐灰色。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{dominant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOMIN}
  \wordsense{【支配的或征服的 domin（= master）】 ['dɔminənt] a.支配的，统治的；居高临下的；高耸的}
  \wordphrase{dominate 支配；统治；高耸于；俯视 domination 支配；优势 dominance 优势；统治}
  \wordexample{The dominant male in a group of dogs is called the "alpha male".（统治狗群的公狗被称做“阿尔法公狗”。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{domineer}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOMIN}
  \wordsense{v.（\textasciitilde{} over）跋扈，作威作福}
  \wordsense{[ˌdɔmi'niə(r)]}
  \wordexample{Big boys sometimes domineer (over) their small sisters.（大男孩们有时候会对小妹妹横行霸道。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{domineering}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOMIN}
  \wordsense{a.跋扈的，盛气凌人的}
  \wordsense{[ˌdɔmi'niəriŋ]}
  \wordexample{He's a very domineering sort of fellow.（他是个盛气凌人的家伙。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{dominion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOMIN}
  \wordsense{n.支配，统治；领土；（英联邦的）自治领}
  \wordsense{[də'minjən]}
  \wordexample{exercise dominion over（对……行使统治权）the Dominion (of Canada)（加拿大自治领）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{domino}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOMIN}
  \wordsense{n.假面服（在聚会或化装舞会上穿的带假面的衣服）；多米诺游戏；多米诺游戏中使用的骨牌（用木头或骨头做的扁小的长方形牌，上面有点数）}
  \wordsense{['dɔminəu]}
  \wordexample{domino effect（多米诺效应（因为一个事件引发一连串事件的连锁效应））domino theory（多米诺理论）}
\end{word}

\chapter{AMBUL[AMBL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根AMBUL[AMBL] 来自拉丁语的 ambul 意为 to walk，变形为 ambl。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 pat。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{preamble}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{【提前走出来 pre（= before）+ ambl<ambul（= walk, go）】 [pri'æmbl] n.（法律、条约等的）序言，前言}
  \wordexample{My editor asked me to write a short preamble to my latest book.（负责我书稿的编辑让我为自己的新书写一篇简短的序言。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{perambulate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{v.走过；徘徊；巡视 *per（= throughout）}
  \wordsense{[pə'ræmbjuleit]}
  \wordexample{perambulation（徘徊；巡视记录）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{perambulator}{pə'ræmbjuleitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{n.四轮婴儿车（简称：pram）（在美国一般用 baby-carriage） *per（= throughout）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ramble}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{v.漫步；漫谈；像蔓草一样横生}
  \wordsense{n.闲逛；漫步 *r<re（= again）}
  \wordsense{['ræmbl]}
  \wordexample{rambler（漫谈者；蔓生植物）}
  \wordexample{rambler roses（蔓生蔷薇）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{rambling}{'ræmbliŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{a.（街道、建筑等）布局零乱的；（演说、随笔等）散漫的 *r<re（= again）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{noctambulist}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{n.梦游病患者 *noct（= night）}
  \wordsense{[nɔk'tæmbjulist]}
  \wordexample{noctambulism（梦游病）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{somnambulism}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{n.梦游病 *somn（= sleep）}
  \wordsense{[sɔm'næmbjulizəm]}
  \wordexample{somnambulist（梦游病患者）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{funambulist}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{n.走绳索的艺人 *fun（= rope）}
  \wordsense{[fju:'næmbjulist]}
  \wordexample{funambulism（走绳索）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{amble}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{v.（马）缓行；从容漫步}
  \wordsense{n.从容的步伐 *ambl<ambul（= walk） ['æmbl]}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ambulance}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AMBUL[AMBL]}
  \wordsense{n.救护车}
  \wordsense{['æmbjuləns]}
  \wordexample{ambulance corps（野战救护队）}
\end{word}

\chapter{POSTER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根POSTER 来自拉丁语的 poster 意为 coming after。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{preposterous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POSTER}
  \wordsense{【本来应该在后面的东西跑到前面的 pre（= before）+ poster（= coming after）】 [pri'pɔstərəs] a.前后颠倒的；反常的；不合理的，荒谬的}
  \wordexample{preposterously（荒谬地，不合理地）}
  \wordexample{He makes the preposterous claim that he is the best tennis player in the city.（他很荒唐地说自己是本市最出色的网球选手。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{posterior}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POSTER}
  \wordsense{a.（时间、顺序）较晚的，以后的}
  \wordsense{n.臀部（= buttocks）}
  \wordsense{[pɔ'stiəriə(r)]}
  \wordexample{kick his posterior（踢他的屁股）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{posterity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POSTER}
  \wordsense{n.子孙，后裔，后代}
  \wordsense{[pɔ'sterəti]}
  \wordexample{plant trees for the benefit of posterity（为了造福后代而植树）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{postern}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POSTER}
  \wordsense{n.暗道；后门；边门；私人出入口}
  \wordsense{['pɔstən]}
  \wordexample{postern door / gate（后门）}
\end{word}

\chapter{MED}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MED 来自拉丁语的 med 意为 to heal, to attend to。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{premeditated}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{【事先注意的 pre（= before）+ med（= attend to, heal）】 [pri:'mediteitid] a.事先想好的，有计划的}
  \wordphrase{premeditate 预先考虑 premeditation 预先的设想}
  \wordexample{Her slapping you wasn't a premeditated act. She was just very angry.（她不是有意要打你，只是当时太生气了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{meditate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{v.（\textasciitilde{} on）深思，沉思；打算，企图 *med（= attend to）}
  \wordsense{['mediteit]}
  \wordexample{meditate mischief / revenge（企图搞恶作剧 / 策划报复）meditate on his misfortune（深思自己的不幸）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{meditation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{n.冥想，沉思，深思；（pl.）沉思录 *med（= attend to）}
  \wordsense{[ˌmedi'teiʃn]}
  \wordexample{meditative（冥想的，乐于冥想的）deep in meditation（陷入沉思的）meditation on the causes of aggression in man（关于人类侵略原因的思考）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{remedy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{n.治疗；药物；补救（措施），矫正（法）}
  \wordsense{v.治疗；矫正；除去（弊端等） *re（= again）, med（= heal）}
  \wordsense{['remədi]}
  \wordexample{good remedy for colds（好的感冒药）the only remedy for the situation（这种情况的唯一补救措施）past / beyond remedy（没有补救方法的）Your faults of pronunciation can be remedied.（你发音上的缺陷可以矫正。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{remedial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{a.治疗的；矫正的；补救的 *re（= again）, med（= heal）}
  \wordsense{[ri'mi:diəl]}
  \wordexample{remediable（可以治疗的；可以矫正的，可以挽回的）remedial measures（补救措施）remedial education / classes（矫正教育 / 学习）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{irremediable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{a.无法治疗的；无法挽回的 *ir<in（= not）, re（= again）, med（= heal）}
  \wordsense{[i'remi:diəbl]}
  \wordexample{irremediable acts / faults（不能挽回的行为 / 过失）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{medical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{a.医学的；医术的；医疗的；内科的}
  \wordsense{n.体检 *med（= heal）}
  \wordsense{['medikl]}
  \wordexample{medical attendant（私人保健医生）medical care（治疗，医疗）medical fertilization（人工授精）medical art（医术）medical examination（体检）medical jurisprudence（法医学）medical science（医学）medical school（医学院）medical student（医学院学生）medical practitioner（开业医师）medical record（医疗记录）medical certificate（诊断书）medical checkup（体检）medical, not surgical, treatment（不是外科的内科治疗）medical / surgical ward（内科 / 外科病房）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{medicament}{mi'dikəmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{n.（内服或外用的）药 *med（= heal）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{medicate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{v.用药治疗；在……里加入药 *med（= heal）}
  \wordsense{['medikeit]}
  \wordexample{medicated bath / soap / gauze（药浴 / 药用肥皂 / 药制纱布）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{medication}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{n.药物治疗；药物 *med（= heal）}
  \wordsense{[ˌmedi'keiʃn]}
  \wordexample{mass medication（在公共自来水中加氟等针对大众的预防或治疗措施）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{medicine}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{n.医学，医术；内科；药物，（特别指）内服药；（原始部落的）咒文，符咒；魔法 *med（= heal）}
  \wordsense{['medsn]}
  \wordexample{Doctor of Medicine（医学博士，缩写：M.D.）practice medicine（（医生）开业）take medicine(s)（吃药）medicine for a cough（治疗咳嗽的药）take one's medicine（忍受不愉快的事情；受到惩罚）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{medicinal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{a.药的；治疗的 *med（= heal）}
  \wordsense{[mə'disnl]}
  \wordexample{medicinal preparations for both internal and external use（内服药和外用药的制作材料）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{medico}{'medikəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{n.医生；医学院学生 *med（= heal）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{Medicare}{'medikeə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{n.（美）（尤指政府为老年人设置的）医疗保障方案（来源于 medical care） *med（= heal）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{Medicaid}{'medikeid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MED}
  \wordsense{n.（美）（尤指政府为低收入家庭、残疾人设置的）医疗补助方案（来源于 medical aid） *med（= heal）}
\end{word}

\chapter{TEXT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TEXT 来自拉丁语的 text 意为 to weave。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{pretext}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEXT}
  \wordsense{【事先编织、编造 pre（= before）+ text（= weave）】 ['pri:tekst] n.借口；托词}
  \wordexample{She used her mild cold as a pretext for missing work today.（今天她以不怎么严重的感冒为借口没去上班。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{context}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEXT}
  \wordsense{n.（文章的）上下文；（事件等的）来龙去脉，背景 *con<com（= together）}
  \wordsense{['kɔntekst]}
  \wordexample{contextual（上下文的）}
  \wordexample{in this context（在这种背景下）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{textile}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEXT}
  \wordsense{n.纺织品；编织原料}
  \wordsense{a.纺织的 *con<com（= together）}
  \wordsense{['tekstail]}
  \wordexample{textile process（纺织过程）the textile industry（纺织业）textile art / fabrics（编织工艺 / 纺织品）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{texture}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEXT}
  \wordsense{n.（织物等的）质地，做工；（文艺作品等的）组织；结构}
  \wordsense{['tekstʃə(r)]}
  \wordexample{cloth with a loose / close texture（质地稀松的 / 紧密的布）texture of a mineral（矿物的构造）skin of fine / coarse texture（细腻 / 粗糙的皮肤）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{text}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEXT}
  \wordsense{n.正文（相对于注释、图解、插图等）；原文；（布道时引用的）圣经的章节；（演说、讨论等的）题目，主题；课文，课本}
  \wordsense{[tekst]}
  \wordexample{too much text and not enough pictures（文字太多，图片太少）stick to one's text（（故事等）没有脱离主题）speaker's text（演讲者的主题）algebra text（代数课本）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{textual}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEXT}
  \wordsense{a.本文的，原文的}
  \wordsense{['tekstʃuəl]}
  \wordexample{textual errors（原文的错误）textual criticism（校勘）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{subtle}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEXT}
  \wordsense{a.微妙的；难以捕捉的；敏感的；敏锐的；巧妙的；精妙的；不知不觉间起作用的；狡猾的 *sub（= precisely, under）}
  \wordsense{['sʌtl]}
  \wordexample{subtle distinction（微妙的区别）subtle charm（神秘的魅力）subtle mind（敏锐的思维）subtle observer / critic（敏锐的观察者 / 批评家）subtle tricks（狡猾的手段）subtle design（精妙的设计）subtle drug（不知不觉间起作用的药）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{subtlety}{'sʌtlti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEXT}
  \wordsense{n.微妙；敏感；精巧；细微的差别；微妙之处 *sub（= precisely, under）}
\end{word}

\chapter{MON[MIN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MON[MIN] 来自拉丁语的 mon 意为 to warn, to advise, to remind。变形为 min。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{admonish}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MON[MIN]}
  \wordsense{【向某人提出忠告 ad（= to）+ mon（= warn, advise, remind）】 [əd'mɔniʃ] vt.训诫，告诫；警告；劝告}
  \wordphrase{admonition 训诫；警告 admonitory 告诫的；警告的}
  \wordexample{The teacher admonished the children not to ridicule the crippled girl.（老师告诫孩子们不要嘲笑那个跛脚的女孩儿。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{premonition}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MON[MIN]}
  \wordsense{【事先警告、使想起 pre（= before）+ mon（= warn, advise, remind）】 [ˌpri:mə'niʃn] n.预感，前兆；预告}
  \wordphrase{premonitory 预告的；前兆的}
  \wordexample{I had a premonition that we were going to have an earthquake.（我预感到了我们会遭遇地震。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{reminisce}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MON[MIN]}
  \wordsense{【返回记忆 re（= back）+ min<mon（= remind, remember, warn）】 [ˌremi'nis] vi.追忆，回想}
  \wordphrase{reminiscence 追忆，回想 reminiscent 追忆的，回想的}
  \wordexample{We had such fun at the 20th high school reunion, reminiscing about the old days.（在高中同学毕业 20 周年聚会上，我们沉浸在对往昔的回忆中，过得非常愉快。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{monitor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MON[MIN]}
  \wordsense{n.（学校的）班长；国外电台监听员；监考员；告诫者；监视器；监测器}
  \wordsense{v.监控；监测；监听；监视 *mon（= warn, advise） ['mɔnitə(r)]}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{monument}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MON[MIN]}
  \wordsense{n.纪念碑；纪念塔；纪念馆；不朽的作品；历史遗迹 *mon（= remind）}
  \wordsense{['mɔnjumənt]}
  \wordexample{Washington Monument（华盛顿纪念碑）the Monument（（1666 年的）伦敦大火纪念塔）ancient monuments（古迹）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{monumental}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MON[MIN]}
  \wordsense{a.纪念的；（著作等）不朽的；极端的；非常的 *mon（= remind）}
  \wordsense{[ˌmɔnju'mentl]}
  \wordexample{monumental work（不朽的作品）monumental ignorance（极度的无知）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{summon}{'sʌmən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MON[MIN]}
  \wordsense{v.召唤；传唤；召集 *sum<sub（= under）, mon（= warn）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{summons}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MON[MIN]}
  \wordsense{n.传唤；传票 *sum<sub（= under）, mon（= warn）}
  \wordsense{['sʌmənz]}
  \wordexample{issue a summons（发传票）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SAG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SAG 来自拉丁语的 sag 意为 to perceive。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{presage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAG}
  \wordsense{【事先感觉、感知 pre（= before）+ sag（= perceive）】 ['presidʒ] vt.预示；预言；预知，预感}
  \wordsense{n.前兆，预感；预知}
  \wordexample{Chest and arm pains, nausea, and shortness of breath can presage a heart attack.（胸部和手臂疼痛、恶心以及气短都可能是心脏病发作的前兆。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{sage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAG}
  \wordsense{n.贤人，哲人}
  \wordsense{a.智慧的；贤明的；（贬）道貌岸然的；一本正经的 [seidʒ]}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sagacious}{sə'geiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAG}
  \wordsense{a.精明的；有洞察力的；（动物）伶俐的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{sagacity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAG}
  \wordsense{n.贤明；睿智}
  \wordsense{[sə'gæsəti]}
  \wordexample{Sagacity, unlike cleverness, may increase with age.（与伶俐不同，睿智可能会随着年龄的增长而增长。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SCI}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SCI 来自拉丁语的 sci 意为 to know。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 gno，not。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{prescience}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{【事先知道 pre（= before）+ sci（= know）】 ['preʃəns] n.预知；先见}
  \wordphrase{prescient 预知的；有先见之明的}
  \wordexample{Thanks to his prescience, he got out of the stock market before it fell.（幸亏他有先见之明，能在股市下跌之前脱身。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{omniscient}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{【无论什么都知道的 omni（= all）+ sci（= know）】 [ɔm'niʃənt] a.无所不知的}
  \wordphrase{omniscience 无所不知的}
  \wordexample{Life would be boring if one were truly omniscient.（如果一个人真的通晓一切，那么他的生活将会很无聊。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conscience}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{n.良心；道义，善恶观念 *con<com（= together）}
  \wordsense{['kɔnʃəns]}
  \wordexample{have no conscience（没有良心）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{conscientious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{a.凭良心的；诚实的；认真的，勤勤恳恳的 *con<com（= together）}
  \wordsense{[ˌkɔnʃi'enʃəs]}
  \wordexample{conscientious student / worker（勤奋的学生 / 勤勤恳恳的工人）conscientious objector（（根据宗教、道义上的信念）拒服兵役的人（缩写：C.O.））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{conscious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{a.意识到的；神志清醒的；（行为等）有意的 *con<com（= together）}
  \wordsense{['kɔnʃəs]}
  \wordexample{consciously（有意识地；自觉地）}
  \wordexample{be conscious of（意识到……）class-conscious（阶级意识的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{consciousness}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{n.知觉；意识 *con<com（= together）}
  \wordsense{['kɔnʃəsnis]}
  \wordexample{class consciousness（阶级意识）stream of consciousness（意识流）consciousness of kind（同类意识）moral consciousness of a political party（政党的道德意识）lose / regain (recover) consciousness（丧失 / 恢复意识）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{unconscionable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{a.不合理的；没良心的 *un（= not）, con<com（= together）}
  \wordsense{[ʌn'kɔnʃənəbl]}
  \wordexample{unconscionable bargain（不合理的交易）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{unconscious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{a.无意识的，不知不觉的；没有知觉的，不省人事的}
  \wordsense{n.无意识 *un（= not）, con<com（= together）}
  \wordsense{[ʌn'kɔnʃəs]}
  \wordexample{unconscious humor（无意识间产生的幽默）unconscious neglect（无意的疏忽）be unconscious of danger（没有意识到危险）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{nescience}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{n.无知 *ne（= not）}
  \wordsense{['neʃəns]}
  \wordexample{nescient（无知的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{plebiscite}{'plebisait}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{n.（对国家重要问题的）公民投票 *pleb（= people）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{sciolistic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{a.一知半解的}
  \wordsense{[ˌsaiəu'listik]}
  \wordexample{sciolist（一知半解的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{science}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{n.科学；（特指）自然科学；学科；技术}
  \wordsense{['saiəns]}
  \wordexample{scientist（自然科学家）}
  \wordexample{natural (physical) / social / applied sciences（自然科学 / 社会科学 / 应用科学）science fiction（科幻小说）political science（政治学）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{scientific}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordsense{a.科学的；系统的；精确的；有技术的 *fic（= make）}
  \wordsense{[ˌsaiən'tifik]}
  \wordexample{scientific methods / farming（科学方法 / 科学种田）scientific name（学名）scientific argument（科学的论证）}
\end{word}

\chapter{CUR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CUR 来自拉丁语的 cur 意为 to take care。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{procure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{【事先注意、小心 pro（= before）+ cur（= take care）】 [prə'kjuə(r)] v.取得，获得；筹措；引起，导致；介绍（娼妓）}
  \wordphrase{procurement 获得；筹措 procurer 拉皮条者 procuress 老鸨 procurator 代诉人}
  \wordphrase{procurable 可获得的}
  \wordexample{My fiancé procured a string quartet to play when he proposed.（我未婚夫在向我求婚时，弄来了一个弦乐四重奏乐团。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{accurate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{a.准确的；精密的，精确的 *ac<ad（= to, intensive）}
  \wordsense{['ækjurət]}
  \wordexample{accurately（精确地）}
  \wordexample{accurate machines / statements（精密的机器 / 精确的陈述）accurate typist（错误极少的打字员）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{accuracy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{n.准确；精密 *ac<ad（= to, intensive）}
  \wordsense{['ækjurəsi]}
  \wordexample{with accuracy（准确地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{secure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{a.安全的；安心的；确定无疑的，确信的；稳固的；稳当的}
  \wordsense{v.使安全，确保；得到（不易获得的东西）；缚牢；关紧 *se（= away）}
  \wordsense{[si'kjuə(r)]}
  \wordexample{securely（安全地；有把握地）}
  \wordexample{secure investments / position（稳当的投资 / 稳固的地位）The success secured his reputation.（成功确保了他的名声。）Please secure a seat for me.（请给我占一个位子。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{security}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{n.安全（感）；保证；保安，防卫；担保（物）；（pl.）债券，证券 *se（= away）}
  \wordsense{[si'kjuərəti]}
  \wordexample{the Security Council（（联合国）安全理事会）security police / forces（秘密警察 / 保安部队）Security is the greatest enemy.（安全是最大的敌人。不能掉以轻心。）security for a loan（贷款的担保）government securities（政府债券）Securities and Exchange Commission（证券交易委员会（缩写：SEC））}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{cure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{n.治疗；治愈；（处理社会问题等的）对策}
  \wordsense{v.治疗；纠正；消除（弊病等）；加工处理（通常指为了保存肉、鱼等而腌制、熏制、风干等）}
  \wordsense{[kjuə(r)]}
  \wordexample{cure（《法》堂区牧师）cure-all（万能药）cure for unemployment（失业的对策）well-cured bacon（加工得好的熏肉）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{curable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{a.可医治的；可以纠正的}
  \wordsense{['kjuərəbl]}
  \wordexample{curability（治愈可能性）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{curative}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{a.有疗效的；治疗的}
  \wordsense{['kjuərətiv]}
  \wordexample{curative value of sunshine and sea air（日光和海边空气的治疗价值）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{curator}{kjuə'reitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{n.（博物馆、美术馆的）馆长；管理者}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{curio}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{n.古董，真品}
  \wordsense{['kjuəriəu]}
  \wordexample{curio dealer（古董商）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{curiosity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{n.好奇心；珍品；古玩}
  \wordsense{[ˌkjuəri'ɔsəti]}
  \wordexample{curiosity shop（古玩店）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{curious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{a.有好奇心的；爱打听别人的事的；奇妙的}
  \wordsense{['kjuəriəs]}
  \wordexample{curiously（好奇地；奇妙地）}
\end{word}

\chapter{MONT,MOUNT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MONT, MOUNT 来自拉丁语的 mont, mount 意为 hill, to rise。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{promontory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{【向前突出的山坡 pro（= forth）+ mont（= hill, rise）】 ['prɔməntəri] n.岬，海角}
  \wordexample{This is a small promontory of land jutting into the Pacific.（这是伸向太平洋的一个小海角。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{paramount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{【在山坡旁边的 para（= beside）+ mount（= hill）】 ['pærəmaunt] a.至高无上的；卓越的；主要的，最重要的}
  \wordphrase{paramountcy 最高权威；至高无上；卓越}
  \wordexample{Childhood vaccinations are of paramount importance to prevent terrible diseases.（从小接种疫苗对预防重大疾病至关重要。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{surmount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{【越过山坡走 sur（= over）+ mount（= hill, rise）】 [sə'maunt] vt.克服（困难、障碍）；越过；在……顶上，高于}
  \wordphrase{surmountable 可以克服的}
  \wordexample{The typical hero of a romance novel surmounts difficulty and triumphs.（典型的爱情小说中的男主人公总是能够克服困难取得胜利。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{insurmountable}{ˌinsə'mauntəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{a.无法克服的；无法逾越的 *in（= not）, sur（= over）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{tantamount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{a.相当于……的 *tanta（= so much）}
  \wordsense{['tæntəmaunt]}
  \wordexample{The Queen's request was tantamount to a command.（女王的要求等同于命令。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{dismount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{v.使下马；使掉下马；卸下；拆开（机器等） *dis（= away）}
  \wordsense{[dis'maunt]}
  \wordexample{dismounted（（骑兵）像步兵一样战斗的）}
  \wordexample{dismount from one's horse / bicycle（从马 / 自行车上下来）dismount a gun（（从炮架上）放下大炮）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{remount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{v.再骑上（马等）；重登上（梯子等）；补充新马}
  \wordsense{n.补充的新马 *re（= again） [ri:'maunt]}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{mountain}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{n.山；（pl.）山脉；〈喻〉堆积成山的东西；大量}
  \wordsense{['mauntin]}
  \wordexample{mountaineer（登山家；在山地生活的人）mountaineering（登山）mountain sickness（高山病）}
  \wordexample{a mountain of debts / difficulties（堆积成山的债务 / 困难）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{mountainous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{a.多山的；有山的；像山一般的；巨大的}
  \wordsense{['mauntənəs]}
  \wordexample{mountainous country（山岳地带）mountainous waves（如山的波浪）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{mount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{v.爬上（山、梯子、楼梯等）；骑（马等）；（物价等）上涨；发动（攻击等）}
  \wordsense{n.骑马；可乘骑的东西（如马、车等）；底座；炮架；山，峰（通常简称 Mt，附加在山的名字上）}
  \wordsense{[maunt]}
  \wordexample{Mount（Mt.）Everest（珠穆朗玛峰）the Sermon on the Mount（《山顶布道》，出自《马太福音第五章》）Mount Rushmore（总统山）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{mountebank}{'mauntibæŋk}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{n.骗子；江湖郎中 *bank（= bench）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{montage}{mɔn'tɑ:ʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{n.蒙太奇}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{Mont Blanc}{'mʌn'blɔŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{n.勃朗峰（位于法国、意大利国境线上的阿尔卑斯山的最高峰，4807 米） *blanc（= white）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{piedmont}{'pi:dmɔnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{n.山麓地带；（P\textasciitilde{}）北美洲 Appalachian（阿巴拉契亚）山脉和大西洋沿岸间的高原 *pied（= foot）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND 来自拉丁语的 fus, fut, fund, found 意为 to pour。同义词根为 chemo。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{profuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND}
  \wordsense{【向前倾泻、倾倒的 pro（= forth）+ fus（= pour）】 [prə'fju:s] a.丰富的；浪费的，奢侈的；毫不吝惜的，慷慨的}
  \wordphrase{profusion 丰富；慷慨；浪费，奢侈}
  \wordphrase{profusely 毫不吝惜地；丰富地}
  \wordexample{The profuse stand of Douglas fir trees perfumes the air with pine scent.（茂密的花旗松的松香弥漫在周围的空气中。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{effusive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND}
  \wordsense{【向外倾泻的 ef<ex（= out）+ fus（= pour）】 [i'fju:siv] a.过分热情的；喷出的，溢出的}
  \wordphrase{effuse 使流出；流露 effusion 流出；流露}
  \wordphrase{effusively 过分热情地}
  \wordexample{I find effusive praise embarrassing, but I do appreciate it all the same.（虽然过分热情的称赞会让我有些不知所措，但我仍然对此表示真心感谢。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{irrefutable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND}
  \wordsense{【不能往回倒的 ir<in（= not）+ re（= back）+ fut（= pour）】 [i'refju:təbl] a.无法反驳的}
  \wordexample{I attended a seminar on the "Irrefutable Laws of Leadership".（我参加了一个有关“无可辩驳的领导法则”的研讨会。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{confuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND}

  \wordexample{confusing（令人困惑的）}
  \wordexample{confusedly（困惑地；混乱地）}
  \wordexample{I got confused.（我困惑了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{confusion}{[]}

  \wordsense{[kən'fju:ʒn]}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{diffusion}{[]}

  \wordbreakdown{向前弯曲pro（=forward）+cliv（=bend)}
  \wordsense{[pra'klivati] n.倾向；癖性；气质}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{acclivity}{a'klivati}

\chapter{FORT, FORC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FORT, FORC 来自拉丁语的 fort，forc意为 strong。同义词根有来自拉丁语的
  \item bil,firm,rob，val/vail等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{reinforce}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FORT, FORC}
  \wordsense{【重新使里面变得坚实re（=again）+in（=in）+forc（=strong）] [riin'fos] v.强化，增强；增援}
  \wordexample{reinforcement强化，增援；（pl.）增援部队Wereinforcedtheroofwith extrabeamstomakeithurricaneproof.（我们另外增加了-一根大梁加固房顶， 以使其经受得住台风。）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{fortification炮台；防御工事}
  \wordphrase{My firstpolitical defeat fortifiedmyresolveto succeedthenexttimearound.（我第一次的从政失败增强了我下一轮必胜的 决心。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{TARD}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TARD 来自拉丁语的tard意为slow。同义词根有来自益格鲁一撒克逊语的
  \item slo，反义词根有来自拉丁语的cel。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{retard}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{因落后而慢re（=back）+tard（=slow)}
  \wordsense{[ri'tad] vt.妨碍；拖延，推迟}
  \wordphrase{retardation妨碍；迟延，拖延Firefightersusecertainchemicalisthat retardthespreadofafire.（消防队员使用特定的化学物质阻止火势蔓延。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{tardy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TARD}
  \wordsense{a.缓慢的，迟钝的；拖拉的；迟到的}
  \wordphrase{['tadi]}
  \wordexample{tardily（拖拖拉拉地，迟缓地）tardiness（缓慢；迟到）?tardyprogress/ repentance（缓慢的进展/迟来的后悔）tardyinofferinghelp（不情愿帮忙的）be tardyforschool（上学迟到）}
\end{word}

\chapter{HAB[HIB]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根HAB[HIB] 来自拉丁语的hab意为to live，tohave，tohold。它的元音弱化形
  \item 为hib。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{rehabilitate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HAB[HIB]}
  \wordsense{【重新拥有re（=again）+hab（=have,live）] [ri:ha'biliteit] vt.使（出狱的人或病愈）恢复正常生活；使复职，复位；使恢复名誉；复原（老建 筑等），修复；使复生}
  \wordphrase{@rehabilitation恢复正常生活；复职；复位，复原；恢复名誉}
  \wordphrase{Istrongly believe prisoners should be rehabilitated during their incarceration.（我坚持认 为，犯人们在监禁期间应该接受正确的管教。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{inhabitant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HAB[HIB]}
  \wordsense{［在里面生活的人in（=in）+hab（=live,have）] [in'haebitant] n.居民，居住者；（某地区）栖息的动物}
  \wordphrase{inhabit居住于；栖息于}
  \wordphrase{inhabitable适合居住的inhabited有人居住}
  \wordphrase{的}
  \wordexample{There are only 7 inhabitants in this small apartment building.（这座小公寓 楼里只住了7个人。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{habituate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HAB[HIB]}
  \wordsense{［使生活hab（=live,have)】 [ha'bitjueit] v.使习惯于}
  \wordexample{Bears have become habituated to tourists as a primary food source. (熊 已经 习惯将游客当作食物的首要来源了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{malady}{[]}

  \wordphrase{My brother's a hypochondriac, always complaining of some malady or other （我的弟弟是个郁症患者，经常抱有这样或那样的病。）}
\end{word}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{relent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEN}
  \wordsense{v.变宽厚，变温和‘re（=again）}

\end{word}

\chapter{NOUNC, NUNCI}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根NOUNC, NUNCI 来自拉丁语的 nounc，nunci，意为 to say，to report。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 dic/dict，fa/fam/fess，locut/loqu，or，parl 等和来自希腊语的 phras，pha/phe/phuo。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{renounce}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{宣布向后退re（=back,against）+nounc（=say)}
  \wordsense{[ri'nauns] v.放弃；拒绝承认；绝交}
  \wordexample{If you want to become a U.s. citizen, you must renounce your prior citizenship.（如果你想成为美国公民，就必须放弃先前的公民身份。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{SCIND, SCIS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 SCIND, SCIS 来自拉丁语的scind，scis意为to cut，同义词根有来自拉丁语的cis，
  \item cop/coup，sect，sever，tail，tons，trench和来自希腊语的tom。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{rescind}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向后裁断，使之消失re（=back,away）+scind（=cut)}
  \wordsense{[ri'sind] vt.废止，使无效，取消}
  \wordexample{The company may rescind an employment offer if references don't check Out.（如果相关资料得不到验证，那么公司可能会取消聘用。）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCIND, SCIS}

\end{word}

\chapter{TAC[TIC]}

  \item 词根TAC[TIC] 来自拉丁语的tac意为silent，其元音弱化后变成tic。

\begin{word}[**]{reticent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAC[TIC]}
  \wordsense{［几乎没有话的re（=intensive）+tic<tac（=silent)]］ [ retrsnt] a.沉默寡言的，缄默的}
  \wordphrase{reticence 沉默寡言，缄默}
  \wordphrase{Employees are often reticent to report fraud for fearoflosingtheirjobs.（职员们常常因担心丢掉工作而不报告欺骗行为，）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{tacit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAC[TIC]}
  \wordsense{a.无言的，沉默的；默示的}
  \wordphrase{['taesit]}
  \wordexample{tacitly（默默地）?tacitconsent/agreement（默许/默契，默示协议）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{taciturn}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAC[TIC]}
  \wordsense{a.无言的，沉默寡言的 ['taesit3in] taciturnity（无言，沉默）}
\end{word}

\chapter{DUB [DOUB]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 DUB [DOUB] 来自拉丁语的dub意为uncertain。其变形为doub。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{redoubtable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DUB [DOUB]}
  \wordsense{[完全不确定或不明确的re（=intensive）+doub<dub（=uncertain）】 [rdautabl] a.可怕的；令人敬畏的}
  \wordphrase{Many modern politicians continue to quote the redoubtable Winston Churchill.（很多现代政治家还在继续引用令人敬畏的温斯顿·丘吉尔的话。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{indubitable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{不是不确定的in（=not）+dub（=uncertain)}
  \wordsense{[in'dju:bitabl] a.不容置疑的，明确的}
  \wordexample{The indubitable conclusion is that you're either drunk or stoned.（结论毫无 疑问：你喝醉了或已烂醉如泥。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{dubious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DUB [DOUB]}
  \wordsense{a.可疑的；半信半疑的；未定的，无把握的；暧昧的，含糊的；名声不好的}
  \wordsense{['dju:bias]}
  \wordexample{dubiouscompliment（明衰实贬的恭维）dubiousblessing（令人怀疑的祝福） dubiouscharacter（名声不好的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{dubiety}{dju:"balati}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DUB [DOUB]}
  \wordsense{n.怀疑}
\end{word}

  \item phen。

\begin{word}[**]{remonstrate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONSTR}
  \wordsense{［重新、反对着展示re（=again,against）+monstr（=show）] ['remanstreit] v.抗议，提出异议；忠告}
  \wordphrase{remonstrance 抗议；忠告 A rabid fan jumped from the stands to remonstrate with the referee.（一位狂热的爱好者跳下观众席，向裁判抗议。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{demonstrate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONSTR}
  \wordsense{v.证明，论证；（用模型、实验等）说明，示范操作；表露；示威 ['demanstreit ] *de (= completely)}
\end{word}

\chapter{TRENCH}

  \item sever,tons和来自希腊语的tom[tem]。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{retrench}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRENCH}
  \wordsense{【向后截断re（=back）+trench（=cut)］ [ri'trent] v.减少（费用），紧缩；节约}
  \wordphrase{retrenchment紧缩；节约经费The corporation was forced to retrench in lightoffallingrevenues.（由于收益减少，那家公司不得不缩减开支，）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{entrench}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{在内部截断en（=in）+trench（=cut)}
  \wordsense{[in'trent] v.用壕沟围住，以壕沟防护；确立}
  \wordphrase{entrenchment 堑壕；挖壕沟Approval of commercial whaling is deeply entrenchedinJapanesesociety.（日本社会对商业性捕鲸活动的认可已经根深 蒂固。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{trenchant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRENCH}

  \wordphrase{利}
  \wordphrase{trenchantly尖锐地，锋利地We'll missyourtrenchantcommentsat}
  \wordexample{trench掘沟，挖战壕；（n.）战壕，沟渠；海沟@trenchancy尖锐，锋 thesemeetingsafteryouretire.（您退任之后，我们会怀念您在会议上所发表的 那些一-针见血的评论的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{trench}{[]}

  \wordsense{n.堑壕，阵地；沟渠 v.挖战壕；挖沟渠 [trentf] trench warfare（堑壕战）trench a field（在田地里挖沟渠）}

\end{word}

\chapter{MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]}

\begin{itemize}

\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{surmise}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]}
  \wordsense{【关于、对于某事寄送sur（=over）+mis（=send)】 [sa'maiz] v.推测；猜想}
  \wordsense{n.推测；猜想}
  \wordexample{The Republicans in this district aren't as conservative as you might surmise （这个地区的共和党人不像你想的那么保守。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{demise}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]}
  \wordsense{【远远地、完全地寄送de（=away.intensive）+mis（=send）] [di'maz] n.去世，驾崩；转让；遗赠}
  \wordsense{v.死；转让；遗赠}
  \wordexample{The demise of a national administration in the United States is never a crisis. （在美国，一个政府的消亡不会成为危机。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{emit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向外寄送e(ex（=out）+mit（=send)}
  \wordsense{[i'mit] vt.散发（光、热、声音、气味等)；发出，发射；发行（货币等）}
  \wordphrase{@emission散发；发射；发射物；发行}
  \wordphrase{Smokedetectorsemitawarning soundwhenthebatteryislow.（当电池电量低时，烟雾探测器会发出警报声。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{intermittent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]}
  \wordsense{【送向其间的inter（=between）+mit（=send] [;inta'mitant] a.间歇的；断断续续的}
  \wordphrase{?intermit暂停，间断intermission中断；休息时间，薪间休息}
  \wordphrase{intermittently 间歇地；断断续续地Oursmokealarmmakesanirritatingintermittentbeep thatwon'tstop.（我们家的火警器没完没了地发出烦人的间歇性的响声。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{noncommittal**【不传送自己的意见的non（=not）+com（=with）+mit（=send）]}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]}
  \wordsense{[inpnka'miti] a.态度不明朗的，不表态的}
  \wordexample{She was noncommittal about selling the house. but I think she wants to. (尽 管她对卖房子一事的态度并不明朗，但我觉得她还是想卖的。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{remit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]}
  \wordsense{【远远地、向后寄送re（=back，away）+mit（=send）] [ri'mit] v.汇款；宽恕；免除；缓解；【律】将诉讼打回下级法院}
  \wordphrase{【医】remittent弛}
  \wordphrase{张的}
  \wordexample{@remission宽恕，赦免；免除；缓解remittance汇款（额）}
  \wordexample{We'll ship the merchandise as soon as you remit payment in full. (你- 将货款全部汇到，我们就用船运送货物。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{remiss}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向后寄送的re（=back）+miss（=send)}
  \wordsense{[ri'mis] a.怠慢的，疏忽的}
  \wordexample{I was remiss not to greet you as you came in.（我急慢了，在您进来时没跟 您打招呼。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{submissive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]}

  \wordphrase{submit 服从，屈服；提交@ submission服从；投降Rather than being submissive or aggressive, it's healthier to be assertive.（比起服从或者挑衅，自 信坚定要更健康有益。）}

\chapter{FORM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FORM 来自拉丁语的form意为form。同义词根有来自希腊语的morph。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{transform}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FORM}
  \wordsense{［使样子交叉trans（=across）+form（=form）] [traens'foim] vt.使变形；改变}
  \wordphrase{@transformation变形，变化}
  \wordphrase{transformable可变形的}
  \wordphrase{TwoweeksinthehotsunonaMexicanbeachwill}
  \wordexample{transformer【电】变压器；促使变化的人（或物） transformyou.（在墨西哥海边炙热的阳光下度过两个星期会让你变样。）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{The committee will formulate a plan to respond to earthquake emergencies. ( 委 员 会将制定一个方案，以应对地震时所发生的紧急情况。）}
\end{word}

\end{word}

\chapter{SCEND[SCENT,SCENS,SCAN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SCEND[SCENT,SCENS,SCAN]
  \item 等。另外，来自拉丁语的scal有ladder的意思，含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{transcend}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{越过某物爬tran<trans（=over,beyond）+scend（=climb）}
  \wordsense{[traen'send] v.超越（人类的经验、理性、信仰、描述能力等的范围）；优于}
  \wordphrase{transcendent出众的，卓越的transcendental先验的；超自然的；晦涩}
  \wordphrase{的}
  \wordphrase{transcendence / transcendency卓越，超越}
  \wordphrase{You can transcend your shynessbydoingtheseexercisesdaily.（你每天做这些练习可以克服羞涩。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{condescend}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCEND[SCENT,SCENS,SCAN]}

  \wordphrase{That instructor condescends to his students by treating them like babies （那个教练不摆架子，像对待孩子似的亲切地对待他的学生。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{VEST}

  \wordsense{v.拙劣地模仿；滑稽地模仿；歪曲}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{investment}{[]}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{vest}{vest}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEST}
  \wordsense{n.背心；（女服胸前的）V字形装饰；防弹背心 v.赋予，授予（权限等）；（财产等）属；使穿衣}
  \wordexample{play it close to the vest（避免冒险）In some countries authority is said to be vested in thepeople.（据说在某些国家权力属于人民，）vestedinterest/rights（【律】既得利益／ 既定权利）power/authoritythatvestsintheCrown（属于王室的权力／权威）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{vestment}{'vestmant}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEST}

\begin{word}[**]{vesture}{ivestfa(r)}

\chapter{HER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根HER 来自拉丁语的her意为heir，注意不要和意为to stick的her/hes相混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{heritage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER}
  \wordsense{［继承人继承的东西her（=heir）] [“heritid3] n.遗产；继承物}
  \wordphrase{heritable 会遗传的；可继承的}
  \wordphrase{As the eldest son, the title of Duke is his heritage.（作为家里的长子，他继承了公爵爵位。）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER}
  \wordsense{n.遗传；遗传的特征}

  \wordsense{[ha'reditri]}

\begin{word}[*]{heritable}{[]}

\end{word}

  \wordsense{v.继承（财产、权力、爵位等）；因遗传而获得（某些特点）‘in（=in） [n'"herit] ?inherit30,000dollars fromhisfather（从他父亲那里继承3万美元）inherither mother's good looksand herfather's bad temper（继承了她母亲的美貌和父亲的坏 脾气）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{inheritance}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER}

\chapter{SALV[SAV]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SALV[SAV] 来自拉丁语的salv意为safe。sav是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{salvage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使安全的行为salv（=safe)}
  \wordsense{['saelvrd3] n.海难救助；抢救的财物，被救的船舶；救助酬金；废物利用}
  \wordsense{vt.救助；打捞；利用（废品）}
  \wordexample{After the tornado, there was literally nothing to salvage from our home.（龙卷 风过后，我们家真的是没有任何值得抢救的东西了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{salvation}{sael'veifn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SALV[SAV]}
  \wordsense{n.拯救，救助；救援工具（或手段） Salvation Army （救世军） Government loans have been the salvation of several shakybusinesscompanies.（政府的贷款挽救了几个濒危的公司。）workoutone's salvation（谋划自救措施）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{salve}{saelv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SALV[SAV]}
  \wordsense{v.安慰，抚慰；使缓和；给……涂抹软膏 n.抚慰，安慰；药膏，软膏 salvehisconsciencebygivingstolenmoneytocharity（把他偷来的钱捐给慈善事业， 以此来安慰良心）lipsalve（护唇膏）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{saviour}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SALV[SAV]}
  \wordsense{n.救助者；（theS\textasciitilde{}）救世主，上帝"sav<salv（=safe） ［‘seivjo(r)] ?TheSaviour，OurSaviour（我们的救世主）}
\end{word}

\chapter{VERB}

\begin{word}[*]{verbiage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VERB}
  \wordsense{【言语的集合verb（=word）］ [iv3ibid3] n.冗词}

  \wordphrase{verbal言 语的；逐字的；动词的verbose啰嗦的；冗长的verbatim逐字的，一字不差}
  \wordphrase{All that}
  \wordexample{的；（ad.）逐字地，一字不差地@verbally口头地；作为动词 verbiage just to say you're sorry?OK-apology accepted.（你啰嗦这么久就只 为说声对不起？好吧，你的道歉我接受了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{verbal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VERB}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{reverberate}{[]}

\chapter{OP}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根OP, OPT, OPHTHALM 来自希腊语的op，opt意为eye。同义词根有来自拉丁语
  \item 的ocul。此外，还有形态和意思均与op，opt相似的来自希腊
  \item 语的ophthalm。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{optional}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{选择的opt（=choose）+ion（=state,quality）+al（=nature of)}
  \wordsense{['ppfani] a.随意的，非强制的；可自由选择的}
  \wordexample{opt选择?option选择权；可选择的东西；选修科目；买卖的特权@optionally 随意地The roasted pine nuts are optional in the recipe.（在食谱上，烤松仁 是可选放的材料。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{myopic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{闭上眼睛的my（=close）+op(opt（=eye）}
  \wordsense{[mai'ppik]】 a.近视的；目光短浅的}
  \wordphrase{myopia 近视}
  \wordexample{He says I have a myopic attitude toward technology, as though that's a bad thing!（他说我对技术持有一种短视的态度，好像那很不好 似的！）}
\end{word}

  \item 词根NOMIN, NOMEN 来自拉丁语的nomin，nomen意为name。另外，来自希腊语的
  \item 意为law，order，rule的nom与该词根形态、意思相似。

  \wordbreakdown{只是名字的、名目的nomin（=name)}

  \wordphrase{nominally 名义上地}
  \wordexample{The chair has nominal authority, but the real person in power is the executive secretary.（主席只拥有名义上的职权，实际掌权的是 行政秘书。）}
\end{word}

  \item 来自拉丁语的ordin，意为to order。

  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAND.MEND}

  \wordsense{n.受托管理国，受托管理者}
  \wordexample{The hour of morning exercise is mandatory during basic training.（在基础训 练期间必须保证晨练的时间。）}
\end{word}

\end{word}

  \wordsense{n.还押；发回重审}
  \wordphrase{@ commendation称赞@commendable值得称赞的}
  \wordphrase{With some trepidation, I commended my young son to the boarding school's care.（把我 儿子托付给寄宿学校，我很担心。） remand ［命令向后发送re（=back）+mand（=order）] [ri'ma:nd] v.将(犯人）还押候审；将（案件)发回原审法院重审}
  \wordexample{After having testified, the prisoner was remanded to his prison cell.（罪犯在 法庭上作证后被押回牢房候审。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{command}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAND.MEND}

  \wordsense{［所有的东西一起带来pan（=all）+egyr（=bring together）） [paena'd3irik] n.颂词}
  \wordexample{Read this panegyric to Elton John on this website. It's great!（读一读这个网 站上关于埃尔顿·约翰的赞词。真是太棒了！）}
\end{word}

\chapter{ECO[ECU,OCE]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根ECO[ECU,OCE] 来自拉丁语的eco意为 house。ecu，oce 是它的变形。同义词根有
  \item 来自拉丁语的caso
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{ecumenical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ECO[ECU,OCE]}
  \wordsense{【拉回家的ecu(eco（=house）+men（=lead）］ [ikju'menikl] a.普遍的；全基督教（会）的；促进全世界基督教团结的}
  \wordphrase{Local ministers formed an ecumenical youth group that's been quite popular.（当地牧师们成立了一个颇具人气的全基督教青年团体。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{ecology}{[]}

\end{word}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{economy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ECO[ECU,OCE]}
  \wordsense{n.经济；（某个国家、地区或时期的）经济制度，经济结构；节约，节俭‘nom（=law） [r'konami] political economy（政治经济学）viable economy（独立经济）the totalitarian economies of Germany before the Second World War（第二次世界大战前德国的极权主 义经济制度）economy class（（特别指客机的）经济舱位，二等舱位）economy of time and labor（时间和劳动力的节约）practice/use economy（节约）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{economic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ECO[ECU,OCE]}
  \wordsense{a.经济（上）的，经济学的"nom（=law）}
  \wordphrase{[ika'npmik]}
  \wordphrase{economic sanctions/zone/power/development/growth/independence（经济制 裁／区域／大国/发展／成长/独立）for economic reasons（因为经济原因）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{economical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ECO[ECU,OCE]}
  \wordsense{a.节约（金钱、时间等）的；经济的；节俭的*nom（=law）}
  \wordphrase{[ika'npmikl]}
  \wordexample{economically（经济地）}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{economics}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ECO[ECU,OCE]}
  \wordsense{n.经济学；（国家的）经济情况nom（=law）}
  \wordphrase{[ika'npmiks]}

\begin{word}[***]{economist}{i'konamist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ECO[ECU,OCE]}
  \wordsense{n.经济学者；节俭的人*nom（=law）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{economize}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ECO[ECU,OCE]}
  \wordsense{v.节约‘nom（=law)}
  \wordsense{[rkonamaiz]}

\begin{word}[**]{diocese}{[]}

\chapter{LITH}

  \wordexample{monolith独块巨石（如obelisk（方尖碑）等）?John Wayne had a monolithic presence in movies that few current stars can match.（约翰·韦恩在电影中的表 演综合而全面，这是当今影星中很少有人可以匹敌的。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{PRAG, PRACT}

\begin{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{pragmatic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRAG, PRACT}
  \wordsense{［不管什么都做的prag（=do,business）] [praeg'maetrk ] a.实际的；实用主义的；实干的}
  \wordphrase{pragmatism实用主义pragmatist实用主义者Thepragmaticdecisionis toforgothevacationandstudy.（务实的决定是放弃休假去学习。） practice n.实行；实施；实践；常规，惯例；习惯；练习；（医生、律师的）业务；诉讼手续 ['praektrs] ".练习；实习；习惯做…；从事(职业）}
  \wordphrase{'praektik]]}
  \wordphrase{有好天气）}
  \wordphrase{考虑一下实际。）}
  \wordexample{practiced（熟练的；老练的）}
  \wordexample{practiceteacher（（本人是学生的）实习教师）puta plan intopractice（将计划付诸实施）practicemedicine/law（行医/做律师） practical a.实际的；实用的；（比起理论）注重行动的；实践的；事实上的，实际上的}
  \wordexample{practically（实际地；实用地；事实上；几乎）havepracticallynofineweather（几乎没}
  \wordexample{practicality（实用性；实际事物）Let'sgetdowntopracticalities.（让我们}
  \wordexample{practical joke（玩笑；恶作剧）practicalEnglish（实用英语） practicalknowledge（实用知识）ideasthatappealtopracticalminds（讲求实用主义的 想法）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{practicable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRAG, PRACT}
  \wordsense{a.适用的；能实行的；可通行的}
  \wordphrase{['praektikab]]}
  \wordphrase{methodsthatarenot}
  \wordexample{practicably（可实行地）}
  \wordexample{practicability（可行性） practicable（不可行的方法）mountain pass that is practicable only in summer（只能在 夏天通行的山路）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{practitioner}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRAG, PRACT}
  \wordsense{n.开业者（尤指医生、律师等）；从业者；实践者；（基督教科学派的）信仰疗法术士}

\chapter{PTO}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PTO 来自希腊语的pto意为to fall。同义词根有来自拉丁语的cid /cad/
  \item cas和laps。
  \item symptomatic ［【一起掉下来的sym<syn（=together）+pto（=fall)]］
  \item [isimpta'maetik] a.征候的；（有）症状的
  \item @ symptom 症状;征候 @ Severe chest pain can be symptomatic of a heart
  \item attack or just simple indigestion.（严重的胸痛可能是心脏病发作的征兆，也有
  \item 可能只是单纯的消化不良。）
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{notorious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{广为人知的not（=know)}

  \wordphrase{notoriety 恶名}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{notable}{[]}

  \wordsense{['natabl] a.值得注意的；显著的；著名的}

  \wordphrase{notability显著；显要人物}
  \wordphrase{notably显著地，明显地Your grandfather was one of the most notable citizens of this town.（你的祖父曾是这个镇上最出 名的人之一。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{nota bene}{'nauta'biini}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOT}

  \wordexample{noteworthy（值得注意的；显著的）take notes of（记录，做笔记）He noted down the main points of the lecture.（他把演讲的要点记录了下来。）Please note my words. （请注意我的话。）I noted her eyes filling with tears.（我注意到她眼中充满泪水。）}
\end{word}

  \wordexample{noticeable（显而易见的；值得注意的）?put up a notice（贴告示）send a notice （下通知）leavewithoutnotice（没有预先通知（无端）地离开）}

  \wordexample{common notion（一般概念）have no notion of what he means（完全搞不懂他是什么意思）}

\chapter{VOR}

  \item 来自希腊语的phag。

\begin{word}[*]{voracious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{多吃的vor（=eat)}
  \wordsense{[va'reifas] a.狼吞虎咽的，暴食的，贪食的；贪婪的，如饥似渴的}
  \wordphrase{voracity贪食；贪婪}
  \wordphrase{voraciously狼吞虎咽地；贪婪地Our two teenage boys have voracious appetites.（我们那两个十来岁的儿子胃口极大。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{omnivorous}{[]}

  \wordexample{Our 2-year-old baby has an omnivorous curiosity. His favorite word is “Why"（我们两岁的宝宝对什么都好奇。他最喜欢的词就是“为什么”。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{devour}{[]}

  \wordexample{The hungry boy devoured his dinner.（那个男孩饿了，他狼吞虎咽地吃了晚饭。）The fire devoured twenty square miles of forest.（火灾吞噬了20平方英里的森林。）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{有宽大的心的 magn(= great)+ anim（=mind)}

  \wordphrase{magnanimity宽宏大量，雅量She couldn't have been more magnanimous when our ball broke her windows.（我们的球打破她的窗户时，她表现得宽容 极了。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{GRAT, GRAC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根GRAT, GRAC 来自拉丁语的grat意为thankful，pleasing等。grac是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{gratuitous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{作为感谢而做的grat(=thank,pleasing)}
  \wordsense{[gra'tjuitas] a.免费的，无报酬的；没有理由的}
  \wordexample{gratuity遣散费；（军人的）退役金Your gratuitous cruelty is abominable. I've had enough!（你那无端的残忍实在令人讨厌。我已经受够了！）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{ingratitude}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{不感谢 in(= not)+ grat（= thank, pleasing, favor)}
  \wordsense{[ in'graetitjuid ] n.忘恩负义；不感恩图报}
  \wordphrase{ingrate 忘恩负义者}
  \wordphrase{Because of her past ingratitude, I won't be giving her any more gifts.（因为她过去不知感恩，所以我不会送给她礼物了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{ingratiate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使对方感到好感in（=in）+grat（=thank,pleasing,favor)}
  \wordsense{[in'greiiet] vt.［～oneself]使满意，讨好，迎合}
  \wordexample{Despite my lack of credentials, I ingratiated myself with the hiring manager. （虽然我不够资格，但我逢迎了招聘主管，使其对我满意。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{gratify}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使高兴grat（=pleasing.thank)}
  \wordsense{['graetifa] v.使高兴，使满意}
  \wordphrase{gratification喜悦；使人满足的事}
  \wordphrase{gratifying令人愉快的，令人满足的}
  \wordexample{What gratifies me most is that I got admitted without any help from my father. （最让我最高兴的是我没有借助爸爸的任何帮助就被录取了。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{congratulate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAT, GRAC}
  \wordsense{v.祝贺*con(com (= together)}
  \wordsense{[kan 'graetfuleit ]}
  \wordexample{congratulatory（祝贺的）congratulatory letter/telegram（贺信/贺电）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{congratulation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAT, GRAC}
  \wordsense{n.祝贺；庆贺；（pl.）祝词con<com（=together）}
  \wordphrase{[kanigrat Ju 'leifn]}
  \wordexample{Congratulations!（祝贺！）offer one's congratulations to（对...说祝贺的话）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{ungrateful}{An'greitfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAT, GRAC}
  \wordsense{a.忘恩负义的，不知感谢的*un（=not）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{grateful}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAT, GRAC}
  \wordsense{a.感谢的，感激的；令人高兴的，令人愉快的}
  \wordphrase{「'greitfl］}
  \wordexample{I am grateful to you for your kindness.（谢谢你的好意。）grateful letter（感谢信） trees that afford a grateful shade（有宜人荫凉的大树）}

\begin{word}[*]{gratis}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAT, GRAC}
  \wordsense{ad./a.免费地（的） ['graetis] beadmittedgratis（免费入场）}

  \wordexample{graceful（优雅的）graceful dancer（优雅的舞蹈家）graceless（难看的；粗俗的，不 知礼的）graceless behavior（粗俗的行为）gracious（亲切的；慈悲的）her gracious}
  \wordexample{Majesty the Queen（女王陛下）}
  \wordexample{airs and graces（装腔作势，做作）days of grace （（期票、保险费等的）宽限期）say（a）grace（谢恩祈祷）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{disgrace}{[]}

\begin{word}[*]{luminous}{[]}

  \wordphrase{luminosity光明，光辉；发光度；发光物}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{luminary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUMIN[LUN]}

\end{word}

  \wordsense{v.照亮，照明；（作为祝贺的表示，在街道上）用灯装饰；阑明；启发；用金色、银色、 ["'luminet] 红色或图案等装饰（起首字母或手稿）i<in（=into） street illuminated by oil lamps（用油灯照明的街道）poorly illuminated rooms（照明 不好的房间）illuminate a difficult passage in a book（阐明书中令人费解的一段）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{lunatic}{[]}

  \wordphrase{[lu:natk]}

\chapter{MELL, MEL[MIL, MOL, MAR]}

  \item [me'lifluas] a.（嗓音、话语、音乐等）甜美的；柔和的；流畅的
  \item The mellifluous monotony of train wheels on tracks soothes me.（火车车轮碰
  \item 到轨道发出的柔和而单调的声音让我感到平静。）
\end{itemize}

\chapter{CRASTIN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CRASTIN 来自拉丁语的crastin意为tomorrow。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{procrastinate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRASTIN}
  \wordsense{【拉向前使走向明天pro（=forward）+crastin（=tomorrow）］ [praukraestineit］v.延迟，耽搁，拖延}
  \wordphrase{procrastination 延迟，拖延}
  \wordphrase{My New Year's resolution is to stop procrastinating.（我的新年决心就是做事不再拖延。） 来自拉丁语的flig意为to drive。同义词根有来自拉丁语的ig /ag/}
\end{word}

\chapter{FLIG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FLIG act，pel/puls[peal]。另外，来自拉丁语的意为to lead的duc/duct
  \item [du]，men[mean]和来自希腊语的agog也有相似的含义。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{profligate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使拉向前pro（=forward）+flig（=drive）}
  \wordsense{['prpfligat] a.放荡的，不检点的；肆意挥霍的，极度浪费的}
  \wordsense{n.放荡者}
  \wordphrase{profligacy放荡，不检点；浪费}
  \wordphrase{Citizens are complaining about the profligate salaries for public officials.（公民在抱怨公务员工资高得有些浪费。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{ART}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根ART 来自拉丁语的art意为skill，art。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{articulate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{『话中含有技术、技巧art（=art,skill)] ['a:'tikjulat] a.（发音）清晰的；表达得清楚有力的；有关节的 [a:'tikjuleit] vt.清晰地发（音）；明确有力地表达：用关节连接}
  \wordphrase{articulation清晰的发音；关节}
  \wordphrase{It's difficult for some men to articulate their real feelings.（有些人很难清楚地表达自己的真实情感。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artifact}{'aitifaekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{n.人造制品‘fact（=make）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artifice}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{n.灵巧；奸计；诡计fic（=make） ['aitifrs] by artifice（用奸计）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artificer}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{n.技工；技术兵；创造者fic（=make） [ar'tifisa(r)] the Great Artificer（伟大的造物主）engine-room artificer（机舱技工）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{artificial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{a.人工的，人为的；人造的，仿造的；不自然的‘fic（=make） [ait'fij] artificial flowers/insemination/light/respiration/satellite/turf（假花/人工授精/人 造光线/人工呼吸/人造卫星/人造草皮）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artisan}{ratitzan}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{n.工匠，技工}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{artillery}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{n.【总称］大炮；炮兵（部队） [a'tiari] ?artilleryman（炮兵）artillery duel（炮战）artillery fire（炮火）heavy/field artillery （重炮/野战炮）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{artist}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{n.艺术家；美术家（尤指画家）；（某方面的）能手 ['aitist] ?artist in words（善言辞的人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artiste}{a:'tirst}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{n.(职业歌手、演员、舞蹈家等)艺人；(某项手艺的)能手}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{artistry}{'aitistri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{n.艺术性；艺术技巧；艺术才能}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artistic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{a.艺术的；艺术家的}
  \wordphrase{[a:'tistk]}
  \wordexample{artistical（=artistic）}
  \wordexample{artistically（艺术地）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{arty}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{a.冒充有艺术价值的，附庸风雅的，装作爱好艺术的 ['aiti] ?arty-crafty（（家具等）美观而不实用的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artful}{'atfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{a.狡猾的，奸猾的；人工的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{artless}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ART}
  \wordsense{a.（和artful相对）单纯的，天真的；自然的"less（=without） ［'aitlas]】 ?artless village girl（纯朴的乡村姑娘）}
\end{word}

\chapter{ACERB}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根ACERB 来自拉丁语的acerb意为bitter，harsh。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{exacerbate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ACERB}
  \wordsense{［使非常痛苦或难受ex（=intensive）+acerb（=bitter,harsh）] [ig'zasabeit ] vt.使(痛苦、疾病等)恶化；激怒}
  \wordphrase{exacerbation恶化；激怒}
  \wordphrase{Many study shows that stress and tension can exacerbate pain.（很多研究表明压力和紧张会加剧疼痛。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{acerbity}{a's3:bati}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ACERB}
  \wordsense{n.尖酸；酸，苦，辣}
\end{word}

\chapter{BIL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根BIL 来自拉丁语的bil意为strong。同义词根有来自拉丁语的firm，forc
  \item /fort,rob,val[vail]等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{debilitate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{对"使结实"加以否定de（=reverse）+bil（=strong）}
  \wordsense{[drbiliteit] vt.使衰弱}
  \wordexample{debility（身体的）虚弱}
  \wordexample{Be brave. Fear debilitates one's will and destroys initiative.（勇敢点儿。恐惧会削弱人的意志，也会破坏主动性。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FUSC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FUSC 来自拉丁语的fusc意为dark。同义词根有来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{maur[moor]。}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUSC}
  \wordsense{OCR 原文未稳定拆分词性与释义，保留原始内容。}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{obfuscate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUSC}
  \wordsense{【盖在某物上，使其变得黑暗ob（=upon，over）+fusc（=dark）］ ['pbfaskeit] vt.使模糊；使困惑；使混乱}
  \wordphrase{Phone companies excel at obfuscating the actual price per minute you pay （电话公司善于模糊你每分钟通话应支付的实际价格。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{fuscous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUSC}
  \wordsense{a.暗褐色的 [ifaskas】}
\end{word}

\chapter{RAT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根RAT 来自拉丁语的rat意为to reckon。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{ratify}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{——地清点rat(=reckon)}
  \wordsense{[raetifar] vt.批准；认可}
  \wordphrase{ratification批准；认可}
  \wordexample{I heartily ratify your choice of restaurants.（你很会 选餐厅，对此我由衷地认可。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{rate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{n.率，比率；价格，费用；速度；等级v.评价；估价；列为}
  \wordphrase{[reit]}
  \wordexample{birth / death rate（出生/死亡率）exchange rate（汇率）walk at the rate of 3 miles an hour（以每小时3英里的速度行走）at a great rate（高速地）first/second/third rate（一级/二级/三级）first-rate teacher（一流的教师）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{rating}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{n.等级；测试得分；评分；《英》水兵，普通海员；声誉；（电视节目的）收视率，（广播的） ['reitp】 收听率}
  \wordexample{efficiency rating system（效率评分系统）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ratio}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{n.比，比率 ［'reiiau] direct / inverse / nutritive ratio（正比/反比/营养比值）ratio 3:2（（读做the ratio of three to two）3：2的比率）in the ratio of（以……的比率）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{ratiocination}{raetipsi'neifn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{n.（尤指用三段论法的）推理}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{ration}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{n.定额，定量，（食品等的）配给（量）；（pl.）【军】（日需）口粮；（日需）给养 ['rafn] v.定量供应（粮食、水等）；平均分配 ration card/book（定量供应卡/（定量）配给票证簿）go and draw rations（去领取分 发的东西）ration the water（定量供水）ration out the bread（分发面包）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{rational}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{a.理智的；理性的；合理的}
  \wordphrase{[ raeJnal]}
  \wordphrase{rational}
  \wordexample{rationally（理性地；合理地）?rationality（理性观点（或行动）；合理性） conduct/explanations（合理的行为/解释）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{rationale}{raefa'na:l}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{n.理由的说明}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{rationalism}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{n.【哲】理性主义；唯理论}
  \wordsense{['rafnalizam]}
  \wordexample{rationalist（理性主义者；唯理论者）}
  \wordexample{rationalistic（理性主义（者）的；唯理论（者）的）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{rationalize}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT}
  \wordsense{v.使合理；合理地解释}
  \wordphrase{【'raeJnalaiz]}
  \wordexample{rationalization（合理化）rationalize his fears/behavior（合理解释他的不安/行为）}
\end{word}

\chapter{JUST}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根JUST 来自拉丁语的词根just意为right。同义词根有来自希腊语的orth（o）。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{justify}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使正确、正当just（=right】}
  \wordsense{['d3Astifa] v.证明正当；为……辩护}
  \wordsense{justification证明为正当；辩护}
  \wordexample{justifiable可证明为正当的；可辩护的?How can we justify space exploration when starvation is rampant on Earth?（地球上 到处饥饿横行，怎么能说这时候进行宇宙探险是正当的呢？）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{justice}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordsense{n.正义；公平；司法，裁判；法官}
  \wordphrase{['d3Astis]}
  \wordexample{Department of Justice（《美》司法部（部长是Attorney General））Chief Justice（《美》） 最高法院首席法官）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{injustice}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordsense{n.不公正；不公平‘in（=not） [in'd3AStis] ?remedy injustice（消除不公正）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{unjust}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordsense{a.不公正的；不公平的；非正义的*un（=not）}
  \wordsense{[An'd3Ast]}
  \wordexample{unjust enrichment（不正当的发财）the just and the unjust（（无论正直与否的）所有人）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{unjustifiable}{An'd3Astifarabi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordsense{a.不合理的；无法辩护的*un（=not）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{adjust}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordsense{v.调整，调节；改变以适应；校准‘ad（=to） [a'd3Ast] adjustable（可以调节的）well-adjusted（完全适应环境的）well-adjusted child（完全 适应环境的孩子）adjuster（（要求赔偿损失时）负责调整赔偿金额的理算员）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{adjustment}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordsense{n.调节；调解；调整 ‘ad(=to)}
  \wordsense{[a'd3Astmant]}
  \wordexample{adjustment board（调解委员会）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{maladjusted}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordsense{a.失调的；（尤指人）无法适应（环境等）的 ‘mal(=bad) [imaela'd3Astid] maladjustment（失调；不适应）}
\end{word}

  \wordsense{n. 报酬；酬劳}
\end{word}

\subsection{vicin(= neighbor)}

\begin{word}[**+]{vicinity}{vatsinati}
  \wordbreakdown{vicin (= neighbor)}
  \wordsense{n. 附近；接近}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{vicinal}{vIsinal}
  \wordbreakdown{vicin (= neighbor)}
  \wordsense{a. 邻近的；本地区的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{vicinage}{visinid3}
  \wordbreakdown{vicin (= neighbor)}
  \wordsense{n. 附近的地区；邻居}
\end{word}

\subsection{warf(= to turn)}

\begin{word}[***]{wharf}{woif}
  \wordbreakdown{warf (= to turn)}
  \wordsense{n. 码头}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{whirl}{w3:l}
  \wordbreakdown{warf (= to turn)}
  \wordsense{v. 旋转；晕眩}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{whorl}{w3:l}
  \wordbreakdown{warf (= to turn)}
  \wordsense{n. 轮生体；螺旋形的物体}
\end{word}

\iffalse
\subsection{vill(= country, dwelling)}

\begin{word}{village}{iviid3}
  \wordbreakdown{vill (= country, dwelling)}
  \wordsense{n. 乡村；村民}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{villa}{ivila}
  \wordbreakdown{vill (= country, dwelling)}
  \wordsense{n. 别墅}
\end{word}

\fi
\subsection{warn(= to protect)}

\begin{word}{warn}{woin}
  \wordbreakdown{warn (= to protect)}
  \wordsense{v. 警告；告诫}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{warrant}{woirant}
  \wordbreakdown{warn (= to protect)}
  \wordsense{n. 根据；凭证}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{warranty}{woiranti}
  \wordbreakdown{warn (= to protect)}
  \wordsense{n. 担保；担保书}
\end{word}

\subsection{whisk(= to flick)}

\begin{word}[***]{whisk}{wisk}
  \wordbreakdown{whisk (= to flick)}
  \wordsense{v. 扫；拂；挥动}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[***]{whisker}{wiska(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{whisk (= to flick)}
  \wordsense{n. 须；连翼胡子}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{whisk broom}{wiskbru:m}
  \wordbreakdown{whisk (= to flick)}
  \wordsense{n. 小帚}
\end{word}

\subsection{xer(= dry)}

\begin{word}{xerophilous}{zi'rpfilas}
  \wordbreakdown{xer (= dry)}
  \wordsense{a. 好干燥的；早生的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{xerography}{,zia'rpgrafi}
  \wordbreakdown{xer (= dry)}
  \wordsense{n. 静电印刷术}
\end{word}

\subsection{xyl(= wood)}

\begin{word}{xylography}{zal lpgrafi}
  \wordbreakdown{xyl (= wood)}
  \wordsense{n. 木版印刷法}
\end{word}

\subsection{-wich,-wick(= town)}

\begin{word}{Norwich}{nsirid3}
  \wordbreakdown{-wich,-wick (= town)}
  \wordsense{n. 诺里奇（英格兰东部 Norfolk 郡首府）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{Warwick}{woirik}
  \wordbreakdown{-wich,-wick (= town)}
  \wordsense{n. 沃里克（Warwickshire 郡首府）}
\end{word}

\subsection{worth(= value)}

\begin{word}[+]{worthy}{w:di}
  \wordbreakdown{worth (= value)}
  \wordsense{n. 杰出人士}
  \wordsense{a. 有价值的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{worship}{w3:fip}
  \wordbreakdown{worth (= value)}
  \wordsense{n./v. 礼拜；崇拜；尊敬}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{stalwart}{stoilwat}
  \wordbreakdown{worth (= value)}
  \wordsense{a. 粗壮结实的；勇敢的}
\end{word}

\subsection{zeal(= fervor)}

\begin{word}[**]{zealot}{izelat}
  \wordbreakdown{zeal (= fervor)}
  \wordsense{n. 狂热者}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{zealous}{'zelas}
  \wordbreakdown{zeal (= fervor)}
  \wordsense{a. 热心的；热情的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{jealous}{'dselas}
  \wordbreakdown{zeal (= fervor)}
  \wordsense{a. 妒忌的}
\end{word}

\subsection{wring(=- to twist)}

\begin{word}[*]{wring}{rin}
  \wordbreakdown{wring (=- to twist)}
  \wordsense{v. 绞}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[+]{wrong}{rDn}
  \wordbreakdown{wring (=- to twist)}
  \wordsense{a. 错误的；不正常的；不道德的}
  \wordsense{n. 不公正；错误}
  \wordsense{v. 不公正地对待；加害}
\end{word}

\subsection{zinc(= zinc)}

\begin{word}[*]{zincite}{'zinkait}
  \wordbreakdown{zinc (= zinc)}
  \wordsense{n. 红锌矿}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{zinciferous}{zin'kifaras}
  \wordbreakdown{zinc (= zinc)}
  \wordsense{a. 含锌的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{zincography}{zin'kgrafi}
  \wordbreakdown{zinc (= zinc)}
  \wordsense{n. 制锌版术}
\end{word}

\subsection{writh(= to twist)}

\begin{word}[***]{writhe}{raio}
  \wordbreakdown{writh (= to twist)}
  \wordsense{v. 翻滚；扭动}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{wrath}{rad}
  \wordbreakdown{writh (= to twist)}
  \wordsense{n. 怒}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[+]{wreath}{ri:o}
  \wordbreakdown{writh (= to twist)}
  \wordsense{n. 花冠；花圈}
\end{word}

\subsection{zircon(= zircon)}

\begin{word}[*]{zirconium}{z3:'kauniam}
  \wordbreakdown{zircon (= zircon)}
  \wordsense{n. 锆}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{zirconate}{z3ikaneit}
  \wordbreakdown{zircon (= zircon)}
  \wordsense{n. 锆酸盐}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{zirconic}{z3:'kpnik}
  \wordbreakdown{zircon (= zircon)}
  \wordsense{a. 锆的}
\end{word}

\subsection{wroht(= to work)}

\begin{word}[***]{wrought}{roit}
  \wordbreakdown{wroht (= to work)}
  \wordsense{a. 加工的；精细的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{cartwright}{kaitrait}
  \wordbreakdown{wroht (= to work)}
  \wordsense{n. 车匠}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[**]{playwright}{pleirait}
  \wordbreakdown{wroht (= to work)}
  \wordsense{n. 剧作家；编剧}
\end{word}

\subsection{zo(= animal)}

\begin{word}[**]{zoology}{zau'plad3i}
  \wordbreakdown{zo (= animal)}
  \wordsense{n. 动物学}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{protozoon}{prautau'zaupn}
  \wordbreakdown{zo (= animal)}
  \wordsense{n. 原生动物}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{spermatozoon}{isp3imata'zaupn}
  \wordbreakdown{zo (= animal)}
  \wordsense{n. 精子}
\end{word}

\subsection{zon(= belt)}

\begin{word}[*]{zone}{zaun}
  \wordbreakdown{zon (= belt)}
  \wordsense{n.（寒带、热带等的）地带}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{zonal}{zounl}
  \wordbreakdown{zon (= belt)}
  \wordsense{a. 带状的；形成地带的}
\end{word}

\subsection{zyg, zeug(= yoke, paired)}

\begin{word}{zygote}{'zaigaut}
  \wordbreakdown{zyg, zeug (= yoke, paired)}
  \wordsense{n. 接合子（体）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{zygodactyl}{,zaigəu'dæktil}
  \wordbreakdown{zyg, zeug (= yoke, paired)}
  \wordsense{a.（鸟）对生趾的}
\end{word}

\subsection{xanth(= yellow)}

\begin{word}{xanthous}{'zænθəs}
  \wordbreakdown{xanth (= yellow)}
  \wordsense{a. 黄色的；蒙古人种的}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{xanthophyll}{'zænθəfil}
  \wordbreakdown{xanth (= yellow)}
  \wordsense{n. 黄色色素；叶黄质}
\end{word}

\iffalse
\subsection{zym(= ferment)}

\begin{word}{zymology}{zai'mɔlədʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{zym (= ferment)}
  \wordsense{n. 发酵学}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{zymurgy}{'zaimə:dʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{zym (= ferment)}
  \wordsense{n. 酿造学}
\end{word}

\subsection{xen(= foreign, strange)}

\begin{word}{enzyme}{'enzaim}
  \wordbreakdown{xen (= foreign, strange)}
  \wordsense{n. 酶}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{xenophobia}{,zenəu'fəubiə}
  \wordbreakdown{xen (= foreign, strange)}
  \wordsense{n. 恐外症}
\end{word}

\begin{word}{xenolith}{'zenəliθ}
  \wordbreakdown{xen (= foreign, strange)}
  \wordsense{n. 捕虏岩（指火成岩中与其无直接成因联系的外来岩块）}
\end{word}
